Although the wooden spider puppet was also a High Tier 6 Puppet, which means that it wasn’t really capable of dealing significant damage to the Water Takeda. However, it also worked the other way too like a vice versa thing, as the Water Takeda also struggled to seriously harm it.

The Wooden spider puppet had been reinforced with extremely rare Tier 6 wood resources, making it exceptionally durable and difficult to destroy.

Her strategy had been simple: the flaming mage puppet served as the primary damage dealer, while the crocodile acted as a disruptor, and the wooden spider functioned as the defensive tank.

That approach worked well when she only needed to hold one Takeda at bay, but now she had to face two.

She didn’t believe she could continue holding the line alone with just those three, and she remembered the message Alec had left for her, almost as if he had foreseen this very situation.

“Will you surrender, or do we have to make you?” the Wind Takeda asked, stepping into a battle stance as a thin green glow gathered around his hand.

The crowd’s attention shifted entirely to her, while her three puppets formed a loose protective semi-circle around her, leaving a small gap between them as if guarding their master.

Beatrice had her hand extended forward, where thin mana threads stretching from her fingertips connected to each puppet. Then, calmly, she flicked her wrist.

“You three are on your own,” she said softly, severing the mana connections to all three puppets, this action of hers caused many in the crowd who were watching to start murmuring again in surprise, as they couldn’t understand why she would do such a thing, since with her intervention her puppets actually seems way stronger.

But then her next action made the Dean and the Grand Dean, who were seated in the VIP section watching the match, shift slightly in their chairs.

As for the Marionette clan elders who had come to watch her, they were already smiling broadly the moment they realized what she was about to do, because they all knew what was inside the coffin.

They had tried several times to communicate with Beatrice since her team arrived at the Capital, urging her to use the power within the coffin to the fullest, but she had shut down every means of communication with them.

So for them, who had long hoped she would display their clan’s power and put them in the limelight found this situation highly favorable.

“Come out, Patches!” Beatrice said, slapping her hand against the side of the coffin as its door began to creak open slowly.

Normally, Beatrice believed when faced with something like this, her opponents were supposed to rush forward to interrupt whatever she was trying to summon, but the Takedas before her were simply too confident.

They clearly didn’t believe anything she summoned could change the outcome of the battle, they just waited, intending to defeat whatever emerged and break her will in ways she wouldn’t expect.

But when the coffin door finally opened fully, they realized it was still dark inside, though it was completely open, yet a thick dark fog seemed to cover its interior making them unable to see what’s inside.

They tried probing it with their mental energy, but it was instantly repelled the moment it touched the coffin, then two mismatched hands appeared, gripping the right edge of the coffin, followed by another two different hands gripping the left side, slowly pulling the body within outward.

That was how the puppet called Patches appeared for the first time on the capital stage, standing about seven feet tall, with long black hair that covered his face down to the neck.

Though his eyes were still visible through the strands, they were pure white, and unlike the rest of Beatrice puppets with the usual puppet eyes, his was different with a tiny black dot at the center.

He stepped out unsteadily from the coffin, apart from his two main arms, two additional arms had been sewn along the region where his ribs is supposed to be located, most of his body was covered in stitches, as if different body parts had been sewn together, very much like a Frankenstein creation.

Though his upper body was bare, but a sword strap crossed his back, with a small axe hung at his waist, and gloves covered his two primary hands, one of the extra arms was green and muscular, making the audience think of an Orc limb, while the other was scaled like that of a reptilian creature.

Even his chest showed mismatched textures, scaled patches, armored sections, and bare flesh, all held together with dark stitching.

Though the parts had been sewn together, it was clear his body wouldn’t easily come apart even if someone targeted the stitches, there were simply too many to cut through before any real damage could be done, and their dark coloration made many suspect they wouldn’t be easy to unravel at all, clearly they weren’t ordinary thread at all, likely reinforced in some way.

“What the heck is that?” the Wind Takeda finally asked, breaking the silence.

At that moment the entire crowd was quiet, watching the strange puppet with fascination, as Beatrice stepped behind him and used two fingers to press several points on his body.

Each touch made Patches twitch slightly, when she finished, she stepped back and flicked her hand outward, sending another set of thin mana threads toward different points on his body from behind.

Immediately the threads connected, his left eye began to glow with a faint blue hue as he stepped aside to give way to Beatrice behind him, no longer moving awkwardly.

“Let’s do this,” Beatrice said, staring straight into Wind Takeda’s eyes.



============================================================
Chapter 1057: Chapter 1057: Sets.
============================================================

The whole crowd started cheering loudly after hearing Beatrice’s words, the mages supporting the School of Silent Night were all shouting for the Takedas present to make quick work of her.

Meanwhile, the few fanatic mages still supporting the War God Academy began chanting Beatrice’s name loudly, though they had never seen the new puppet before and weren’t aware of what it was capable of, what they knew however was that no matter the odds against them, they would still support the War God Mage Team to the very end.

It was actually surprising to many of the foreign mages present to see a group still stubbornly supporting a team they believed would soon lose, regardless of what Beatrice had shown.

They were especially surprised because the rest of the Northern academies hadn’t received such treatment, and it was the War God Academy who isn’t even among the northern great 8 academies that was getting such treatments which made many of them start suspecting there must be a lot of foul plays present.

Then, before they could fully process what was happening, both Takedas turned into blurs as they rushed toward Beatrice.

Behind the Wind Takeda was a trailing green glow, while a blue hue followed the Water Takeda, from the way they moved, it was clear they planned to avoid the puppets entirely, as if they weren’t even there , and they were just going to go straight for Beatrice.

“I’ll deal with her, help me keep these three busy,” the Wind Takeda said in the last moment diverting the Water Takeda’s attention as wind particles began gathering near his hands, spiralling until two translucent wind katanas formed in his grip.

Once they formed, he didn’t waste any time, as he pushed off the ground with his left leg, and even as he leapt high, he kept pushing higher with his right.

As he stepped through the air like he was walking casually, wind footprints appeared beneath his feet.

The three puppets acting as the first line of defence all looked toward him. In fact, the flaming mage puppet was just about to act, but as he raised his hand to draw his sword, he suddenly felt something curling tightly around his wrist.

Which caused him to raise his head to clearly see what had restrained him from drawing his blade, that was when he noticed the chain-like weapon wrapped around his hand was connected to the weapon wielded by the Water Takeda.

From the sides, the other two puppets were already closing in on him, trying to restrain him and free the flaming mage puppet, to the Water Takeda they were acting more like a pest to him with the way they were acting together as it always made it hard for him to pick them out one by one.

At the same time, the Wind Takeda was already descending from his high jump, but Patches simply stepped forward slowly, positioning himself in front of Beatrice.

His right hand moved behind his shoulder, smoothly drawing the sword strapped across his back.

In one fluid motion, the Wind Takeda spun mid-air, before twisting his dual katanas into a scissoring strike, but the puppet merely raised its sword and blocked the attack.

A loud clang rang out on impact, as a shockwave rippled across the arena floor, with dust lifting in a circular wave around them. Immediately, Takeda opened his mouth to cast a spell, but his mental senses suddenly caught something.

Instinctively, he raised his leg high to block in mid-air.

Bang!

He felt the bones in his leg creak as if they were about to snap upon impact, being an expert, he quickly used his ninjutsu to divert most of the force, and then used the remaining momentum to propel himself upward.

He drifted back down like he was guided by a gentle breeze, somersaulting once before landing lightly on the ground, when he looked up at the puppet again, the casual expression he usually wore was gone.

A throbbing pain pulsed through his left leg, the one he had used to block the punch, which had clearly come from the orc arm stitched onto Patches.

He studied the puppet carefully, even at this distance, he still couldn’t accurately determine its realm.

Meanwhile, the Water Takeda had already pulled back slightly, realizing it was becoming difficult to handle all three puppets at once.

“Wind, I don’t think I’ll be able to handle all three of them like this for long while fighting with a handicap,” the Water Takeda said.

That made the Wind Takeda pause in thought, he had been the one who proposed the three-versus-three fight, but the Earth Takeda still hadn’t gotten back up after his last bout against lucas, which has made him effectively useless since then.

He knew he needed a third fighter to gain a real advantage against Beatrice, yet his pride wouldn’t allow him to summon another Takeda through the wind gate after he had greatly underestimated the war god mages.

As he felt calling one of the remaining two Takedas would feel like admitting weakness, after a moment of thought, he decided it was better to let the Water Takeda go all out instead.

“Do it!” the Wind Takeda said, stepping forward again and swinging his wind-forged blades at Patches, the puppet’s sword movements looked slightly slower than his, yet somehow every strike was still blocked with precise timing.

Meanwhile, the Water Takeda smiled faintly, clearly pleased, he already knew that when the three Tier-6 puppets worked together, gaining an advantage was difficult. Now that he had permission, he pushed off the stage and retreated several steps away from them.

As he moved back, runic tattoos began spreading across his body and ninja attire. Within seconds, parts of the outfit turned a deep icy blue.

His mask shifted colour as well, and even his weapon seemed wrapped in a new energy layer, subtly altering its form.

Frosty air began to seep from his hands, and cold vapour escaped through the tiny openings of his mask, when he lifted his gaze toward the three puppets again, he was now clad in what clearly looked like a Tier-6 spirit equipment set crafted specifically to complement his abilities.

The spectators reacted immediately.

Until now, the Takedas had only fought in their standard ninja uniforms — the official attire of their institution, It also seemed to be inscribed with a Tier-5 protection rune. But now the spectators were realising that apart from possessing that seemingly cheat level special ability, Takeda also seems to have the power to equip every one of those bodies with spirit equipment sets.

And not just any spirit equipment, but likely equipment sets that were perfectly tailored to their individual abilities, the thought alone made many in the crowd shift their gaze toward the Wind Takeda, waiting to see if he would reveal something similar, however he didn’t.

Instead, it was the Water Takeda , who had been fighting defensively up until now, changed in his presence completely, like a whole new person.

The moment his spirit equipment set completely manifested, he dashed forward, abandoning caution as he charged straight at the three puppets.



============================================================
Chapter 1058: Chapter 1058: Sets 2.
============================================================

Seeing him dash so recklessly toward the three puppets without any hint of caution made many spectators uneasy.

However the flaming mage puppet was the one to react first, by dashing forward and thrusting out its left hand forward, and from seemingly nowhere, flaming lances formed as it manipulated the fire particles in the air to conjure them.

With a sharp motion, the lances shot toward the Water Takeda, however; he just weaved through them like a blue phantom, each near miss leaving a faint azure glow in his wake.

Within moments, he was already in front of the flaming puppet.

“Die!” the flaming puppet mage said in a flat, emotionless tone, showing that clearly its only objective was the elimination of the Water Takeda.

His blade wrapped in a blazing aura, swung down in a diagonal motion, the crowd gasped as his strike appeared to connect, but a split second later, they noticed a blue streak sliding past the puppet.

It almost looked as if the Water Takeda had phased through the puppet, but that wasn’t the case, because before the strike had landed, he had already cast the Ice Body Clone spell, so what the flaming puppet had cut was merely an ice replica, which was perfectly positioned to take the hit.

With his spirit equipment set equipped and active, Water Takedas’s speed and strength didn’t just increase significantly, more importantly, it seems to have helped neutralize one of his biggest weaknesses, which was the interference of Ice.

While clad in that set, he could control ice element but that wasn’t all , as he could also instantly convert it to water, freely switching between the primary and sub-elements as needed.

The spectators watched as the blur they’d been tracking began to stabilise, for a moment it resembled flowing liquid, then it solidified, revealing the Water Takeda once again.

Who had used his ninjutsu technique to switch positions, going from standing before the flaming mage puppet one moment to appearing a few meters behind it the next.

“Arrggh!” The flaming mage puppet, finally realising it had been tricked, as he tried to pull his sword free from the frozen sculpture, but that was easier said than done.

As not only was the blade firmly stuck, but frost had already begun creeping up his right hand, travelling through the sword connection.

The Water Takeda didn’t even bother turning back, from experience, he knew the spell would be able to restrain the flaming puppet for at least a minute, and to him that was more than enough time.

With the main damage dealer temporarily neutralised, he lowered his stance and accelerated forward, dashing toward the wooden spider puppet and the crocodile puppet a few meters away.

Each step he took left faint frozen footprints behind, sending the Silent Night supporters into another frenzy of cheers.

The wooden spider advanced first, swinging its two elongated wooden legs in a pincer-like motion to trap him.

But Water Takeda simply rotated his weapon, hooking the curved ends around the incoming limbs while dropping into a low slide, as he slipped cleanly beneath the spider puppet’s body.

He knew damaging that reinforced wooden frame would be difficult, so he didn’t waste effort trying.

Instead, a long icy trail formed behind him as he slid, and when he reached the underside of the puppet, he pushed off the ground and sprang upward into the air...

With him launching into the air like that with all his strength, the trailing end of the Kusarigama chain which was still latched onto the spider puppet’s front legs — tugged violently.

The force from it twisted the puppet off balance, flipping it upside down and exposing its underbelly, by the time the Water Takeda landed atop it, the chain had already slipped free from its legs.

As he immediately casted a spell by forming a hand seal which he pushed downward.

[> Mid Ninjutsu Spell — Ice Age. <]

Using his legs like conduits, frost spread rapidly outward, crawling across the wooden spider puppet’s body, since outright destroying it would take too long, he chose the smarter option, which was immobilisation.

Ice layered over the puppet, stiffening its joints and sealing its movements, turning it into a massive ice sculpture.

Just then, the flaming mage puppet finally broke free, while the crocodile puppet lunged toward him as he stood on the frozen spider’s body.

The Water Takeda flicked his Kusarigama forward in a sharp motion, then tugged the midpoint of the chain to recall it in a fast move after flickering it out.

Fortunately the attack had already triggered before the weapon was recalled, as a thin wave of pale-blue energy shot outward the instant the weapon snapped forward.

The crocodile puppet opened its jaws and exhaled another burst of frost breath to counter it, but while it was occupied, the flaming mage puppet leapt high, sword raised, descending straight toward The Water Takeda.

Yet when it landed, he had struck nothing, the Water Takeda’s body dissolved into a blue afterimage, and when he reappeared, the crowd saw him standing behind the flaming mage puppet, with his chain already looped tightly around its neck, and his right knee raised and pressing at the puppets back.

The puppet reached back with its free left hand, trying to resist, but the Water Takeda released his right grip on the chain which was near the sickle which is at the end of the chain.

Then he yanked hard with his left hand, the chain snapped sideways, dragging the sickle across the puppet’s neck in a brutal arc, eventually slicing clean through.

Immediately, blood began pouring out of the neck, but he clearly wasn’t done with the puppet, now that his weapon was fully free, he gripped the Kusarigama with both hands again and kept flicking and tugging the chain, deliberately slicing through every joint of the flaming mage puppet from behind, as he wasn’t willing to take any chances.

A single cut to the neck might have been enough to disable a normal opponent, but to him what was before him is still a puppet and a weapon of Beatrice, so if she stubbornly tried to force it to keep moving manually with her mana threads, it could still pose a threat.

So he methodically severed each joint, ensuring that even manual control wouldn’t allow it to perform basic movements.

After finishing, he twisted his body, spinning the chain with him, and after two full rotations later, he came to a halt, settling into a stance, as his right hand gripped a section of the chain, using that point to guide the sickle as it flew outward.

The blade hooked cleanly into the open mouth of the crocodile puppet, which had leapt at him again, this time he yanked hard, dragging it down before slamming its body against the ground.

But he wasn’t done as he swung the chain again this time to the opposite direction— another slam.

Then again. And again. He repeated the motion about ten times until he could tell most of the creature’s bones were shattered.

With a smooth flick of his right hand while his left held the opposite end steady, he unhooked the sickle from the crocodile’s mouth.

Finally, he jumped down from the wooden spider puppet, which was still fully encased in ice.

The prolonged contact of his leg on the puppet while channeling frost through his feet had ensured the freezing held firm.

Keeping the spider immobilized had been deliberate , as he believed it was the most troublesome of the three and could easily interfere with his plan to eliminate the others.



============================================================
Chapter 1059: Chapter 1059: Patches And Beatrice 1.
============================================================

Thud!

A light shockwave rippled outward the instant the Water Takeda dropped from atop the wooden spider puppet, which was still completely frozen in place.

Now it was effectively two main bodies against Beatrice, who had only one active puppet left, the Wind Takeda hadn’t moved since his last exchange, and neither had Patches.

The puppet simply stood there, motionless, almost as if it were patiently waiting for them to make the first move.

“Remember when I gently asked you to surrender earlier? I’ll give you the same option again, so are you surrendering, or do we have to make things difficult for you? Because believe me, as strong as that puppet of yours may be, you should know there’s no way it can take both of us at once,” the Wind Takeda said, settling into a combat stance.

Beside him, the Water Takeda casually twirled his weapon, with his left hand holding one end while his right twisted the chain attached to the sickle blade.

“Unfortunately, I know a certain stubborn guy who would do the exact opposite,” Beatrice replied.

“And honestly, I hate that all these responsibilities suddenly fall on me whenever he isn’t around. But what choice do I have?”

“I just happen to be his vice-captain, and he always seems to have this ridiculous amount of faith in me, so even if I wanted to back out now, my conscience wouldn’t let me rest. If you want this win, you’ll have to earn it properly, as I’m not giving you an easy way out.”

“I understand what you’re doing,” she continued calmly.

“You’re hesitating because you think pushing this further might expose more than you intended. Unfortunately for you, things won’t go your way today, because even if I lose... I’ll make sure I go down fighting.”

At first when she started talking, The Wind Takeda had thought she was actually considering his offer, and he had been trying to search inside of his mind for the right words say in order to completely cajole her into letting everything go, but by the time she finished speaking, his brows were already deeply furrowed.

It was now clear to him, she wasn’t going to budge, he could tell that surrendering had never been an option for her.

Then Beatrice finally moved, as her hands dragged through the air as if pulling invisible strings, seeing that motion made the Water Takeda instinctively step back; as he knew from experience that whenever she did that, an attack usually followed.

The Wind Takeda, however, didn’t think much of it at first — until, in the next instant, Patches vanished from his sight, immediately confusion flickered across his face as he quickly attempted to retreat.

Wind wings had already begun forming around his legs, the beginnings of a movement spell, but before it could fully activate, Patches reappeared right in front of him.

The puppet swung its sword diagonally downward from right to left. Reacting instantly, the Wind Takeda raised his conjured wind katana upward, as the two blades merged as he attempted to block while already planning to use the impact to propel himself further back.

But just before the blades were meant to clash, Patches abruptly withdrew its sword, and from below, one of the extra arms (the orc arm), shot forward in a brutal punch.

Because of his upward swing, a small opening had been left in his guard... and it was exploited perfectly by the puppet.

Bang!

The punch landed squarely on his stomach, and for a split second, time seemed to freeze as his body lifted slightly off the ground, then a visible shockwave burst out from his back, and he was violently hurled away in the direction the force travelled a second later.

Meanwhile, Patches twisted on its heel and immediately dashed toward the Water Takeda.

“So I was your real target all along, huh?” The Water Takeda said with a faint smile as he began circling the stage at speed, though it looked as if he had just been running aimlessly earlier, there was far more intention behind his movements.

Now that Patches twisted unnaturally and dashed straight for him, he swung his weapon, causing the chain to coil rapidly around his body until it wrapped tightly across his chest like a belt.

But he wasn’t finished as he did that to free his hands, and immediately he started forming hand seals quickly, and just before he completed them, Patches was already in front of him with his right hand stretched forward.

[> Mid Ninjutsu Spell — Water Seal Prison <]

In that instant, several spots along the path he had run suddenly lit up with a blue hue, as water runes appeared on the ground, and bubble-like tendrils of compressed water surged upward toward Patches.

A single water tendril wouldn’t have been enough to restrain Patches, but more and more wrapped around his arms, legs, and shoulders, tightening with crushing pressure, this wasn’t ordinary water; it was highly compressed, dense enough to act like solid restraints.

Then, from the corner of his eye, the Water Takeda noticed movement , someone was slipping low past the outer range of the water seal prison he had created without triggering any of the traps.

When he got a clearer look, the recognition hit him instantly.

Beatrice.

The very puppeteer whose puppet he was currently exerting his full attention to keep restrained.

He hadn’t expected her to close the distance herself, since the fight began, many had underestimated her, assuming she was just another traditional summoner type of mage, one that was dangerous only through her puppets and otherwise helpless once those summons were restrained.

That assumption was precisely why he had been circling earlier, secretly laying traps across the battlefield, to restrain the puppet.

All of this had been done so that once he kept the seal active, the Wind Takeda could finish the job from above.

In fact, the Wind Takeda, who had been punched away earlier, had already halted his momentum using the wind currents in the air to steady himself after regaining control and was now floating in the air, with semi-visible wind wings flapping behind him as he waited for the Water Takeda’s signal to launch his attack.



============================================================
Chapter 1060: Chapter 1060: Patches And Beatrice 2.
============================================================

But from what he was seeing, it clearly wouldn’t be easy, and now he was truly regretting underestimating the War God Mage Academy Team, as he started to feel if not for his big mouth earlier, he would have just simply summoned another main body and all these would have been all over.

“Water, leave the seal and get out of range,” Wind Takeda said.

“But—” Water Takeda was about to complain, but in the end he said nothing, as he began retreating backward while still keeping both his hands locked in the seal, he wanted to put a good distance between them before letting go, because he knew the moment he broke the seal, the compressed water prison that had been restraining Patches would collapse.

But as he tried to escape, Beatrice swung her hand outward, as barely visible mana threads spread through the air, seeing her make a move and not wanting to take any risks, he cast a spell blowing out from his mouth.

[> Low Ninjutsu Spell – Ice Mist. <]

As he activated it, a cold mist spread outward from his lips into the air, normally, this wasn’t a spell that made much difference at his current realm, but that wasn’t his goal.

What he really wanted was to distinguish where Beatrice’s mana threads were located, and when the mist spread, it revealed them immediately, as thin white lines began appearing in the air as the icy particles clung to the threads like magnets after spreading in the air before him.

He quickly realised the mana strings were already surrounding him, and all that remained was for her to tighten her grip, with danger so close, he couldn’t afford to keep holding Patches in place.

His fingers separated, breaking the seal he had been maintaining with such effort.

The compressed water currents restraining Patches instantly collapsed to the ground once his hands came apart. Meanwhile, Beatrice, who had slid sideways out of Patch’s way, dragged her threads sharply through the air.

Now, with the ice mist hanging in the air, her mana threads became far more visible, though they weren’t completely clear, but everyone could now see the faint white strings suspended across the battlefield, all leading back to a single point — her fingers, which seemed to be controlling well over fifty separate thread positions at once, they keep wondering just where the mana strings were anchored to for it to work the way Beatrice was using them but no matter how much they strained their thoughts they were still unable to come up with anything.

With a forceful yank, she pulled backward with both hands, as the threads that had been floating loosely suddenly converged at incredible speed.

And it just so happened that the one standing at the central point where those strings were about to close in... was the Water Takeda.

“Fuck!” the Water Takeda cursed, shaking his body slightly so his weapon could slide free from his chest, he immediately grabbed one end of the chain with his right hand immediately it started coming off, pouring both his mana and Qi into it , especially an excess amount of his Qi, using it to sharpen the edge of the Kusarigama sickle for what he intended to do next.

He whipped the weapon around himself in a tight arc, as the energized chain sliced through several visible threads he could see at once, forcing an opening. Without hesitation, he retreated.

But just as he retreated, his danger senses screamed, not knowing exactly where the next strike would come from, he dropped low into a knee slide.

As he started to feel something with tremendous pulling force sliced past his face, grazing his cheek.

Pain flared instantly as he could feel his blood pouring, but he ignored it, as he slid he used the momentum to bend his head towards the opposite direction, he then saw Beatrice drawing her left hand back, and he could tell that it was the very motion of that hand that must had tightened the threads enough to nearly carve into his face.

If he hadn’t bent when he did, even he knew that the damage would have been far worse. Still, that didn’t matter now, the important thing was that he had regained distance.

[> Mid Shinobi Spell – Wind Slam. <]

The moment he cleared the immediate danger zone, the Wind Takeda acted without waiting for any signal, from high above, he thrust both palms downward, as a crushing wave of wind pressure started to gather around his hand waiting to descend instantly on intent.

Beatrice reacted at once, stretching her hand toward the wooden puppet which was still frozen near the center of the stage.

“Come here!” she shouted, dragging her hands sharply through the air, though many of the lower-rank mages couldn’t clearly see what she had done, but some mid-rank mages with stronger mental perception had caught it, she had swept her mana threads in a straight line, and they latched onto the frozen wooden spider almost like they had magnetic magic imbued to them not even removing as she yanked that hard.

The ice anchoring the puppet to the ground began cracking slowly before snapping apart, the massive wooden spider shifting back toward its original position.

Beatrice then dashed forward at the same time, sliding in the final moment before the Wind Takeda’s attack fully descended, from the height where the Wind Takeda floated, the air gathered densely around his hands, compressing into a terrifying downward force.

Bang!!!!

The stage seemed to sink under the pressure, debris blasted outward, dust lifting violently as the lower stands closest to the arena were hit by powerful gusts, even spectators far away could feel the oppressive wind.

Beatrice had tried to use the frozen wooden spider puppet as a buffer against the incoming force, but when the Wind Slam fully landed and the massive puppet slammed into her, she couldn’t stop herself from coughing out a mouthful of blood.

Patches fared slightly better, the shockwave forced him down to one knee, but despite the crushing pressure, he refused to collapse completely.

His stitched body trembled as he struggled against the weight bearing down on him.

At the edge of the battlefield, the Water Takeda calmly raised one hand, and a bow formed from condensed water in his grip, its surface shimmering with icy mana, and he drew the string back with his second hand, as a water arrow manifested instantly, pulsing with dense power.

He released it, but he didn’t stop there as he drew again, and again.

He ended up shooting four shots in rapid succession, each aimed at a different vital point.

Under normal circumstances, Patches might have dodged or intercepted them, but under the lingering pressure of the Wind Slam, his movement was severely restricted, he could only brace his different muscles as the arrows struck.

Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom!

Each impact carried not only crushing force but intense freezing energy, wherever an arrow struck, ice spread rapidly across his body, it wasn’t gradual, it raced outward aggressively, locking joints and stiffening movement.

His shoulders were hit first, as frost crawled across them instantly, making it difficult for him to lift his main arms.

Then the last two arrows were directed at his legs which weren’t kneeling, as the freezing energy spread instantly on impact, locking his lower limbs in place, with both his shoulders and legs frozen over, Patches finally lost his balance completely under the pressure, and as the Wind Slam reached its peak force, his entire body was driven violently into the ground.

[> Mid Ninjutsu Spell – Second Move – Swirling Tornado <]

After finally forcing Patches down, Patches seemed to blanked out from the strain as his eyes turned completely black, while above them, the Wind Takeda who was still levitating began slowly rotating his hands.

Immediately, the compressed wind below responded, as air currents twisted violently around Patches’ fallen body, forming a tight swirling vortex that lifted debris and fragments from the shattered stage.

It almost looked as if the winds were mirroring the exact motion of his hands, spiraling faster and tighter with every turn, wrenching Patches’ body within the miniature tornado.



============================================================
Chapter 1061: Chapter 1061: Patches And Beatrice 3.
============================================================

Without wasting time, Wind Takeda raised his hands toward the sky, as the small tornado he had formed mirrored his movement, lifting Patches who was caught inside it higher into the air.

Once Patches was high enough, he pushed his hands downward, and this time the tornado changed direction as its descent was dropping far faster than its ascent, and the wind that had been mirroring his motion accelerated violently as it crashed down.

Bang!

Another loud explosion rang out as dust swiftly blanketed the arena, wind Takeda hovered effortlessly with his wind wings flapping behind him, while the Water Takeda had already risen into the air.

Both stared into the dust cloud, even pushing their mental senses forward in an attempt to locate Beatrice or her remaining active puppet.

But no matter how hard they tried, they couldn’t get a clear read on either of them.

“Have you been able to sense the puppet’s realm?” Water Takeda finally decided to ask the one question that has been bothering him in his mind, as he fixed his gaze on the Wind Takeda.

Hearing the question, Wind Takeda’s brows lifted slightly, truthfully, he hadn’t been able to gauge the puppet’s realm since the fight began, he had just simply assumed it was a strong peak Tier 6 puppet based on its battle prowess.

“I didn’t get a proper read, but judging from the battle prowess it has shown so far, it should be Peak Tier 6, if not for its master supporting it, we should’ve gotten rid of it by now,” Wind Takeda replied confidently.

Just then, the dust slowly began to clear.

At the center of the arena stood Patches, casually cracking his neck and twisting his stitched arms together. Seeing how relaxed he looked forced Wind Takeda into deeper thought, because with the spell he had just casted he doesn’t believe there is any Tier 6 mage that would be able to bypass and survive it so casually without serious injuries.

“That is no Tier 6 puppet , it’s Tier 7 Puppet, she clearly hasn’t been using its full strength, as there must be a restriction or something” The Water Takeda who already had suspicions said as he dashed forward, noticing Beatrice had moved behind Patches, the frozen wooden spider puppet had disappeared which could only mean she had recalled it, as she was making slight adjustments on Patches by pressing glowing mana-lit points along his back.

“Shit,” Wind Takeda muttered, and he instantly didn’t see the need to keep holding on as he summoned his spirit equipment set, after the tattoos spread around him it appeared, with two new katana materialized on his back, parts of his black ninja attire shifted into a cyan hue, and the upper hood covering his hair retracted.

Only the mask from his nose downward remained.

His grey hair, not very long, flowed freely in the breeze, the moment the transformation finished, he began forming hand seals, but at the same time, Water Takeda’s body was suddenly blasted backward by a back hand punch from Patches sending him back towards Wind Takeda location after he had charged at him to stop whatever Beatrice was doing.

Wind Takeda instinctively tilted aside letting Water Takeda’s body fly past him, as his attempt to summon the Wind Gate spell was interrupted, from the corner of his eye, he saw Patches standing there, now emanating a far more dangerous aura than ever before.

It was completely different from earlier, just one look at Patches and it told him that the puppet was truly more dangerous than before.

Meanwhile, Beatrice looked close to losing consciousness, she sat cross-legged a short distance behind Patches, thick mana threads visibly linking her to the puppet, as she had her eyes closed.

Patches raised his upper right hand and used it to brush the hair away from his face, for the first time, the entire crowd saw his features clearly.

Contrary to expectations, he wasn’t grotesque or monstrous despite the stitched body. Many had assumed his face was hidden because it was ugly or disfigured.

Instead, what appeared was a strikingly handsome face, in fact the only irregularity on his face was the long stitch running from his lower right jaw across his cheek, almost as if it were holding his face together.

“Finally,” Patches said as the blue glow in his left eye burned brighter.

“Ten minutes — no, at most seven — is all I can maintain you at full strength, if you start casting spells, it’ll drain much faster,” Beatrice said, her face pale.

She was clearly straining herself to allow Patches to access his true power, all this time, he had been fighting purely with physical strength, even while restrained without Qi and without mana, he had still delivered blows comparable to a Peak Tier 6 mage.

“That’s enough time to get rid of the two of them,” Patches replied, raising his upper right hand and pointing at the two Takedas hovering in the air.

“Count again, bastard. I’m still in this,” the Earth Takeda said in a shaky voice, after remaining down for so long, he had finally gathered enough strength to stand.

“Oh... just you? You’re already exhausted. Get lost.”

Patches’ tone began amused but turned ice-cold by the end, his lower left arm, the scaled one that seemed taken from a reptilian humanoid beast, lifted and pointed toward the Earth Takeda.

At its fingertip, a purple-green energy began to gather, it compressed into a small sphere before Patches flicked his index finger, sending it hurtling forward.

Boom!

The projectile traveled so fast that the Earth Takeda, who was still recovering, couldn’t even react, it struck him square in the stomach and launched him high into the air.

His back slammed violently against the arena’s protective light shield before he began falling back down toward the field, even though the spectators knew the shield would prevent him from crashing into them, they still flinched as his massive body was hurled so effortlessly.

The Water Takeda reached him first, twisting his chain mid-air and tugging sharply, using the rotational momentum to accelerate the sickle toward Patches.

But Beatrice, who had her eyes closed as she floated slightly off the ground behind Patches, drew her right hand back, as a few of the mana strings attached to that hand came into view.

In response, Patches gave a slight nudge with his right shoulder, letting the Kusarigama pass just beside him, and he stepped in immediately with very few movements.

His lower right arm (the orc arm) slammed forward, the Water Takeda raised his leg to block, but a sharp crack echoed out, his face contorting in pain.

Before he could recover, Patches’ upper left hand seized his head and flung him outward, as his body shot into the air, hurtling straight toward the edge of the arena, clearly on course to be thrown out of the ring.



============================================================
Chapter 1062: Chapter 1062: Patches And Beatrice Final.
============================================================

“If you keep using moves like that, I’ll only last about three minutes, right now, five minutes is my absolute limit,” Beatrice complained, clearly unhappy that he had fired that poison beam at the Earth Takeda when other methods would have sufficed.

While she was busy scolding him, the Wind Takeda was already manipulating the air currents to stabilize the Water Takeda in air, who still seemed to be in pain.

“I have to admit, I never expected someone like you to push me this far. But clearly, I can’t keep fighting you this way,” the Wind Takeda said as he descended, deliberately creating distance between them.

“Then come at me!” Patches replied, swinging his sword, as a massive crescent arc of Qi burst from the blade and shot toward the Wind Takeda.

Yet unlike earlier, his expression remained calm, almost resolved.

“For this battle... I accept it as my loss, even if I end up winning it.” Wind Takeda said while staring straight at Beatrice, clearly she had been able to make him show more than he would have wanted.

[> High Tier Spell – Wind Gate. <]

The Wind Takeda immediately cast the high-tier spell, as his face instantly turned pale from the strain, but once the gate fully formed behind him, his complexion improved slightly.

The top-tier mages watching understood why they could see the not so invisible streams of Mana consumption; he had somehow distributed among his other bodies, as some of that even went into the summoned gate, with five peak Tier 6 mages taking the consumption it is clear they could cast more than one High rank spell.

However; he wasn’t done, as the Water Takeda landed beside him, still wincing from the damage to his leg, and together they began forming hand seals that many couldn’t tell what it represented.

By now the entire crowd was focused on the two Takedas, eager to see what their final move would be, many spectators were already speculating that the Wind Gate meant WInd Takeda intended to summon the remaining Takeda bodies, after all he already said he lost the battle so they were all trying to read meaning to his words.

After all, they felt if two couldn’t secure victory, then four might, especially when they were also considering the fact that Beatrice’s mana was running dry fast.

To most observers, they were starting to feel that it was beginning to look less like a duel and it would likely end as a battle of endurance.

However, while people were busy speculating about what the Takedas might do next, no one expected what followed, because from the very start, Takeda’s objective had been simple, force an early surrender, or, once forced with no other option than to expose some moves of his, he was going to beat submission out of their opponent.

[> Innate Skill – Body Merge: Back to Four <]

By that moment, the blade of Qi had already crossed the midpoint and was closing in fast on the two remaining Takedas.

Everyone waited for new bodies to emerge from the Wind Gate, but that never happened.

Instead, the two Takeda bodies dissolved into blue-green motes of energy, far denser and more potent than ordinary mana particles, and these energy forms slammed together at tremendous speed, as flesh rapidly began reforming from the collision.

A thin mist rose around the forming figure, almost as if deliberately shielding his naked body while reconstruction continued at a fast pace.

Bang!

The sword Qi finally struck, it wasn’t a light impact, from within the mist, a crystalline-looking hand that was covered in some type of water bubble defence shot out to block the attack, however it failed to stop it completely, but rather than being blasted away as before, his feet dug into the arena floor, carving long trenches as he was pushed backward.

Up in the third VIP tier, the Northern King was grinding his teeth. Some nearby spectators assumed he was frustrated that the War God Mage Academy seemed to be performing well — after all, many knew of his dislike for them.

But they were completely mistaken. His irritation wasn’t about the academy at all — it was about the arena itself.

The fog finally cleared, revealing Takeda’s newly formed body, he stood slightly taller than before, and his ninja attire had changed completely, now trimmed with flowing blue and cyan-green hues along the edges, showing he was still in his spirit equipment set mode, and it was quite baffling when the crowd started to process the fact that the sets of both Takedas also merged to form something new.

The Kusarigama was coiled tightly around his chest, one katana rested at his waist while another remained strapped behind him, his irises revolved in a Yin-Yang pattern, one side glowing blue like water, the other cyan-green like wind.

The moment he fully materialized, the entire crowd felt the pressure shift, the aura he emitted was on par with Patches who was standing across from him.

“Hoo... Marionette Girl, I don’t think I can keep fighting without casting spells now, at least I would have to coat each of my strikes with Qi” Patches muttered, slightly tilting his head so Beatrice could hear, and understand things were no longer going to be simple.

Bang!

Without wasting a single word, the newly merged Takeda dashed forward. No grand speech. No theatrics. Just pure speed.

The spectators were stunned, as the merged version radiated the presence of a Low Tier 7 mage a she moved.

[> Mid Tier Skill – Iaido <]

In one fluid motion, he drew the katana from his left waist using his right hand and executed a diagonal upward slash, it was so fast that many mages couldn’t even tell the blade had left its sheath until they saw the reinforced wind blade that followed, amplifying the Qi.

Patches reacted instantly, swinging his own Qi-coated blade to deflect the strike, as the Blade Qi which was reinforced with the wind blade spell flew into the air until it crashed against the arena shield.

Clang!!!

Before the audience could process the exchange, Takeda was already in front of him, slashing relentlessly. Strike after strike came in rapid succession, each one aimed at breaking Patches’ balance.

Whenever Patches attempted a counterattack, Takeda simply parried and pressed forward again, maintaining the initiative and refusing to give him breathing room.

At times he even swung the Kusarigama scythe around, but when the crowd looked closely, they realized something was different, he had done something to his weapons as the chain a the end of the Kusarigama was now attached to the hilt of his katana, creating a hybrid like weapon.

As the fight dragged on, he began casting spells they had never seen him use before, and those spells looked more like the combinations of both wind and water elements.

Storm bursts which flew more like water bombs compressed with wind elements , aqua gusts, which were blade-like water currents formed through the idea of the wind manipulation of wind blade spell as even the execution was the same way.

Patches responded in kind, casting earth spikes and poison beams as the two clashed fiercely across the battlefield. Despite the intensity, both fighters wore faint smiles, clearly enjoying the challenge.

But about three minutes later, Patches’ aura suddenly faded, as Beatrice fainted.

With her unconscious, the mana flow sustaining him vanished, turning his body stiffened, and his strength dropping sharply until he could only use his muscle strength, and before long, he was overwhelmed and defeated.



============================================================
Chapter 1063: Chapter 1063: The First Three 1.
============================================================

“I would love to fight with you another time again in the future, my master is the reason I’m restrained to such limits,” Patches said as he bent down to pick up Beatrice’s body.

“Well, next time I might have to go all out from the very beginning, because now that I know how much of a threat your master poses, and with me knowing much more about you now, I doubt you guys can win, as i would never give you the chance to even gain an advantage" the merged Takeda replied slightly implying that he might have held back during the battle.

“Well, hopefully we cross swords again.” With those last words, Patches gently handed Beatrice over to Brandon, who was already waiting at the edge of the battle ring to receive his teammate.

Afterward, Patches’ body blurred and returned to the coffin, the door creaked shut on its own once he entered.

While the merged version of Takeda then walked calmly into the still-open Wind Gate as the crowd erupted into loud cheers, although the School of Silent Night had won, many of the other participating mages that were observing the battle felt they had gained valuable information about Takeda’s abilities.

Though they still didn’t know his true limits, but it was now clear he could merge two bodies to reach Low Tier 7 mage strength.

And at the moment, they believe only one other mage seemed capable of matching him properly — and that was Ren, the captain of Shinsei Academy, mainly because of the fact that he could summon a spirit with Tier 7-level prowess.

Even then, matching Takeda and defeating him were two very different matters.

After all, aside from the two bodies that merged, there were still others main bodies remaining that have not even made their appearances, the vague possibility that more could merge or that the remaining bodies could combine with the current form made many uneasy, and Mages often feared the unknown most.

“TAKEDA!!!”

While competing teams worried about facing the seemingly unstoppable School of Silent Night, the crowd itself had no such concerns, they simply roared his name in excitement.

As those who supported the School of Silent Night began cheering loudly, they used the moment to air out their pent-up emotions, to them, it felt more like relief than pure celebration, because Patches had genuinely scared them.

For a brief moment, they had wondered what would happen if things didn’t go as expected, but now that the School of Silent Night had secured the win, nothing else seemed to matter much to them.

The fans quickly began bragging, claiming Takeda could have ended the match from the very beginning if he had simply merged his main bodies earlier.

Some even insisted the battle dragged on only because he wanted to have fun, making all sorts of excuses for him, yet when they tried to rub it in the faces of the fanatic War God supporters, those fans had little to say.

In their hearts, the supposedly unbeatable team had finally lost, and one question lingered among many of them — where exactly was Alec Gordons?

_ _

The remaining two matches of the day carried just as much noise and anticipation as the first, but unlike that opening clash, no one could confidently predict a winner.

In the second match, Bloodline Academy faced the Blade Clan team, despite the Blades arriving fully prepared and determined to push for victory, things didn’t go as planned right from the start.

Carmilla and Damien dashed forward, their eyes glowing with a crimson hue as Damien used his blood control to conjure a blood scythe for Carmilla.

Not far behind them, Dragov advanced while dragging his massive greatsword along the ground, while Connor and Kira moved along the wings behind them

Seeing such a direct and aggressive formation clearly aiming for a head-on clash, Kaelus immediately dashed forward to intercept Dragov.

In his mind, he intended to blast past the two fighters in front, believing they weren’t his real targets, he believed only fighting the captain of the other team was his main purpose.

But there was just one major problem, he wasn’t coordinating with the rest of his team. Charging forward like that without support would only get him surrounded.

Seeing him recklessly rushing into danger, especially after the elder had repeatedly warned them that only teamwork would secure victory, left Aeron feeling helpless. Kaelus had acted as if he listened earlier when the elder had scolded them both, yet now that the battle had begun, he was doing the exact opposite.

And the truth was simple — Kaelus was deliberately forcing Aeron’s hand, he knew Aeron wouldn’t leave him stranded.

As he closed in, Carmilla swung her Blood Scythe, aiming to cleave him straight down the middle. Kaelus reacted instantly, sliding on his knees as the crimson blade swept just inches from his face.

But Carmilla wasn’t about to waste the chance to strike down someone of captain-level standing, as she dug her foot into the ground, gripped the scythe with both hands, reversed its direction, and swung it back toward him.

At the same time, her brother Damien, who was positioned slightly behind her and facing the sliding Kaelus, extended his right index finger and cast a low-tier spell almost instantly.

[> Low Bloodline Spell – Blood Needles <]

At such close range, it looked impossible for Kaelus to evade the ten blood needles that formed and shot toward him, yet just before both the scythe and the blood needles could connect, a body suddenly flew into their midst.

Aeron had been launched forward by Darius Blade who had propelled him ahead, after gripping Aeron’s arm, twisting, and releasing him, Darius effectively hurled him forward like a projectile rather than a teammate.

Aeron reached the center with both short swords already drawn, the moment he arrived, he twisted his body and kicked Kaelus forward on his back, sending him toward Dragov — almost as if he were saying, “since you’re so eager to face the enemy captain, go ahead and fulfill that wish.”

In the process, Aeron managed to block Carmilla’s scythe, but he wasn’t able to stop all the blood needles, although he deflected most of them, a few still slipped through his defense.



============================================================
Chapter 1064: Chapter 1064: The First Three 2.
============================================================

Normally, the Blood Needle spell was considered a low Bloodline spell, but the top members of the Sorin bloodline and other clans experienced with blood-based abilities knew it could never truly be judged by its tier alone.

In the hands of an amateur blood mage, its real potential would never be reached, but in the hands of the right blood mage it became one of their most frequently used spells.

That was why many mages often wondered why blood mages still relied on the low-tier Blood Needle spell when there were supposedly stronger alternatives, practically speaking, blood spells always had the option to escalate in power as long as the proper conditions were met.

For a mage like Damien, who not only possessed a higher bloodline ceiling than most elders of his clan but also exceptional control over blood manipulation, it simply meant he had what it took to push the spell far beyond its supposed level.

Aeron ended up with about three blood needles embedded in his left shoulder, at first he didn’t care much, but the moment he was hit, Damien instructed his sister to deal with the remaining Blade clansmen alongside their teammates.

During the heated battle, Darius had managed to keep Connor occupied, preventing him from unleashing his great werewolf strength against the rest of the Blade clansmen. However, while he was busy restraining Connor, Carmilla remained unchecked.

With support from Kira Nightfang, the remaining two Blade clansmen were swiftly eliminated, they then circled back to where Connor was still locked in combat with Darius, and once the three of them joined forces, Darius was quickly pushed out of the battle ring.

Unlike the Blade clansmen, the Bloodline Academy members had no difficulty fighting either as a team or individually, they had clearly worked on patching their weaknesses since the last Northern Kingdom competition they participated in.

One of the biggest changes was Dragov himself, he no longer just swung his greatsword wildly, nor did he rely excessively on his bloodline power like before.

At this point, Dragov was executing smooth sword techniques, and not at a slow pace either, he swung the massive greatsword with one hand as if it weighed nothing, with each of his strikes carrying crushing weight and dense Qi, making it nearly impossible for Kaelus to properly display his own skills.

No matter what Kaelus attempted, Dragov’s overwhelming power broke through it.

When Dragov realized the fight would drag on at this rate, he decided to activate his Stage One Bloodline form.

The moment he transformed, he abandoned defense entirely and committed fully to offense, with every swing of his blade releasing massive crescent-shaped wind blades that tore across the battlefield.

This aggressive shift forced Kaelus onto the defensive, as he was forced to parried sword moves and deflected every incoming wind blade frantically, working to push them off one after another.

But even when his counterattacks landed, even when his enhanced Qi cut into Dragov’s Dragon scales, the injuries began healing almost instantly.

The regeneration in Dragov’s first stage shocked Kaelus, worse still, the wind blades that he had deflected didn’t dissipate, instead they remained suspended in the air, hovering as if they were being held in place by an unseen force.

Before long, more than a hundred wind blades floated behind him, following wherever the fight moved, then Dragov stepped back, drove his greatsword into the ground, and dragged his free hand through the air as if pulling invisible threads.

[> Mid Tier Spell – Wind Blade Barrage <]

That was the moment Kaelus realized he had walked straight into a carefully prepared trap, as the suspended blades suddenly began to rain down on him all at once following Dragov’s hand movement, and there was no way for him to block them all.

He was even forced to turn his back to Dragov just to survive the barrage, when it finally ended, despite deflecting as many as he could, deep, bone-cutting wounds covered his body, leaving him bloodied and shaken.

But at that same moment, Kaelus heard a sharp clap, Dragov had slammed his hands together, casting the Wind Slam spell.

While Kaelus had been busy deflecting the barrage, Dragov had already been preparing his next move, and when the wind pressure finally crashed down, it stunned the watching mages.

Only minutes earlier they had seen Wind Takeda perform a similar spell, but the range and sheer power behind Dragov’s version made it clear there were levels to this.

Kaelus tried to escape, but the spell’s radius was too wide, and the pressure instantly forced him straight to his knees.

Seeing him still struggling like that, Dragov knew sustaining the second phase of the spell wouldn’t be easy. Instead, he dashed forward and drove a heavy kick into Kaelus’s neck as he was still under the pressure of the wind slam and was unable to move, and that kick knocked him unconscious instantly.

He followed up with another kick, this one to the stomach, sending Kaelus lightly airborne and out of the battle ring.

When Dragov turned back, the fight between Damien and Aeron had already ended. Damien sat casually on the ground with a tired smile, completely unharmed, though clearly drained of mana.

He had gone all out from the start, determined to eliminate Aeron quickly, using the Blood Needles embedded in Aeron’s body, he disrupted his left hand movements and later activated a blood spike spell.

The blood needle inside Aeron’s hand formed massive spikes that burst outward, causing intense pain and nearly driving him mad, as part of his blood was also manipulated

Yet Aeron endured, even while bleeding heavily, he kept fighting with what little strength he had left. He defended, countered when possible, and refused to fall immediately.

In the end, Damien had exhausted his mana first, leaving him seated on the ground while Aeron remained standing ,battered but unyielding, but his whole team had fallen again and he couldn’t help sighing.

“You’re one tough guy, I’ll give you that,” Damien said just as Aeron finally lost consciousness after holding on that long, the Qi armor surrounding him fading away.



============================================================
Chapter 1065: Chapter 1065: He Is Back 1.
============================================================

The battle between Ember Citadel and Shinsei Academy began almost immediately, for this match, it was a clash between the Southern Kingdom mages fans and Eastern Kingdom mages fans in the stands.

As for the other three kingdoms, they mostly remained neutral spectators; as they didn’t feel the need to cheer loudly for either side, especially since a Northern Kingdom academy wasn’t involved, they didn’t feel that much excitement shading any team that wasn’t a northern academy as it won’t rile up the northern fans they have found so much pleasure in disturbing.

Still, that didn’t stop many from placing bets and sharing opinions about who they believed would win.

For most, they already felt it would be an easy victory for the Eastern academy — mainly because of Hoshino Ren, the Shinsei captain.

Many held the biased view that no one amazon the Ember Citadel team could match or stop him, given his Tier-7 spirit, conveniently forgetting that this was a team battle, not an individual duel.

At the same time, Ember Citadel wasn’t rated highly, mainly from the fact that they were a Southern Kingdom, this major fact worked against them; and there was also the fact that every other southern academy had already been eliminated, leaving Ember Citadel as the lone representative of the Southern Kingdom.

And in addition was the lingering prejudice against their darker skin and cultural background, and many assumed the judges themselves would prefer not to see what they unfairly labeled as “barbaric” or “local” mages advancing further.

_ _

But ten minutes into the fight, the entire stadium fell silent, no one could quite believe what they were witnessing.

The Shinsei Academy had been completely defeated, and Babatunde stood over Ren, who had collapsed on the battle stage, to the spectators it still felt so unreal to them.

Bare-chested, Babatunde displayed totem-like tattoos across his body, as it resembled a natural armor set, his flaming bracers glowed intensely, with faint steam rising from his body as if his power was still radiating outward.

He looked down at Ren’s unconscious body with a sly smile on his face , calm and composed despite the shock his victory had caused.

#Ten minutes ago.

When the judge called for the start of the match, the very first thing Babatunde did was swing his greatsword toward Ren, who stood at the front, the power imbued in the blade was so immense that a booming shockwave rang out as it cut through the air toward Ren on the opposite side.

With that single move, Babatunde effectively stopped Ren from summoning Akagami, as the weapon was already bearing down on him.

One of his teammates, Shun, immediately moved to cover him, stepping in front while Ren was already retreating backward from the incoming strike.

Shun raised both hands. Unlike Ren, he had just already begun summoning his beast power the moment the match started, so when he realized his captain was in danger, he reacted first.

Black smoke engulfed him, and a dark spirit shield appeared before him, its surface engraved with the face of a living Oni, seeing the shield, many watching mages quickly connected it to the vague demonic image they had glimpsed in the swirling black smoke.

Bang!

The sword finally struck. Shun instantly felt his arms go numb and silently cursed the sheer force behind the attack, he began wondering just how physically strong a mage had to be to unleash that level of power.

But while he was lost in those thoughts, the sword rebounded and flew back into the hands of Babatunde who had already begun dashing forward, Babatunde caught it smoothly, spun once, and hurled it forward again.

This time, the throw carried even greater force, leaving Shun barely any time to register what was happening before the blade was already on its way, the rest of the Ember Citadel members participating were Kwame, Olumide, Obi, and lastly Asha.

Kwame, Obi, and Olumide dashed forward just behind Babatunde, while Asha remained at the backline, forming rapid hand signs as flaming runes and sigils began to appear around her.

Soon, flaming familiars shaped like beast animals materialized, there were three in total, but the last one to appear stood out the most, it was a lion seemingly formed entirely from fire.

At that moment, Babatunde’s greatsword slammed down for the second time, and Shun, who was far less prepared to withstand such overwhelming force this time, was sent flying into the air.

His body crashed into Ren, who had been a few meters behind him trying once again to summon Akagami, seeing Ren’s attempt disrupted for the second consecutive time, Babatunde broke into a wide laugh as he and his teammates quickly moved aside.

Giving a line of sight to the flaming lion spirit that had just been summoned as it roared toward the spot where Shun and Ren stood, releasing blazing rings of fire that blasted toward them.

This time, another Shinsei mage stepped in to block the attack — Seishō Suijin.

He clapped his hands together, and about five spirit barriers formed in front of him, all facing the incoming flaming roar.

Three of the barriers shattered instantly they came in contact with the roar, but the fourth finally held firm.

Suijin was just about to sigh in relief when he suddenly saw cracks spreading across the light-green barrier.

This time, another flame spirit was responsible for the damage, a massive woodpecker made of fire had darted in at incredible speed that even Suijin didn’t notice until it was already before him, it repeatedly pecked at the cracked barrier.

Though the strikes were rapid and continuous, it took less than a second for the remaining barriers to collapse completely, at this point, anyone watching could clearly see Ember Citadel’s strategy.

They knew Hoshino Ren was their biggest obstacle to victory, so before he could summon Akagami, they intended to eliminate him first.

Normally, summoning a spirit shouldn’t have taken this much time, but Akagami was already a spirit whose realm was being restrained because of his host.

Since Ren hadn’t stepped into the high-rank realm yet, it was far harder for him to properly summon Akagami who was already a High Rank Spirit.

The process of summoning took several seconds between 40 to 45 seconds, and it couldn’t be interrupted or he would have to start all over again, if he were already in the high ranks, that wouldn’t be the case.

However, since he was still in the mid ranks, he remained limited by such restrictions, and this crucial detail was one Babatunde had carefully taken note of.

That was why he was going all out to knock Hoshino Ren out before he had any chance to summon his bound spirit, even though Babatunde believed he could win regardless, he wasn’t about to put himself through unnecessary stress when he could eliminate the problem early.

_ _

Meanwhile, elsewhere in Estonia City, the space axis had finally been repaired, and it began operating again with a whirling sound.

“Quickly send someone to the clan house to inform the young master that the teleportation axis has been repaired,” a mage instructed those nearby.

Without hesitation, they dashed out of the city hall at high speed, eager to deliver the news to Alec, who had been waiting for confirmation that it was fixed.



============================================================
Chapter 1066: Chapter 1066: The First Three Final.
============================================================

Despite knowing exactly what the Ember Citadel Team was trying to do, Shinsei Academy was still finding it hard to contain them.

This was due to certain conditions where they had put themselves on the line for their captain, believing that only with him in the battle would they have a higher chance of winning.

Unfortunately, this had left two of their teammates in a bad position, one was Shun as his Black Iron War Spirit shield had been canceled, and for a spirit mage, during the time when their connection with their spirits broke, they were left vulnerable.

This was because it would take a short while before they would be able to merge again with their Bounded Spirit, almost like some type of cooldown skill, as for Suijin, he was still in a much better situation, as he was able to curb the backlash from having his barriers broken the way they did.

Now, he was going around, trying his best to evade the strikes from the flaming lion and the flaming woodpecker, and honestly, he wasn’t doing that great of a job.

The woodpecker’s each strike seemed like an expert swordmaster-piercing skill.

Even when he retreated, trying to keep a safe distance between them, it could send the pierce like a sword light, only this time, it had a flaming aura coating it.

And that was just the trouble from the flaming woodpecker, the flaming lion was the most troublesome; it had used its claws to tear into his skin at intervals, the funny thing was that it didn’t even attack that much, it just frolicked around while the woodpecker bird attacked.

That way, it was able to not only draw Suijin’s attention by making him lose concentration, but it was also able to take advantage of any slip-up when it showed up.

While Asha was controlling and coordinating her flame spirit beasts, Kwame, Olamide, and Obi confronted Yurei and Kagekiri.

Though the Shinsei team members were good at such team battles, but against three members who seemed to have great synergy, they lost ground and were swiftly thrown out of the battle ring.

It wasn’t long before even Suijin could no longer hold on, as a well perfectly timed, massive paw strike slammed into his back and hurled him clean out of the battle ring.

With his elimination, only Ren and Shun remained.

At that exact moment, Ren finally completed the spirit summoning he had been trying to get settled since, as Akagami materialized in a swirl of crimson light and merged with him instantly.

Beside him, Shun’s cooldown had ended, he called forth his spirit once more, but this time he didn’t summon the defensive Black Iron War Spirit shield, instead, a heavy obsidian spear formed in his grip, as he showed that he was ready to fight shoulder-to-shoulder with his captain.

The Ember Citadel team took one look at them and burst into laughter.

Even though Ren had successfully brought out his high-rank spirit, it had cost him the elimination of three teammates, and the lone ally still standing beside him clearly wouldn’t be enough to stop what was coming.

Babatunde was the first to move.

He exploded forward, his body igniting in roaring flames which burnt his clothes and exposed his flaming totem tattoos, while his aura surged violently, teetering on the edge of breaking into the low Tier 7 realm.

In that instant he truly looked like the living incarnation of fire itself, as his blazing sword slashed down toward Ren.

Ren, who was now clad in ancient, battle-worn Japanese armor that seems to have been conjured from pure Qi, answered with his own blade, the weapon shimmered, with Qi shaping it into a long, gleaming katana.

Clang!

One precise strike from him deflected Babatunde’s sword and sent his hand away, Ren then twisted his wrist, driving the katana forward, as the tip sank deep into the muscle just inches below Babatunde’s shoulder.

With a vicious upward slash, he ripped the blade free from Babatunde’s flesh, as blood went out spraying across the ring, before Babatunde could recover, Ren spun smoothly and delivered a lightning-fast one-two kick straight into his chest, blasting him backward.

Babatunde dropped to one knee when he finally got rid of the kicks momentum, pain flashing across his face, yet when he lifted his head, that same arrogant smile was still plastered on his lips.

Only then did Ren realize the truth.

He was completely surrounded, but he didn’t show any fear.

He simply tilted his katana through the air, flinging the blood from its blade in a crimson arc, then slowly sheathed it back into its scabbard, before spreading his legs wide, as he dropped into a balanced stance.

“You might not think too much of me, since a single peak Tier 6 mage would never amount to a Tier 7 mage, after all, the high rank is another stage entirely, but what about when you add four more peak Tier 6 mages into the fray?” Babatunde said as he stood back up.

The gash on his shoulder ignited with flames, sealing the wound shut, not truly healed, but forcefully cauterized, and despite all that he kept his smile not even groaning with all the pain he was feeling.

“Go!” With a sharp forward thrust of his hand, the other four charged toward Hoshino Ren; at that moment, the crowd finally witnessed Hoshino in his full glory.

Even against four peak Tier 6 mages, he held his ground, he took cuts here and there, but for every minor wound he suffered, he made sure the Ember Citadel mages traded it for a far more critical hit in return, they were trying their best to hurt him since despite his attack and moves having Tier 7 prowess he still had a Tier 6 body.

Even Kwame endured a slash so deep it nearly severed his right arm at the shoulder, but before Ren could finish the cut, Olamide thrust his short sword forward from the side, forcing Ren to abandon the attack and dodge out of the way.

In the midst of evading that strike it freed up space, as Dakarai Obi lunged in and slammed his fist into Ren’s face, the punch’s power was amplified by the gauntlet he wore.

A sharp explosion rang out, however, not from Ren’s face, but from Obi’s gauntlet itself, as the force from the gauntlet rippled through the air.

Obi pressed the assault, dashing forward with a rapid one-two: a straight jab to the nose followed by a cross just a the lower jaw, the barrage kept Hoshino Ren on the defensive, unable to mount a proper counter.

But after about ten flurry blows, Ren finally seized his momentum back, as he blocked the next punch with his blade which he switched to his left hand inversely and retaliated with a palm strike from his right hand to Obi’s chest, shoving him back a step.

Then, in a blur, he unleashed two swift slashes in opposing directions, carving an X-shaped gash across Obi’s entire chest after switching the katana to his right hand.

Blood welled up in its wake.

However; Ren didn’t even wait, as he dashed past the staggering Obi, who spurted blood from his mouth, he continued swinging his blade toward Asha’s spirit lion, this brutal exchange raged on for over Five minutes.

Despite their mounting injuries, the Ember Citadel team pressed relentlessly. In the end, a still mostly unscathed Babatunde awaited an exhausted Ren, who now had precious little mana left to sustain his merge with Akagami after defeating all four mages.

And that was when the real pounding began. Babatunde discarded his greatsword entirely, opting instead to pummel Ren with his bare fists, hoping to beat the living daylights out of him.

His strategy mirrored that of his teammates: trade injury for injury. Fortunately for Babatunde, Ren was already gravely wounded from taking down the other four, so he collapsed even before his mana fully depleted.

That was how Ember Citadel emerged victorious, the entire southern stands erupted in a standing ovation, mages cheering wildly as the sole representing academy from their kingdom had finally qualified.

While fans from the Eastern Kingdom accused them of tricks and foul play, supporters from the Southern Kingdom insisted they had won through superior tactics and endurance.

And thus, the first three teams to advance to the next stage were determined:

School of Silent Night

Bloodline Academy

Ember Citadel

The next day would feature the loser’s bracket battles to decide which team would join the other three and compete for the fourth spot, this was literally the last loser’s bracket match that would allow an academy to advance; the losers of the next round would merely fight to determine third and fourth place.



============================================================
Chapter 1067: Chapter 1067: He Is Backkk 2.
============================================================

It was nighttime in the capital, and though the hour was late, celebrations still raged in many corners of the city, as mages from the Eastern and Southern kingdoms joined the Northern citizens in revelry, toasting to the three academy teams that had advanced to the semi-finals of the competition, because they belonged from their kingdom.

As for the mages from the Central and Western kingdoms, they kept to themselves, sequestered in their suites and inns.

While all this merriment unfolded, the three qualified academy teams had decided to rest and enjoy the night. Meanwhile, the remaining academies slated for the final loser’s bracket battles were locked in intense meetings with their deans.

Amidst it all, at the capital Space Axis, two figures emerged.

Alec and Terran had finally returned to the capital city, without delay, they took to the skies, flying straight toward the quarters assigned to the War God Mage Academy, after being informed of the restoration of Estonia City’s Space Axis, Alec had fetched his master.

He was deeply worried about the state of his team, he could only hope things had gone well with them since he had left.
