He began imagining Bulwark would raise both of his arms up to block in an high defense, only to be pushed backward by the impact, the rebound would then send his shield flying back to him, giving him the chance to seize the initiative and rush forward himself to close the distance.

But reality rarely unfolds the way one imagines, because the moment Bulwark saw the shield flying toward him at high speed, he suddenly dropped low and slid forward.

The large shield strapped to his back pressed against the ground, acting almost like a sled that transported him.

Because most of the battlefield had already been covered in Titan’s ice domain, Bulwark’s sliding speed actually increased as he glided across the frozen surface, as Shun’s shield passed just right above him.

With that single movement, Bulwark not only avoided the attack but also closed the distance far faster than Shun expected.

And he came to a stop when he got into range of Shun, Bulwark lashed out with his right leg, sweeping it hard into Shun’s right leg.

Whack!

The strike was so sudden that Shun immediately dropped to one knee as his balance collapsed, before he could recover, Bulwark followed up with a brutal uppercut, driving the small shield mounted on his right arm diagonally into Shun’s jaw.

Just moments before the blow landed, Shun had already stretched his hand out, trying to recall his thrown shield after realizing his attack had been completely bypassed.

But now his shield, which had been hanging in midair and returning to him, suddenly lost its power after the uppercut landed and dissipated in the air.

Although the strike had momentarily stunned him, Shun still had a bit of strength left, as he tried to throw a punch downward, but Bulwark simply raised his arm and covered his face while he cast a spell.

In the next moment, a block of ice suddenly formed around Shun’s head, causing him to stagger backward, struggling to breathe as the freezing construct imprisoned his face.

Once the ice block formed, Shun completely abandoned the punch he had intended to deliver, and with Shun effectively neutralized, Bulwark stepped forward and launched another uppercut.

This time the blow was decisive, cursing Shun to lose consciousness instantly, this time he was unable to resist the darkness that overtook him as his body levitated slightly into the air before falling back with a loud thud sound.

On the brighter side, the block of ice encasing his head shattered into fragments as he collapsed.

“Bulwark!”

Just as he finished dealing with his opponent, Bulwark heard his name being called, he didn’t even bother turning to look.

Instead, he simply twisted his body and swung his left arm outward, sending the curved shield with the half-moon cut spinning toward the opposite side.

Clang!

The shield flew straight into a barrage of wind blades that had been rushing toward Glacius, intercepting and dispersing them, with the threat handled, the shield rebounded through the air.

Glacius extended his left hand and caught it effortlessly, as he spun and sent it back towards Bulwark back, Kiri, upon seeing her attack blocked, quickly began casting another spell, but it was already too late.

Glacius had closed the distance, he didn’t just turn to send the shield flying; he had also used that same momentum to swing his ice spear, specifically positioning the shaft sideways.

The blunt strike cracked against Kiri’s head, knocking her unconscious instantly.

Just like that, Hoshino Ren became the only active member left from the Shinsei Academy.

And judging from the way his battle with Alec was unfolding, things weren’t looking promising for him either.

At that moment, Ren was already panting heavily, the prolonged use of his merged state had clearly begun to backfire, and it seemed he had shot himself in the foot by maintaining it for too long.

In truth, Ren had never properly reached the level required to merge with a much higher-ranked spirit. Normally, however, he was able to defeat his opponents before the strain of that limitation began to show.

But against Alec, things were different, he hadn’t been able to get the upper hand against Alec at all. In fact, deep down he could tell that if Alec truly wanted to end the fight, he probably could have done so already.

Yet the battle continued, and Alec kept fighting using only his Changdao which is coated in Blood Killing Intent, that was how Ren’s Crimson Qi continued clashing against Alec’s bloodlust-infused blade.

Now, however, Ren had used far too much Qi and was reaching his limit, and maintaining Akagami’s Crimson equipment manifestation for such a prolonged period had pushed him to the brink.

He wasn’t just in a dire state, he was also completely exhausted, as he knew drawing further power from Akagami to stabilize himself would begin draining his life force, yet as he looked at Alec who seemed increasingly relaxed while casually deflecting his katana—Ren couldn’t shake the feeling that even that sacrifice might not be enough to secure victory.

Alec watched him silently, waiting, but the longer he observed Ren, the more it seemed like he wouldn’t gain much more from this fight again.

Ren believed Alec had been toying with him, but that wasn’t the truth, for the first time since Alec’s blade and sword mastery entered the advanced level, he had encountered someone capable of pushing him to genuinely swing his weapon with full focus.

Alec had been completely locked into the battle, at the same time, he had been receiving real-time notifications that his mastery was improving.

In a way, Ren had unknowingly become a grinding partner for Alec’s mastery progression, and the smile on Alec’s face came from that satisfaction.

But Ren misunderstood it, from the very beginning, Ren had felt that Alec never took them seriously, largely because of Alec’s calm and confident demeanor.

In reality, it wasn’t arrogance, it was simply Alec’s confidence in his own strength.

“Let’s end this. One final move,” Ren said as he raised his glowing crimson katana high into the air.

He poured every last bit of Qi he had left into the blade, as the energy surged violently, and behind him a towering phantom image began to manifest, the figure happened to be Akagami and he mirrored Ren’s movement, amplifying the pressure radiating from him.

Seeing this, Alec smiled, he had heard a great deal about Ren’s power from Agnes before the battle, and out of respect he decided to go all out for this final exchange.

“Alright.” Alec replied calmly.

He spun his sword once through the air before sliding it back into its scabbard, the moment the blade settled inside, the scabbard began to vibrate slightly.

Alec then took his stance, with his legs spread apart, while his right foot slid forward, and his right hand rested firmly on the hilt of the changdao, while his left hand pressed against the middle of the scabbard to stabilize it.

At the same time, both mages prepared their finishing techniques.

[> Mid Tier Skill – Cleave <]

[> Mid Tier Skill – Iaido <]



============================================================
Chapter 1077: Chapter 1077: Qualified.
============================================================

“Leave them. They are mine.”

While Alec and Hoshino Ren were locked in their clash, Titan decided to deal with the Blade Clan members himself.

When he spoke, his gaze fell directly on Brandon, Arthur, and Knight, he completely ignored Lucas, who was still bleeding but seemed to be holding his own against Aeron.

Both of them were laughing wildly as they exchanged blows, their weapons clashing repeatedly while enhanced by Qi.

Although Lucas appeared to have the advantage in raw strength, as each swing of his glaive forced Aeron several steps back, but Aeron was clever enough to maneuver around some of the attacks and search for faster ways to take Lucas down.

However, with lightning coursing through Lucas’ body, his reaction speed and evasiveness had greatly increased making it hard for Aeron to swiftly finish him in a very fast speed.

Because of that, he managed to stay on his feet and avoided getting trapped or pinned down by Aeron’s tactics.

After Titan spoke, the three Gordon clan members reluctantly stepped back to clear the way, though they weren’t happy about it—in fact, they felt confident about winning the fight with a little more time, but when it came to Titan, there was a small amount of fear they couldn’t ignore.

With the Gordons moving aside, Titan began walking toward the three Blade Clan fighters, his expression still cold and emotionless, seeing they had little choice than to face this scary Golem before them, the three of them raised their weapons and rushed towards him together.

The first to reach him was Darius, he was gripping his claymore with both hands, as he swung from the left in a powerful horizontal cleave, aiming straight for Titan’s neck.

Not far behind him, Talon Blades attacked from the right, the moment his fist shot forward, a small image of a roaring dragon formed in flames around his arm.

Though only the head of the dragon was clearly visible, the fire extended outward like a blazing neck following the path of his punch.

Meanwhile, Sora took the rear position, as he struck from behind, delivering long-range support attacks, every time he punched forward with his tonfas, compressed wind pressure shot out toward Titan.

And in a split second, Titan demolished all three of them.

First, a short ice axe materialized in his left hand, as he swung it and hooked the blade of Darius’ claymore, trapping the weapon mid-swing.

Before Darius could react, Titan’s right leg slammed into his knee, forcing him down, as Darius lost his balance and began to fall, Titan’s leg rose again and smashed into his jaw, sending him sprawling backward in an awkward crash.

With Darius temporarily out of the fight, Titan extended his left palm and caught Talon’s flaming dragon punch head-on.

Not only did he stop the attack, but he tightened his grip around Talon’s fist as his frost energy quenched the fiery energy that was being released by Talon’s fist, at the same time, Titan swung his left hand, and an ice mace materialized instantly. In one swift motion, he whacked it against Talon’s head from the side.

The blow knocked Talon unconscious immediately, while the ice mace broke down back into ice element particles.

Titan then grabbed Talon’s limp body and pulled him in front of himself just as the compressed wind blasts fired by Sora arrived.

Boom!!!

Titan used Talon’s unconscious body as a human shield while the wind pressure attacks slammed into his back, each impact made Talon’s body jerk violently.

The powerful wind strikes tore into his back, causing ruptures in his clothing and flesh, but Titan clearly didn’t care, once the barrage ended, Titan casually tossed Talon’s body aside.

At that moment, Darius suddenly reappeared, bending low with his claymore poised as he thrust it forward toward Titan.

Titan simply caught the blade with his bare hand.

Frost energy immediately spread across the weapon, racing along the steel and creeping toward Darius’ grip. Realizing the danger, Darius barely managed to wrench his hand free before the ice reached him.

But Titan was already moving again.

His left hand shot forward and grabbed Darius by the neck, lifting him into the air as he gasped for breath and tried to break Titan’s lock on his neck.

However, Titan’s freezing aura kept seeping into him, as Darius’ face began turning pale, as if the blood inside his body were slowly freezing.

Seeing this, Sora launched another attack toward Titan, this time though, Titan shifted the suspended Darius to the side and thrust his right hand forward toward Sora’s position, not interested to let him run wild.

In the next instant, jagged ice spikes erupted from the ground, shooting up toward where Sora stood.

It happened so fast that Sora had no chance to escape, the ice spikes ensnared him completely, lifting him off the ground, pushed him until he was pinned him against the arena wall as if he had been crucified, his arms spread wide while the rest of his body was trapped inside thick, jagged ice.

Once Titan finished dealing with Sora, he turned his gaze back toward Darius. Slowly raising his right hand, he pointed two of his fingers toward Darius’ head and fired a thin ice beam.

Boom!

A muffled sound echoed, the condensed energy within the beam wasn’t powerful enough to kill him, but it carried just enough force to knock him unconscious instantly.

While this was happening, Alec and Ren’s final attacks finally collided.

In the aftermath of the exchange, their positions had completely switched, with Alec now standing where Ren had been moments ago, while Ren stood where Alec had once been.

The entire arena fell silent, and then a second later the crowd noticed a long line of blood stretched across Alec’s chest.

Gasps erupted from the section filled with Northern Kingdom supporters, as they couldn’t believe that he was cut, but Alec simply placed his hand on his chest, touched the blood and smiled.

“That wasn’t half bad,” Alec said calmly.

“That was... a beautiful sword skill,” Ren replied with his back still turned to Alec, his voice trembling slightly as he spoke before his body collapsed forward onto the ground.

Only then did the spectators realize that Ren had actually been the one who lost the exchange, but then he had fallen face-first, making it difficult for them to immediately see the true extent of Alec’s strike.

With Ren down, only Aeron remained standing, however; the remaining fighters of the War God Team stepped back and allowed the duel between Aeron and Brandon to continue without interference.

In the end, both fighters exhausted themselves completely and collapsed, with Bradon looking like he might go into a coma, even so, the War God Mage Academy still had four mages left standing.

At the moment the result became clear, and the fanatic supporters erupted into deafening cheers even before the judge dictated them the winner.

Once again, Alec’s name echoed throughout the arena as he reintroduced himself in spectacular fashion, but while his teammates celebrated and waved toward the roaring crowd, Alec wasn’t looking at them.

Instead, his gaze remained fixed on Takeda, who had been watching the entire battle, though, considering what he had heard from Agnes, Alec supposed that the body watching the battle was most likely not one of Takeda’s main bodies.

It was probably just a clone, one that Takeda probably must have sent to observe the match and gather information about who his next opponents would be.



============================================================
Chapter 1078: Chapter 1078: Thoughts About The New Team.
============================================================

Alec’s name echoed loudly through the arena as the fanatic supporters among the Northern Kingdom crowd chanted it again and again, the way they celebrated made it seem as though they had already won the entire tournament.

Meanwhile, Alec’s gaze remained focused on Takeda, even though Agnes had mentioned that the one watching was likely just a clone, Alec still had the strange feeling that the real Takeda might somehow be able to observe through it.

And if that was the case, Alec wanted him to understand something clearly, that deep down, he was already looking forward to defeating him.

_ _

About two minutes later, Alec had left the stage, walking through the same tunnel his team had used to enter earlier.

Even after the battlefield was cleared and the participants had left, the discussions throughout the arena didn’t stop, in the VIP sections, the high-ranking spectators were already engaged in heated conversations.

“Finally, he’s back, let’s see what’s new this time.” Dragov said as he stood up.

The rest of the Bloodline Academy team rose with him, their expressions filled with interest, they too looked clearly eager for a chance to battle the War God Mage Team.

From the Ember Citadel team, however, only one person was present—the vice-captain, El-Masri.

She didn’t say a word. Instead, she simply frowned before standing up and heading toward the exit, what she had witnessed today weighed heavily on her mind.

As she walked, she kept calculating the probability of her team defeating the other qualified teams.

Secretly, she has been hoping that whichever team emerged from the loser’s bracket would be weaker and give her team better chances of winning against them in case they were paired together in the semi final.

But deep down, she had already realized something troubling, what she had just witnessed might have been an even greater monster than she anticipated.

After all, Alec had gone head-to-head with a mage whom her entire team had struggled to defeat, even after relying on careful strategies and teamwork. And he hadn’t just fought Ren...

He had won.

Yes, Titan might have been responsible for the weakened state Ren found himself in the fight, but El-Masri refused to let that cloud her judgment, because she understood one undeniable truth.

Her team wouldn’t just have to deal with Alec, they would also have to face his golem... Him and the rest of his team.

_ _

While the mages who had come merely to spectate the match began leaving after confirming who the fourth team would be, many other events were still unfolding within the first and second VIP levels.

On the second VIP level, Old Man Alderic burst into loud laughter, unable to contain his excitement. This time, no one dared say anything to interrupt him.

Even the Dean looked satisfied, the War God Mage Academy had managed to defeat two academies at once, an achievement that spoke for itself.

In doing so, Alec had not only silenced any attempts to downplay their victory, because claiming their win was unimpressive would also imply that the other teams they face were much weaker,

But they had also been able to qualify in a way that no one could reasonably question, after defeating both teams alone without having to do it three-ways.

Meanwhile, the King of the Northern Kingdom stormed out in anger, and a few minutes later, he appeared inside his throne room, where two mages knelt before him.

“Why in the hell was the information about Alec returning to the city not reported to me immediately?” the King demanded.

The two mages trembled in fear. After a few seconds, one of them finally gathered the courage to step forward and speak.

“My King, I am deeply sorry, the space axis was used very late last night, and we only received the report this morning. At the time, we didn’t think much of it. It was only after we arrived at the arena and saw him appear that we realized he must have been one of the mages who used the space axis during the night.”

Seeing his companion speak, the second mage reluctantly added his own explanation.

“And... my King, you previously instructed us never to disturb you at night under any circumstances, since that is the time you spend with the queens in your harem.”

“Insolence!” The King roared the moment he heard that.

The two mages immediately pressed their foreheads to the floor, trembling as they begged for mercy.

Though the King was furious at what had been said, it also gave him a moment to calm down and think more clearly.

If the news about Alec had truly been brought to him that late at night, he probably would have dismissed the messengers before they even had the chance to explain.

In fact, if he had been in a worse mood than usual, he might have even ordered them beheaded on the spot, and in the morning he had already spent time having breakfast with one of his queens before heading straight to the battle arena.

Thinking about it carefully, he realized there really might not have been any proper way for them to reach him in time.

“But if he came back... then what happened in Estonia City?” the King asked.

This second question immediately put the two servants in an even more difficult position, and it took them some time to gather their thoughts, but after nearly ten minutes they were able to explain everything they had heard about the events in Estonia City.

Though they couldn’t provide full details, only the main reports that had been passed along through their channels, according to the information they received, after several days of besieging the Gordons Clan the City Lord army was finally able to breach into their core territory, where the decisive battle had begun.

At first, everyone believed the City Lord’s army would win.

However, as the fighting continued, the spies stationed around the area reported that the sounds of battle were growing increasingly violent.

Before anyone could understand what had truly happened, everything changed.

The battle that they predicted would last days and it would end with the Gordons Clan collapsing and its surviving members hunted down like stray dogs for information about their other clansmen who were able to escape—never occurred.

Instead, the opposite happened, the Gordons Clan won.

The outcome caused a massive uproar, and what followed forced the capital to intervene in a matter of jurisdiction.

After all, the city’s main governing force had effectively been overthrown.

King Charles would have gladly used the situation as an excuse to punish and eliminate the Gordons Clan entirely, but he knew better than to act recklessly.

With the kingdom’s laws in place and countless witnesses capable of testifying to what truly happened, it would be foolish for him to fabricate accusations.

In fact, given how things had unfolded, the only option he could think of now was to instigate the Dragonmir Clan Patriarch to act.

After all, one of the cities governed by a branch member of his clan had now fallen into the hands of another clan.

The truth was, he had already known about the City Lord’s intention to attack the Gordons Clan, but just like how the War God Mage Academy had spies with-in Estonia City itself, he had secretly sent his own mages a few days earlier to investigate the clan that had managed to produce a talent like Alec.

That investigation was when he first learned that the Gordons Clan would soon be attacked, yet even after discovering such a situation, he chose to do nothing.

Deep down, he actually supported the move. Since he couldn’t openly dirty his own hands, he had been more than willing to let those minor branch mages of the Dragonmir Clan deal with the Gordons for him.

If the attack had succeeded, he would have simply swept the entire matter under the rug.

But now that they had lost, the situation could no longer be hidden.

He would have to either appoint a new City Lord or formally recognize the Gordons Clan as the new guardian clan of Estonia City.



============================================================
Chapter 1079: Chapter 1079: Another Format.
============================================================

By the next day, all four teams that had qualified for the semi-finals were already waiting for the match format and battle pairings to be announced.

From the changing room, they could clearly hear the roaring cheers of the crowd as everyone waited for the day’s matches to begin, while they were still waiting, the Dean walked in, accompanied by Miss Selene, who had come to wish them good luck.

After all, she had been the one who originally selected the team, and seeing how far they had progressed filled her with pride, they had definitely grown far beyond anything she had imagined when she first chose them.

After the team greeted her and she exchanged a few brief words with Alec, the Dean finally spoke.

“The team we will be facing has been confirmed,” the Dean began.

That single sentence immediately captured everyone’s attention as they turned toward him.

“We will be facing the Bloodline Academy, and the format for this match has also been decided, it will be something called a Rune-Point Domination Match.”

He paused briefly before continuing.

“The arena array system will include something known as a Creation Stone.”

“Although it’s called a Creation Stone, its function isn’t limited to changing the terrain, it will also be able to generate a temporary astral body for each of you. That astral body will be deployed onto the battlefield in place of your real body.”

“The most important part of this system is that each of you will have three lives during the match.”

Everyone in the room began smiling as they waited for the rest of the explanation.

However, the more Alec listened, the more uneasy he felt, from his experience, tournament formats rarely favored them and something about this one made him suspect it might backfire.

“And by three lives,” the Dean continued,

“I mean that if you are killed during the battle, you will be able to reincarnate and rejoin the fight, since it’s only your astral body participating, your real body won’t be harmed with the same wound that was inflicted on you during the dead period.”

“However, keep this in mind, that each death will leave you feeling extremely fatigued once the battle ends. It’s simply a side effect of the astral connection, nothing too serious.”

Hearing this, no one seemed to have any problems with it. After all, they clearly understood what it meant to be able to reincarnate three times in a battle.

They were fully aware that such an advantage would naturally come with some form of cost, and since to them the only price to pay was temporary fatigue after the match, it didn’t seem like a serious drawback to them.

“There will be five Rune Points spread across the battlefield,” the Dean continued.

“Your mission is to capture and control them, the more Rune Points your team controls, the faster your score will increase.”

He then paused briefly before adding the final rule.

“And there is one restriction—summons are not allowed.”

The moment the Dean finished speaking, everyone finally understood why this new format had been introduced, even the most simple-minded mage could tell that this rule had been specifically designed to target them, because they were the only team surviving who seems to have a summoning type of mage available.

Alec, on the other hand, merely chuckled after hearing the rule, it was obvious to him that summoning Titan during the previous match must have frightened certain people enough that they decided to impose restrictions to prevent him from using that advantage again.

And by those people, Alec was thinking about King Charles of the Northern Kingdom and the group of mages who carried out his orders must have talked and worked out things with the Judges.

What Alec couldn’t understand, however, was why they hadn’t arranged for his team to face the School of Silent Night instead, as it was publicly acknowledged as the strongest team of the moment.

He had actually been looking forward to that matchup.

The fact that their captain, Takeda, had been responsible for leaving Lucas and Beatrice in such terrible condition still irritated him.

Even though he knew Takeda had simply done what was necessary to qualify, Alec couldn’t completely shake off the grudge he felt.

Still, since that battle wasn’t happening now, he forced himself to focus on the opponent his team had actually been assigned to face, the Bloodline Academy.

When he thought about it carefully, he realized how disadvantageous the situation seemed for them.

First of all, Alec was already familiar with this type of battle format, even though his teammates looked slightly confused, he had seen similar systems in games he used to play back on Earth, games like Call of Duty or Mech Arena, where the objective revolved around capturing and holding strategic points on the battlefield.

But this situation was very different, one of the rules stated that a player had to remain inside a rune circle for ten uninterrupted seconds before their team could successfully capture the point.

After a rune point was captured, it would generate one point per second for the controlling team.

That meant the only way to win was to constantly maintain control over more rune points than the opponent, and the total score required to win was 500 points.

Alec couldn’t help but feel that this format had clearly been designed to target them, if he had been allowed to summon his golems, securing the rune points would have been far easier.

On top of that, Beatrice could have also used her puppet to help them cover grounds, but since her puppets also counted as summons, she was affected by the restriction as well.

He shook his head slightly, realizing that whoever designed the format had thought everything through very carefully, they clearly believed the rest of Alec’s team wouldn’t pose much of a threat, especially since Bloodline Academy was known for having an extremely well-balanced team.

Of course, there was still another way to win the match, and that is by eliminating every member of the opposing team if they couldn’t compete effectively in the rune point battle.

But even that possibility had been partially neutralized by the three-life reincarnation system.

Earlier, his teammates had been celebrating the advantage of having multiple lives, but they hadn’t realized that it also made it harder to win through pure combat.

Alec simply smiled.

He felt strangely satisfied knowing that he had scared certain people enough for them to constantly change the rules just to limit him, and honestly, he welcomed the challenge.

If anything, it only made him more determined to break their carefully crafted system in a way they would never expect.

After all, nothing brought him more satisfaction than shocking people who were certain he couldn’t win.



============================================================
Chapter 1080: Chapter 1080: The Re-Match.
============================================================

While the Dean was busy bringing them up to speed on how the new battle format would work, outside in the arena the judge was already addressing the crowd, explaining the updated rules and how the match would be carried out.

_ _

After a few minutes, all the proper introductions had been completed by the judge. It was finally time for him to usher in the teams that would be competing.

“For the two matches taking place today,” the judge announced, “we will first have Bloodline Academy facing War God Mage Academy. The second match will be between the Ember Citadel Team and the School of Silent Night.”

From inside their tunnels, the teams could clearly hear the judge’s voice echoing through the arena.

It wasn’t as if they didn’t already know who their opponents would be, but hearing it announced again made everything feel far more real.

For the mages of Ember Citadel, simply hearing the name School of Silent Night sent chills down their spines. Even though no one openly talked about it, it had become an unspoken truth among the competing teams—everyone was trying their best to avoid facing them.

Not only had no one figured out a reliable way to counter Takeda, who still hadn’t even revealed his full strength, but fighting someone who possessed five bodies was already troublesome enough.

There were also rumors circulating that Takeda’s main bodies wouldn’t be considered summons under the new rules. The restriction only applied to external clones or summoned entities, meaning his core ability would remain largely unaffected.

As Babatunde sat quietly, listening from the Ember Citadel side, he couldn’t help but let out a small snicker.

To him, it almost felt ridiculous.

The judges had introduced a new rule that supposedly restricted certain abilities, yet it barely affected the School of Silent Night at all. If anything, it only placed small limitations on them while pretending the playing field had been balanced.

The more he thought about it, the more it seemed as if the judges were subtly favoring Takeda—almost like they were quietly guiding him toward victory.

_ _

“Now let’s welcome the first team into the battle arena, from the right side, the mages of Bloodline Academy,” the judge announced, beginning the introductions.

As they stepped out, Dragov Dragonmir walked at the forefront, a short distance behind him was Damien, followed by Carmilla, Lupus Connor, and finally Kira Nightfang.

It almost looked as if they had arranged themselves according to their hierarchy within the team.

As usual, their uniforms were striking and stylish, as each mage wore the same base design, but the edges were accented with different colors, almost as if the uniforms had been tailored to complement each of them individually.

The primary color of the attire was a dark crimson-black, giving off an intimidating aura.

For Dragov, the edges of his uniform, designed almost like a military coat—were cyan. Damien’s accents were a vivid blood-red, perfectly matching the nature of his vampire bloodline, while Carmilla’s color was also red, but hers carried a slightly darker shade, far less bright than Damien’s.

Meanwhile, Lupus’s trim was silver, reminiscent of the moon, while Kira’s was pure black, making it seem as if her uniform carried no special accent at all.

The moment the Bloodline Academy team appeared, many of the high-class mages from the Northern Kingdom began to cheer loudly.

Meanwhile, the fanatical supporters of War God Mage Academy simply sat back and looked around with a certain air like the Bloodline Academy was none of their concern for the day.

To them, Alec had returned, and that alone meant that no obstacle truly mattered, they firmly believed that since Alec had once defeated Dragov, he would be able to do it again, this time with even less effort.

Seeing the northern crowd, which greatly outnumbered them, finally divided now that two academies from the Northern Kingdom were facing each other brought smiles to the faces of many mages from other kingdoms who had chosen to sit back and watch this match neutrally.

After all, the Northern Kingdom fans had been heavily mocked during the earlier stages of the tournament, and there was a saying back then that their academies would never amount to much, however now they had two academies in the semi-finals.

Thinking about it now, none of them had expected things to turn out this way.

The only Northern academy most people had truly rated from the beginning was Bloodline Academy, as not only had they proven their strength repeatedly, but records also showed that they were the official champions of the Northern Kingdom.

In fact, the remaining teams in the tournament were all champions of their respective regions, the School of Silent Night which represented the Eastern Kingdom champions, Ember Citadel which stood as the Southern Kingdom champions, War God Mage Academy who were the true champions of the Northern Kingdom, while Bloodline Academy were the champions personally chosen by the Northern King himself.

Of course, most people didn’t know the last few details, only the mages from the Northern Kingdom were aware of it, and none of them were eager to expose their king’s corruption.

As revealing such things would only give other kingdoms ammunition to mock them later during drunken arguments in taverns and pubs.

“Now, for the team they will be facing, let’s welcome the War God Mage Team!” the referee announced, this time pointing toward the opposite tunnel, inside the tunnel on the left side, Alec began calling out names.

“Lucas, Brandon, Endrick, and Pale. You guys will be participating in this battle with me.”

After saying that, Alec immediately stepped forward and walked out without waiting for anyone.

Lucas and Brandon didn’t seem surprised that their names had been called, almost as if they had expected it. Endrick, however, looked slightly startled, while Pale clearly hadn’t anticipated being chosen at all.

Even so, they quickly followed.

Brandon and Lucas stepped forward first, leaving the tunnel shortly after Alec, while Endrick and Pale came out just behind them.

To the audience, it looked as if Alec had entered the arena first, followed closely by Brandon and Lucas, with Endrick and Pale trailing slightly behind.

Almost immediately, many spectators turned their attention toward the customized uniforms of the War God Mage Academy, while the Fanatic Fc started cheering.

As usual, the base color was black with crimson red linings. Alec’s outfit was styled more like a kimono, decorated with the academy’s insignia and additional design elements that gave it a refined yet intimidating look.

Lucas and Brandon wore a more standard uniform design, long-sleeved tops paired with fitted combat pants, though Brandon’s outfit had buttoned cuffs along the sleeves, giving his attire a slightly different appearance.

Lucas’s long sleeves, however, were covered with intricate runic inscriptions, they were clearly newly added, and from the way they shimmered faintly, it was obvious that War God Mage Academy had invested heavily to upgrade their uniforms to Tier-5 cloth equipment.

As for Endrick and Pale, their styles were quite different from the others.

Pale’s uniform contained far more runes than the rest, and its design resembled that of a military officer’s service uniform, though it lacked the traditional white cap that usually accompanied such attire, but he had a more combatant pants instead.

Endrick’s uniform appeared more ordinary at first glance, but a closer look revealed numerous plant-type runes woven into the fabric. The embroidery carried a strange color. something between brown and gold, spreading across his uniform like subtle vines etched into the cloth.



============================================================
Chapter 2745: The Golem Mage
============================================================

Alec found himself reincarnated into a world where magic reigned supreme.

He was initially confused by the turn of events, but little did he know that this was just the beginning of a much more extraordinary journey.

As fate would have it, he soon discovered that he was the illegitimate son of one of the most influential families in the capital, the Blaze Clan but His grandfather, taking on the responsibility, cared for him until he was old enough to embark on his path as a mage.

Ironically, Alec never truly grasped the significance of his family's ancient secrets, dismissing them as trivial.

However, everything changed when he awakened his dormant system and gain abilities that made him unique.

From that moment on, he dedicated himself to becoming one of the world's most renowned mages, all while working towards fulfilling his grandfather's unfulfilled aspirations and carrying on the golem mages' legacy.



============================================================
Chapter 1046: Chapter 1046: Source Of Confusion.
============================================================

The Patriarch clearly knew his grandson carried many secrets, and as long as Alec wasn’t interested in revealing them, he was perfectly content not to pry.

“I want you to use one of those now. I’m hoping my prediction is right and it does exactly what I’m expecting,” Alec said, pointing toward the pill bottle that housed the Tier 7 mental pill.

The Patriarch immediately understood, without hesitation, he uncorked the bottle, took one pill, and swallowed it.

Right after that, he glanced at Alec as if expecting further instructions—but Alec simply watched him quietly, as though waiting for something to happen.

At first, nothing did.

That alone surprised him, he had taken the Tier 7 cultivation pills Alec gave him in the past, though far fewer than what lay before him now, and he had treasured each one, since the clan could rarely afford such resources.

Still, whenever he consumed one, he usually felt the effects almost immediately.

This time, however, there was nothing, for a moment, he wondered if something was wrong.

Then, about two seconds later, it began.

A strange heat surged inside him, there were no flames, yet it felt as though an intense force was burning through his body from within. Dark impurities started seeping out through his skin, pushed outward by that same internal pressure.

And that wasn’t all.

When the Patriarch sent his mental senses inward, he could clearly feel his mana veins expanding. Veins that had aged, narrowed, or even partially blocked over the years were slowly reopening under the influx of powerful energy.

He didn’t want to jump to conclusions, but he felt like he could already tell what was happening to him.

Because he had always believed that if those blockages fully broke open...he would be pushed straight into the Tier 8 mage realm, without any other actions needed.

The very realm he had struggled to reach for so long was finally within his grasp, just as he tried to suppress the surge, a sharp bang erupted from his body, and his head snapped backward, as he began to levitate into the air.

The sheer energy radiating from him cracked the ground beneath like a spreading spiderweb.

Alec simply watched with a faint smile, he couldn’t quite explain it, but he felt genuinely happy in that moment, he had been hoping the first effects of the mental pill would help his grandfather step into the Tier 8 realm.

After all, he still remembered how the old man used to complain about needing more Tier 7 cultivation pills in order to break through, even while already at the peak of the Tier 7 realm, which had always puzzled Alec.

He always had this biased feeling that his grandfather was extremely talented, and there was no reason he should require so much assistance to move through just one minor realm.

Eventually, Alec had concluded that it wasn’t a lack of talent at all, it was years of intense training without proper detoxification, combined with inconsistent medicinal support, he believed that it must have been what caused the blockages within his body, making advancement far more difficult.

That didn’t make him less gifted, just burdened by the accumulated strain, which was why Alec had insisted on him taking the mental pills before him.

Because beyond its enhancing mental abilities, it also seems to carry a strong purifying effect, flushing out hidden impurities on its first usage.

As Alec watched his grandfather continue levitating, he started to feel powerful aura fluctuations suddenly approach with his mental senses.

Five elder mages appeared around him almost instantly, the Grand Elder took one side, while the Third and Fourth Elders positioned themselves at others, without hesitation, they began forming rapid hand seals, channeling a foreign energy through their mana.

It was clear—they were stabilizing the environment, ensuring the breakthrough wouldn’t spiral out of control.

As the elders’ energy surged upward, it spread across the sky like a veil, clearly meant to suppress and conceal the aura pouring out from the Patriarch.

But just then, the seemingly unconscious Patriarch jerked slightly, as his mental image materialized behind him.

The towering dark golem apparition that seems to have been made from dark iron opened its eyes and turned toward the elders.

The Gordons’ elders were doing everything they could to mask the Patriarch’s breakthrough, because if he truly stepped into the Tier 8 realm, they wanted no outsider to realize it—not yet.

But it was already too late, as the mental image suddenly struck at the center of the stabilizing energy field, and like falling dominoes, the concealment collapsed instantly.

As a bright brown radiance burst outward in all directions, and even the Patriarch’s aura began to change, as the dark golem behind him also started undergoing its own transformation, its form growing heavier, more defined, almost alive.

The elders exchanged helpless glances, they all knew there was no hiding this anymore. Concealing the Patriarch alone had been difficult enough, but hiding a colossal dark-iron golem mental image of that scale was simply impossible, they had tried this formation on Tier 7 mages and it worked but on a Tier 8 breakthrough they realised how hard they were reaching for considering their low realm in comparison.

_ _

From a distance, Terran stared at the dark golem figure with closed eyes as its body shifted and evolved. He slowly shook his head.

He knew exactly what this phenomenon meant.

Someone was breaking into the Tier 8 realm.

And since it was happening within the Gordons Clan’s territory, there was little doubt about who it was.

Still, he couldn’t wrap his head around it.

He hadn’t seen any mid-rank clan this abnormal before. In fact, he didn’t even believe he could still call them a mid-rank clan anymore.

The mere fact that they now possessed a Tier 8 mage already qualified them as a weak high-rank clan, and that was without even factoring in the existence of their Tier 7 mages.

What unsettled Terran even more was the Gordons’ apparent ability to conjure pills and armor as if from thin air. It was precisely because of this that his suspicions kept growing. All this while, the spies they had planted throughout the city had been completely unable to uncover the true origin of the pills and spirit equipment that were auctioned off, as if their source had been deliberately erased from the world itself.

Clearly, they were bound to fail from the start, as the person responsible for their troubles was far away from their reach, the true reason behind their confusion was so unbelievable that even Terran had never once suspected Alec to be the one responsible for creating them.



============================================================
Chapter 1047: Chapter 1047: Double Aura.
============================================================

This extraordinary phenomenon involving the Gordons Patriarch persisted for some time, and every high-rank and mid-rank mage in the Estonia city could sense it, they could all tell that he was breaking through into the Tier 8 realm.

The enormous Mental Image Manifestation continued to radiate brilliant light, and the wild mana storm raging around its evolution made any attempt at concealment impossible.

For many of the city’s mages, the sight brought a rare sense of reassurance to them that now, a new Tier 8 mage lived among them.

_ _

After roughly thirty more minutes, the Patriarch slowly descended, his breakthrough complete, and his radiant Mental Image gradually dimming until it faded entirely from sight.

Across every household in Estonia City, conversations buzzed about the Gordons clan, they knew that with the city hall forces now destroyed and with the Gordons Patriarch having stepped into Tier 8, it was clear to all, that the clan would rule unchallenged for now, at least until the capital finally sent investigators to probe the City Lord’s death.

The moment the Patriarch touched the ground, all the clan elders were already waiting for him around, even Terran had come to watch him finish his breakthrough.

Immediately they began to offer their congratulations to George, while Alec watched from a short distance, observing his grandfather as the elders swarmed him with respectful greetings and bows.

Even from where he stood, Alec could see the change, his grandfather looked noticeably younger. Many strands of hair had turned jet black once more, and the deep wrinkles that time had carved into his face had smoothed away almost entirely.

The breakthrough had clearly extended his lifespan further, and the physical rejuvenation was the most visible proof.

“Beautiful, isn’t it?” Terran’s voice came from beside him as the older man stepped up to stand level with Alec.

Alec turned slightly.

“I was planning to find you after this,” Alec said,

“But since you’re here...Take this. It should be more than enough to handle your problems.” Alec continued by summoning a small pill bottle from his spatial ring and sent it floating toward Terran.

Terran raised his right hand toward his left as the pill bottle floated into his palm, it took only a split second for his casual expression to shatter, after his mental senses had gone through the contents of the bottle.

“I... I...” Terran stammered, with his eyes fixed on the bottle, no matter how long he stared, words refused to form from him.

“I remember you saying you’d need around a hundred Tier 7 mental pills to fully recover from your injuries, there are only about forty in there, but I have reason to believe they’ll get the job done.” Alec said calmly.

“They’re clearly the highest quality you’ll find anywhere in the Northern Kingdom,” he added, turning to face his master directly.

Terran finally found his voice, though it trembled.

“I don’t understand why your clan is helping me, with this many Tier 7 mental pills at your disposal, you could have raised far more high-rank mages within the Gordons clan. Yet instead...”

Alec cut in gently.

“Master, you should never try to understand the ways of my clan, and this isn’t a gesture from the clan—if that were your thoughts skewered towards then you’re greatly mistaken.”

“This is just me helping you reach perfect health, because the stronger you become, the more freely I can act until I leave the academy. And please, don’t mention paying me back again, because we both know that even if you sold everything you own, it wouldn’t come close to paying for half of those pills.”

Alec dropped those words with a faint smile on his face.

Terran listened in silence, the only part he clung to was Alec’s warning not to try understanding the Gordons clan, that single sentence felt like permission to stop questioning.

“Then I won’t act like a stranger,” Terran said quietly.

As he immediately sat cross-legged on the ground, uncorked the bottle, and poured all forty pills into his mouth at once.

At that moment, Terran let go of all shame, he simply accepted his fate.

Deep inside, he felt overwhelmingly indebted to Alec, and still believed that somewhere deep hidden with-in the Gordons clan, someone was calling the shots, and because of Alec, he had been shown this unprecedented favor.

In fact, Terran didn’t even attempt to enter a safer meditative state first before taking the pills, nor did he pause for a moment to consider whether the pills Alec had given him might cause irreversible repercussions later.

Alec, standing off to the side, was equally stunned to see his master swallow all forty pills at once.

And of course the effects were immediate, and far more explosive than when the Gordons Patriarch had taken just one, as a violent surge of energy erupted from Terran’s body, and almost instantly his Mental Image began to manifest.

A massive mountain-like visage took shape before him, radiating an aura of immovable strength and overwhelming gravitational force.

The moment it appeared, Alec was forced to stagger backward, because just by standing nearby, the gravity around him intensified to a level he could no longer manipulate or ease.

He retreated several steps, eyes widening.

Behind his master, the cracked mountain began to knit itself back together at a visible pace, this time the restoration wasn’t limited to one section, it was happening from every direction at once, stone fusing seamlessly as though guided by an invisible hand.

The other Gordons elders watching nearby exchanged glances, they could all sense it, that once the mountain fully healed, there was a very real chance Terran would break through to the Tier 8 realm himself.

Just as they began to wonder if there was anything they could do, or if they could contain the situation, as they reminisced on how they had failed to suppress even the Patriarch’s earlier outburst, but then in that moment the Patriarch’s calm voice cut through their tension.

“What are you all standing there looking lost for? Set up the barrier, with me here, we should be able to conceal most of the breakthrough aura.”

The elders immediately snapped into motion, swiftly taking positions around Terran, as the Patriarch stepped forward to serve as the formation’s core.

___

Meanwhile, across Estonia City, countless clans had turned their attention back toward the Gordons’ estate once more.

For a brief while they had believed things were settling after the Patriarch’s evolution concluded, but now it was obvious that had only been the beginning, mere minutes after the Patriarch’s aura had stabilized, a second, entirely different aura and pressure began to build momentum, rising steadily from the same location.

“My son, from this day forward, the Gordons clan must never be offended, no matter what happens. Go and spread this order to every enforcer. Anyone who dares disobey will be punished severely.”

The Patriarch of a certain clan spoke these words to his son the moment he sensed the rising energy being swiftly concealed.

At that instant, not only he but the heads of various other clans were issuing the same stern warning to their own members: do not provoke the Gordons, as they all believed the Gordons clan was clearly on the rise.

These were the very same people who, in the past, hadn’t hesitated to talk bad about the Gordons clan when they believed they were already down and would soon be destroyed after being attacked by the city lord.



============================================================
Chapter 1048: Chapter 1048: Blades.
============================================================

Immediately the judge called for the start of the battle, the mages of the School of Silent Night wasted no time, as they surged forward, bearing down on the War God Mage Academy team like a dark tide.

Shin Jae-Hyun and Kenta were the first to move, Kenta gripped his tonfa, its usual blunt striking ends were replaced with razor-sharp blades that gave the weapon a vicious, gleaming edge.

Beside him, Shin dashed forward, with a kunai clenched in his right hand and a coiled wired hook wrapped around his left forearm.

“That one’s mine,” Pale declared, charging forward away from the whole team and straight for Kenta.

Behind him, Lucas silently raised a hand and drew his greatsword, though he said nothing; however, his stance and the place he was facing made it clear that he had chosen Shin as his target.

The moment the two sides were about to collide, the crowd erupted into wild cheers, anticipation electric in the air.

The School of Silent Night liked to fight as a unit, and they rarely engaged alone unless forced to, and it was clear to them now that is what the war god team was trying to accomplish.

From Shin’s left hand, two smoke bombs materialized, he hurled them directly at Lucas.

Agnes had already warned them about the smoke’s effects and the importance of avoiding it at all costs, recognizing the threat, Lucas shifted the angle of his blade and swung in a wide arc, swatting the bombs away as if batting a ball.

He had intended to redirect them back toward Shin, but things didn’t go as planned, as the flat of his sword struck the small orbs, they detonated instantly.

There was no shrapnel or explosive force, no visible injury—but thick, choking smoke billowed outward in an instant, completely engulfing Lucas.

The dark mist pressed down on him, heavily suppressing his mental powers and clouding his senses while he remained within its range.

On the other side, Pale had finally closed the distance with Kenta, who thrust his left hand forward in what looked like a simple jab, but the bladed edge of Kenta’s tonfa came with it, slashing straight for Pale’s throat.

Pale twisted at the last instant, letting the blade graze past, and slicing a thin line across his neck but missing anything vital.

As Kenta’s arm extended past him, Pale snapped his right hand up and seized Kenta’s wrist in a vice grip.

[> Low Tier Spell – Chill Touch <]

Pale cast the spell instantly, as his right hand turned ghostly white, frost spreading rapidly up Kenta’s sleeve and into his clothes, cold bit deep, numbing his muscle and slowing his blood flow.

Kenta immediately reacted without hesitation, as he used his free right hand by snapping upward in a brutal uppercut aimed at Pale’s chin.

Pale swiftly released the grip when he saw that move and shoved off the ground just in time, leaping backward, causing the uppercut to miss by inches.

Though Kenta had lost the initial exchange, but he wasn’t about to let the small advantage he had gotten slip away, as he pressed forward aggressively.

A palm-sized patch of ice had already formed on Kenta’s left hand. Beneath his feet, frost crept outward across the arena floor, not fully spread yet, but spreading fast, and Pale was the source of it.

Pale also now wields two short ice blades in reverse grip, their edges glinting with dangerous light as if they were sharper than even steel, his stance was low and coiled, ready to meet the next charge.

Across the field away from them, Lucas burst out of the dissipating smoke, as Beatrice immediately retreated several steps back, dragging her massive coffin before her with her left hand, while also keeping it positioned as a shield.

On the opposite flank, Endrick and Katie moved without hesitation, targeting Liang Xuefeng and Ayame Ren respectively.

Lucas swung his greatsword in wide, heavy arcs, lightning crackled along the blade in thin, electric veins, each strike carrying terrifying momentum.

Shin met every blow with only his small wind kunai, deflecting the massive swings with precise, almost impossible parries.

Every block forced Shin backward a step or two, the sheer power behind Lucas’s attacks was no joke, yet Shin endured with the pain in his wrist, and then his left wrist finally twitched.

As the wired hook shot out like a striking snake, aimed straight for Lucas’s right leg.

The wired hook coiled tightly around Lucas’s leg before he could bring his greatsword down to sever it, Shin yanked hard, dragging Lucas off balance and forcing his legs apart.

Lucas crashed to the ground, knees spread wide, with Shin’s first objective complete, he closed the distance in an instant, thrusting his wind kunai straight toward Lucas’s throat.

Lucas raised his left hand at the last possible moment, allowing the kunai to pierce clean through his palm, to the other side.

“Hngh!” Pain flared through his hand, but Lucas gritted his teeth, as he knew Shin’s speed outmatched his own, he felt this was his only chance to trap him, without hesitation, Lucas clamped his fingers down around Shin’s wrist, locking him in place.

Shin twisted, trying to pull free, but the grip was iron tight, for the first time, Shin was forced to look directly into Lucas’s face.

And all he could see was a wild, feral grin spread across it, if Alec had seen that expression, he would have recognized it instantly, and smiled in approval.

This was berserk-mode Lucas, the one who lived for mad, unrestrained battle.

“My turn.”

Lucas said as surged upward, his right hand snapping around Shin’s neck, as he lifted him high, then slammed him down hard onto the black stone arena floor.

Shin’s mouth flew open as the impact drove the air from his lungs in a violent rush, but Lucas wasn’t finished, as he raised his legs and stomped downward—once, twice, again, each strike hammering into Shin’s chest with brutal force.

Lightning energy crackled along Lucas’s boots, every stomp sent electric arcs spreading through Shin’s body, numbing his muscle and nerve alike.

At first, the numbness was bearable, and Shin could still resist it, But the strikes kept coming, overlapping, stacking.

And each new jolt deepened the paralysis until his limbs refused to obey.

Shin tried to lift a hand, to block, to fight back, but he got nothing, no response from his hands, his arms just lay limp at his sides, heavy and useless, while Lucas loomed above him, eyes burning with savage delight.

Seeing Shin’s limp, numbed form, Lucas grabbed a fistful of his hair and swung with brutal force, as Shin’s body arced through the air before crashing out of bounds, tumbling across the arena floor outside the marked lines.

The entire crowd fell silent.

The War God Mage Academy team which everyone had already written off, and believed would suffer a crushing defeat had now drawn first blood,

Lucas’s decisive throw acted like a spark, and as if a fierce fire ignited in his teammates. Pale, Endrick, and Katie, surged forward with renewed savagery, clashing against their opponents with ferocious intensity.



============================================================
Chapter 1049: Chapter 1049: Mirage Ninja.
============================================================

The defeat of Shin hit the School of Silent Night mages like a shockwave, as none of them had imagined him losing and definitely not so soon at the beginning of the battle.

But the thoughts of his defeat only lingered for a moment before they shoved it aside, refocusing on their own fights, as a fierce determination rose in them, many of them starting to feel as if they needed to take down at least one War God member to even the score.

Elsewhere, Kenta had finally closed in on Pale, as he whipped his right hand forward; the tonfa twisted mid-swing, its bladed end slicing toward Pale’s throat like a hidden dagger.

Pale swiftly raised his left short ice blade just in time to parry, as metal rang against frost, but Kenta’s left fist was already coming, a vicious jab aimed at Pale’s face.

Pale twisted his second short sword up to deflect it at the last second, which caused the trajectory of the blade to shift, but the blade still grazed his cheek, drawing a thin line of blood.

While the rest of Kenta’s fist slammed into his nose with a dull crunch, this caused Pale to stagger back, as he could feel his vision blurring.

Kenta pressed the advantage instantly with a lightning-fast triple kick: one to the knee, waist, and head, with each impact sending Pale reeling sideways, overwhelmed by the relentless martial onslaught from Kenta.

Blood now trickled from the corner of Pale’s mouth, and from the edge of his vision, he saw Lucas standing victorious over Shin, and already scanning the field for his next target.

Pale’s pride flared, he was a mage who craved the spotlight; so he didn’t want to be the one who needed rescue , as he believed that would only serve to ruin his reputation.

[> Mid Tier Spell – Cold Zone <]

Pale planted his feet wide, dropping into a solid stance, and the frost that had only faintly trailed his footsteps now exploded outward, spreading rapidly in a twenty-meter circle around him—an icy domain of slick ground, biting cold, and slowed movement for anyone but himself.

But he wasn’t finished. Pale’s fingers flashed through another series of hand signs as ice runes flickered in mid-air around him, when the last seal locked in, he thrust both palms forward toward Kenta, who was already closing the distance in a hard sprint.

[> Mid Tier Spell – Glacier Spike <]

Immediately a massive ice spike erupted from the edge of the twenty-meter frozen domain, lancing upward and outward in a deadly arc aimed straight at Kenta who was charging forward.

The spike rose like a spear thrown by a giant, climbing toward the sky. Kenta’s eyes narrowed, he knew he couldn’t block it head-on.

Instead, he waited until the spike was almost upon him, then leaped high, planting his left palm on its surface to vault over and continue his charge.

But if he thought of that, even Pale, who was watching him closely, could already predict his next move before he made it.

[> Low Tier Spell – Ice Explosion <]

Pale clenched his right fist, as the glacier spike mirrored the motion and detonated in a wide, violent burst of razor-sharp shards and freezing mist, filling the air with a lethal cloud.

[> Mid Shinobi Technique – Mirror Phantasm <]

Kenta’s deep voice rang out from within the explosion.

Causing Pale to spread out his mental senses towards the location around him, inside his mental scope, he suddenly detected a second figure materializing from a completve opposite direction, identical aura, identical presence to Kenta.

Pale couldn’t tell how Kenta had managed to cross the distance to the other side without him knowing as it would require an almost impossible speed, but he wasn’t taking chances.

Instantly Pale raised his right hand as if lifting invisible weights, as ten ice lances ripped free from the frozen ground around him, hovering like spears ready to fly.

“Fire!”

He then thrust his hand forward, the lances shot toward the approaching Kenta in a deadly volley, then surprise flashed across Pale’s face.

As from the opposite direction, the side he had turned his back to, he could feel another identical Kenta aura finally appearing in his mental scope, and it was closing fast.

Without turning, Pale could tell he’d been deceived, that was because the ice lances had finally struck the figure he’d targeted and all he got was flickering as the ice lances passed straight through it.

“An illusion.” Pale muttered.

From the opposite side, the real Kenta burst out of the lingering explosion fog,Pale managed only a half-turn before Kenta was on him.

Frozen patches mottled Kenta’s skin; blood streaked from cuts and grazes, his disheveled state confirmed this was the genuine body, but Pale had no time to react.

As Kenta’s leg swept low, hooking Pale’s ankle and robbing him of his balance, following that was a rising kick almost instantly, slamming into Pale’s chin and snapping his head backward.

The tonfa were gone from Kenta’s hands, in their place he gripped a wired hook with both fists.

In one fluid motion, Kenta spun behind Pale—back to back, and looped the wire around his neck, as he yanked it tight.

Pale choked, his hands flying up to seize the wire before it could bite deeper into his neck, blood welled instantly as the thin metal cut into his palms.

Kenta then leaned back, using his full weight, as Pale could his feet leaving the ground., before he could mount any real resistance, he was been hurled over the arena’s edge, and crashed outside the boundary.

“Good riddance,” Kenta muttered, waving dismissively as Pale tumbled, he knew he had survived only because of his illusion arts.

Had Pale not been fooled by the phantom body, he would still have been facing elimination now, because after that ice explosion, Pale would have had his ice lances poised and ready.

Even Kenta knew he wouldn’t have survived that barrage.

So when the fog had risen, he had shattered his own smoke bomb, in a bid to suppress Pale’s mental senses just long enough to cast the Mirror Phantasm, which was designed to perfectly mimic his movements, and since Pale couldn’t locate the real body.

He had mistaken the illusion for the genuine one, and that was how the mirage he had up had flipped his disadvantage into a decisive advantage.



============================================================
Chapter 1050: Chapter 1050: Blades 2.
============================================================

“We’re finally back to square one with the War God Mage Academy te—” Kenta was still speaking when he turned toward the battle ring all he met with was the sudden appearance of Lucas Fist heading for his face.

Immediately, in a fast fashion he formed a hand seal to cast a spell, while pushing off the ground and moving backwards to gain ground.

[> Mid Shinobi Skill – Fading Afterimage. <]

The moment he activated this spell, multiple versions of him appeared, all flickering like unstable reflections which made it hard to figure out which was his main body as there was nothing that stood out, since all the bodies he created were all glowing in weird glows.

At once, all these versions of Kenta all swung their wired hooks toward Lucas.

“Hmmm,” Lucas muttered in surprise as he realized the hooks had actually coiled around his outstretched right fist, from Agnes’ report, this mage supposedly uses mirage magic, meaning most of his attacks were usually more illusory than real.

That was why Lucas hadn’t reacted much when there was more than one of Kenta before him, but now he couldn’t help wondering whether he was trapped in an illusion or facing reality due to the pain he felt when the wired hook coiled around his hands, he just felt he had a very high mental will and these should have not gotten to him.

Still, he chose to press forward despite the pain, trusting only in his own strength, he stretched out his left hand behind him and unhooked the long staff strapped there.

At the same time, he tightened and twisted his right hand around the hooked wires, as the pain that shot through him increased more than double fold, it was sharp enough to make him feel like he might just lose his hand, but he didn’t care.

Instead, he pulled harder, using his superior strength to drag the mirage versions of Kenta toward him.

While this was happening, his greatsword, which had been lying on the ground nearby, levitated and rotated midair toward the outstretched staff he had outward, and the blade handle slid perfectly into the top shaft, as his Qi coated the weapon almost immediately this happened, triggering what looked like a reforging process.

Its shape shifted, locking firmly into place until a glaive formed in his grasp.

“Horizontal Sweep,” Lucas said as he swung the blade toward the incoming mirages before him.

The Qi-coated glaive which was crackling faintly with sparks of lightning, already emitted a thunderous roar before it even connected, this caused Kenta who had been hiding among the mirage bodies, to finally give up his concealment as he was scared of letting that glaive hit him in consideration of the power of wild Qi he could feel even before the glaive arrived,

In a rush he released his hold over the wired hook, while retreating backward.

“Got you!” Lucas said as the pain he was feeling in his hands suddenly vanished the moment the real Kenta moved away, that didn’t stop him though as he made sure to complete his swing, causing the mirage versions of Kenta before him to instantly disappear.

With a smooth motion, he shifted the glaive from his left hand to his right, still maintaining a firm two-handed grip.

In a lightning-fast move, he thrust forward as if piercing, though Kenta saw the attack coming, but he was barely able to react before he realized the glaive was already in front of him, it had traveled over at a speed even he was unused to.

But he still twisted his body in an unconventional way, doing everything possible to avoid a fatal injury, as a loud bang sounded out even without hitting anything, the lightning coursing around Lucas not only amplified his attack power and added a numbing effect to his strikes, but it also seemed to significantly boost his attacking speed.

At this moment, even Kenta was clearly at the fact that he was at another disadvantage again, with his weapon being much shorter, and Lucas’ previous strike had already forced him back while perfectly setting up a follow-up attack motion, he started to think wide trying to find a loop for himself from his current situation.

But Lucas just continued with his attack motion as he sharply withdrew the glaive, twirling it once in his right hand before raising it high overhead.

Then he brought it down in a heavy chop, in a diagonal strike from right to left, aiming for the lower rib region; the air above the weapon churned violently as the blade descended.

Bang!

The glaive finally landed, though Kenta hastily cast another mirage spell all in a bid to reinforce his defense, and he also tried to outpace the strike by moving toward Lucas, hoping to slip past the descending blow by bending.

But he had overestimated himself, because the glaive speed increased again and the moment the glaive connected, every layer of defense he was so proud of shattered instantly.

He instantly felt his insides twist painfully as he threw his hands up in a desperate guard, his tonfa baton acting as the last line of defense, even then, his protective coatings cracked apart on impact.

It didn’t help — internal injuries, check; overwhelming Qi energy, check; a deafening shockwave, check.

Kenta watched helplessly as his body was flung through the air from sheer strength, the crowd screamed when they saw him hurtling toward the stands, but just before he could crash into the spectators, his body suddenly halted.

A transparent light shield flickered into existence where he would have landed, and he slid down it at considerable speed, before he even hit the ground, he had lost consciousness.

“Lucas!!!”

At that moment, the fanatic supporters of the War God Mage Academy finally found their voices again as they cheered Lucas name, though they still didn’t feel like victory was guaranteed, especially with Alec, their Golem Prince, absent from the arena, but they began to feel far better than at the start of the battle, when everything had seemed bleak.

_ _

Not far away, the battle between Katie and Ayame finally concluded, with Katie emerging victorious.

Initially, Ayame’s fan-blade shinobi arts gave her the upper hand, allowing her to dominate Katie for a while, with her weapon and wind manipulation.

However, Katie endured every hit and waited patiently, and once she sensed Ayame’s mana was running low, she summoned her spirit equipment set.

With it, she transformed into a more empowered version of herself, the set had clearly been crafted with her unique abilities in mind, serving to amplify her strengths.

Although it wasn’t forged by one of the elite masters Alec’s system could supposedly summon and conjure using his system points, whoever created Katie’s gear was undeniably a top-tier forger when it came to physical aspect sets.

With the boost from the equipment, Katie seized the advantage, seamlessly switching between her Super strength and Super speed abilities whenever it suited her.

Her fists, coated in flaming aura, eventually overpowered Ayame in a brutal barrage of punches.

On Endrick’s side, things hadn’t gone as smoothly as hoped, both he and Liang ended up taking each other out of the fight.

Well, in fact, many of the top mages watching from their VIP booths hadn’t expected the outcome to turn out this way, because from the start Liang’s ice elemental control made him, in some sense, a natural counter to Endrick’s element.



============================================================
Chapter 1051: Chapter 1051: Blades 3.
============================================================

With every spell Endrick tried to cast, Liang made sure to freeze it over, not only slowing the growth of the plants and sometimes, with enough ice power, he was able to put them into a full hibernation state which rendered them useless before the spell could fully materialize.

This gradually turned the battle into more of a hand-to-hand confrontation, which seems to be exactly what Liang had been aiming for, in some ways, the mages of Silent Night seemed very confident in close combat, but against Endrick it didn’t go quite as Liang expected.

After cutting off his ability to freely grow plants, Endrick simply switched tactics, as he drew his spirit weapon in its crossbow form and began firing bolts at Liang while steadily increasing the distance between them.

For a mage like Liang, though that wasn’t much fun, but ninjas of Silent Night never complained about ranged skirmishes.

As he too pulled out several shurikens and hurled them toward the incoming bolts, altering their direction by deflecting them just enough for him to close the gap during such period.

By the time he reached striking distance, Liang swung his daggers in a flurry, aiming to pierce Endrick’s guard.

However it seems Endrick was ready for him as he had already shifted his weapon into its bow form, with its edges lined with razor-sharp blades, and using the bow like a makeshift staff, he blocked the daggers with his left hand while countering occasionally with a short blade in his right.

It was a tense deadlock, parrying a proficient ninja’s relentless strikes with one hand while keeping the other free for opportunistic jabs.

After two minutes of this stalemate, realizing they were evenly matched, Liang decided to go all out, as he shoved back hard during one clash, creating space, and began channeling his ice mana and his Qi energy for a decisive final move to end Endrick.

But as he gathered power, Endrick did the same, funneling his mana into one last plant spell, one he believed potent enough to overwhelm any ice that might try to halt it, but neither of them had considered that clashing at such close range, without either side holding a clear advantage, was a terrible idea.

When they finally unleashed their attacks, one side of the battle ring was dyed green while the other turned icy white.

Neither managed to overpower the other, the colliding energies eventually exploded outward, blasting both mages off the ring.

Now only Takeda remained on the School of Silent Night’s side, while the War God Mage Team still had Katie, Lucas, and Beatrice who had still not made a single move since the battle started.

Even though only one opponent was left, the War God team didn’t relax.

If anything, they became quieter, more focused, fully aware of what they were about to face.

Though they hadn’t been present when Takeda first revealed what made many mages currently view him as the most terrifying mage present in the tournament, but Agnes had provided them with detailed reports of his battle style, and the Dean had even obtained a costly crystal recording of Takeda’s last battle.

In fact, he had bragged a lot about how much it had cost him to obtain it, so they had to make sure they didn’t just go out there and lose woefully after all that effort.

After studying it, they understood just how dangerous Takeda truly was, that was why they weren’t offended by the crowd rooting against them, because even them knew exactly what they were up against.

“Let’s get this over with, all of you just come at me,” the clone body Takeda said, curling his fingers in a casual beckoning gesture, which provoked Katie as she was aware that he is just a clone.

Lucas on the other hand didn’t waste time, as his body twitched once, and in the next instant he was already in front of Takeda, glaive thrust forward in a sharp, one-handed strike.

He thrust it low, as if trying to shove Takeda off the battle stage, but Takeda simply raised his left hand in a high guard to block.

Clang!

The sound of the glaive blade scraping across the surface of his hand guard rang out, yet he kept his composure, closing the distance, he shot out his right arm instead, driving his elbow toward Lucas’ chin.

But Lucas didn’t budge, as there was already Qi energy lingered there as if he had anticipated the strike.

At such close range, Lucas released his glaive, grabbed Takeda’s head, and slammed his forehead into his, which caused Takeda to stagger backward before Lucas seized his collar, twisted, and then released him, by flinging him toward the stands.

“Ouuuush!”

The crowd of mages waiting in the stands gasped, surprised at how easily Lucas seemed to have handled Takeda.

However, before Takeda’s body could crash into the invisible barrier shielding the spectators, something unexpected happened.

A wind door appeared in the middle of the arena and swung open, and unlike the last time, when the other Takeda bodies had taken their time emerging, a long chain-like weapon shot out immediately toward the clone representing Takeda.

It coiled around him and dragged him back.
