He then thrust his hand forward, the lances shot toward the approaching Kenta in a deadly volley, then surprise flashed across Pale’s face.

As from the opposite direction, the side he had turned his back to, he could feel another identical Kenta aura finally appearing in his mental scope, and it was closing fast.

Without turning, Pale could tell he’d been deceived, that was because the ice lances had finally struck the figure he’d targeted and all he got was flickering as the ice lances passed straight through it.

“An illusion.” Pale muttered.

From the opposite side, the real Kenta burst out of the lingering explosion fog,Pale managed only a half-turn before Kenta was on him.

Frozen patches mottled Kenta’s skin; blood streaked from cuts and grazes, his disheveled state confirmed this was the genuine body, but Pale had no time to react.

As Kenta’s leg swept low, hooking Pale’s ankle and robbing him of his balance, following that was a rising kick almost instantly, slamming into Pale’s chin and snapping his head backward.

The tonfa were gone from Kenta’s hands, in their place he gripped a wired hook with both fists.

In one fluid motion, Kenta spun behind Pale—back to back, and looped the wire around his neck, as he yanked it tight.

Pale choked, his hands flying up to seize the wire before it could bite deeper into his neck, blood welled instantly as the thin metal cut into his palms.

Kenta then leaned back, using his full weight, as Pale could his feet leaving the ground., before he could mount any real resistance, he was been hurled over the arena’s edge, and crashed outside the boundary.

“Good riddance,” Kenta muttered, waving dismissively as Pale tumbled, he knew he had survived only because of his illusion arts.

Had Pale not been fooled by the phantom body, he would still have been facing elimination now, because after that ice explosion, Pale would have had his ice lances poised and ready.

Even Kenta knew he wouldn’t have survived that barrage.

So when the fog had risen, he had shattered his own smoke bomb, in a bid to suppress Pale’s mental senses just long enough to cast the Mirror Phantasm, which was designed to perfectly mimic his movements, and since Pale couldn’t locate the real body.

He had mistaken the illusion for the genuine one, and that was how the mirage he had up had flipped his disadvantage into a decisive advantage.



============================================================
Chapter 1050: Chapter 1050: Blades 2.
============================================================

“We’re finally back to square one with the War God Mage Academy te—” Kenta was still speaking when he turned toward the battle ring all he met with was the sudden appearance of Lucas Fist heading for his face.

Immediately, in a fast fashion he formed a hand seal to cast a spell, while pushing off the ground and moving backwards to gain ground.

[> Mid Shinobi Skill – Fading Afterimage. <]

The moment he activated this spell, multiple versions of him appeared, all flickering like unstable reflections which made it hard to figure out which was his main body as there was nothing that stood out, since all the bodies he created were all glowing in weird glows.

At once, all these versions of Kenta all swung their wired hooks toward Lucas.

“Hmmm,” Lucas muttered in surprise as he realized the hooks had actually coiled around his outstretched right fist, from Agnes’ report, this mage supposedly uses mirage magic, meaning most of his attacks were usually more illusory than real.

That was why Lucas hadn’t reacted much when there was more than one of Kenta before him, but now he couldn’t help wondering whether he was trapped in an illusion or facing reality due to the pain he felt when the wired hook coiled around his hands, he just felt he had a very high mental will and these should have not gotten to him.

Still, he chose to press forward despite the pain, trusting only in his own strength, he stretched out his left hand behind him and unhooked the long staff strapped there.

At the same time, he tightened and twisted his right hand around the hooked wires, as the pain that shot through him increased more than double fold, it was sharp enough to make him feel like he might just lose his hand, but he didn’t care.

Instead, he pulled harder, using his superior strength to drag the mirage versions of Kenta toward him.

While this was happening, his greatsword, which had been lying on the ground nearby, levitated and rotated midair toward the outstretched staff he had outward, and the blade handle slid perfectly into the top shaft, as his Qi coated the weapon almost immediately this happened, triggering what looked like a reforging process.

Its shape shifted, locking firmly into place until a glaive formed in his grasp.

“Horizontal Sweep,” Lucas said as he swung the blade toward the incoming mirages before him.

The Qi-coated glaive which was crackling faintly with sparks of lightning, already emitted a thunderous roar before it even connected, this caused Kenta who had been hiding among the mirage bodies, to finally give up his concealment as he was scared of letting that glaive hit him in consideration of the power of wild Qi he could feel even before the glaive arrived,

In a rush he released his hold over the wired hook, while retreating backward.

“Got you!” Lucas said as the pain he was feeling in his hands suddenly vanished the moment the real Kenta moved away, that didn’t stop him though as he made sure to complete his swing, causing the mirage versions of Kenta before him to instantly disappear.

With a smooth motion, he shifted the glaive from his left hand to his right, still maintaining a firm two-handed grip.

In a lightning-fast move, he thrust forward as if piercing, though Kenta saw the attack coming, but he was barely able to react before he realized the glaive was already in front of him, it had traveled over at a speed even he was unused to.

But he still twisted his body in an unconventional way, doing everything possible to avoid a fatal injury, as a loud bang sounded out even without hitting anything, the lightning coursing around Lucas not only amplified his attack power and added a numbing effect to his strikes, but it also seemed to significantly boost his attacking speed.

At this moment, even Kenta was clearly at the fact that he was at another disadvantage again, with his weapon being much shorter, and Lucas’ previous strike had already forced him back while perfectly setting up a follow-up attack motion, he started to think wide trying to find a loop for himself from his current situation.

But Lucas just continued with his attack motion as he sharply withdrew the glaive, twirling it once in his right hand before raising it high overhead.

Then he brought it down in a heavy chop, in a diagonal strike from right to left, aiming for the lower rib region; the air above the weapon churned violently as the blade descended.

Bang!

The glaive finally landed, though Kenta hastily cast another mirage spell all in a bid to reinforce his defense, and he also tried to outpace the strike by moving toward Lucas, hoping to slip past the descending blow by bending.

But he had overestimated himself, because the glaive speed increased again and the moment the glaive connected, every layer of defense he was so proud of shattered instantly.

He instantly felt his insides twist painfully as he threw his hands up in a desperate guard, his tonfa baton acting as the last line of defense, even then, his protective coatings cracked apart on impact.

It didn’t help — internal injuries, check; overwhelming Qi energy, check; a deafening shockwave, check.

Kenta watched helplessly as his body was flung through the air from sheer strength, the crowd screamed when they saw him hurtling toward the stands, but just before he could crash into the spectators, his body suddenly halted.

A transparent light shield flickered into existence where he would have landed, and he slid down it at considerable speed, before he even hit the ground, he had lost consciousness.

“Lucas!!!”

At that moment, the fanatic supporters of the War God Mage Academy finally found their voices again as they cheered Lucas name, though they still didn’t feel like victory was guaranteed, especially with Alec, their Golem Prince, absent from the arena, but they began to feel far better than at the start of the battle, when everything had seemed bleak.

_ _

Not far away, the battle between Katie and Ayame finally concluded, with Katie emerging victorious.

Initially, Ayame’s fan-blade shinobi arts gave her the upper hand, allowing her to dominate Katie for a while, with her weapon and wind manipulation.

However, Katie endured every hit and waited patiently, and once she sensed Ayame’s mana was running low, she summoned her spirit equipment set.

With it, she transformed into a more empowered version of herself, the set had clearly been crafted with her unique abilities in mind, serving to amplify her strengths.

Although it wasn’t forged by one of the elite masters Alec’s system could supposedly summon and conjure using his system points, whoever created Katie’s gear was undeniably a top-tier forger when it came to physical aspect sets.

With the boost from the equipment, Katie seized the advantage, seamlessly switching between her Super strength and Super speed abilities whenever it suited her.

Her fists, coated in flaming aura, eventually overpowered Ayame in a brutal barrage of punches.

On Endrick’s side, things hadn’t gone as smoothly as hoped, both he and Liang ended up taking each other out of the fight.

Well, in fact, many of the top mages watching from their VIP booths hadn’t expected the outcome to turn out this way, because from the start Liang’s ice elemental control made him, in some sense, a natural counter to Endrick’s element.



============================================================
Chapter 1051: Chapter 1051: Blades 3.
============================================================

With every spell Endrick tried to cast, Liang made sure to freeze it over, not only slowing the growth of the plants and sometimes, with enough ice power, he was able to put them into a full hibernation state which rendered them useless before the spell could fully materialize.

This gradually turned the battle into more of a hand-to-hand confrontation, which seems to be exactly what Liang had been aiming for, in some ways, the mages of Silent Night seemed very confident in close combat, but against Endrick it didn’t go quite as Liang expected.

After cutting off his ability to freely grow plants, Endrick simply switched tactics, as he drew his spirit weapon in its crossbow form and began firing bolts at Liang while steadily increasing the distance between them.

For a mage like Liang, though that wasn’t much fun, but ninjas of Silent Night never complained about ranged skirmishes.

As he too pulled out several shurikens and hurled them toward the incoming bolts, altering their direction by deflecting them just enough for him to close the gap during such period.

By the time he reached striking distance, Liang swung his daggers in a flurry, aiming to pierce Endrick’s guard.

However it seems Endrick was ready for him as he had already shifted his weapon into its bow form, with its edges lined with razor-sharp blades, and using the bow like a makeshift staff, he blocked the daggers with his left hand while countering occasionally with a short blade in his right.

It was a tense deadlock, parrying a proficient ninja’s relentless strikes with one hand while keeping the other free for opportunistic jabs.

After two minutes of this stalemate, realizing they were evenly matched, Liang decided to go all out, as he shoved back hard during one clash, creating space, and began channeling his ice mana and his Qi energy for a decisive final move to end Endrick.

But as he gathered power, Endrick did the same, funneling his mana into one last plant spell, one he believed potent enough to overwhelm any ice that might try to halt it, but neither of them had considered that clashing at such close range, without either side holding a clear advantage, was a terrible idea.

When they finally unleashed their attacks, one side of the battle ring was dyed green while the other turned icy white.

Neither managed to overpower the other, the colliding energies eventually exploded outward, blasting both mages off the ring.

Now only Takeda remained on the School of Silent Night’s side, while the War God Mage Team still had Katie, Lucas, and Beatrice who had still not made a single move since the battle started.

Even though only one opponent was left, the War God team didn’t relax.

If anything, they became quieter, more focused, fully aware of what they were about to face.

Though they hadn’t been present when Takeda first revealed what made many mages currently view him as the most terrifying mage present in the tournament, but Agnes had provided them with detailed reports of his battle style, and the Dean had even obtained a costly crystal recording of Takeda’s last battle.

In fact, he had bragged a lot about how much it had cost him to obtain it, so they had to make sure they didn’t just go out there and lose woefully after all that effort.

After studying it, they understood just how dangerous Takeda truly was, that was why they weren’t offended by the crowd rooting against them, because even them knew exactly what they were up against.

“Let’s get this over with, all of you just come at me,” the clone body Takeda said, curling his fingers in a casual beckoning gesture, which provoked Katie as she was aware that he is just a clone.

Lucas on the other hand didn’t waste time, as his body twitched once, and in the next instant he was already in front of Takeda, glaive thrust forward in a sharp, one-handed strike.

He thrust it low, as if trying to shove Takeda off the battle stage, but Takeda simply raised his left hand in a high guard to block.

Clang!

The sound of the glaive blade scraping across the surface of his hand guard rang out, yet he kept his composure, closing the distance, he shot out his right arm instead, driving his elbow toward Lucas’ chin.

But Lucas didn’t budge, as there was already Qi energy lingered there as if he had anticipated the strike.

At such close range, Lucas released his glaive, grabbed Takeda’s head, and slammed his forehead into his, which caused Takeda to stagger backward before Lucas seized his collar, twisted, and then released him, by flinging him toward the stands.

“Ouuuush!”

The crowd of mages waiting in the stands gasped, surprised at how easily Lucas seemed to have handled Takeda.

However, before Takeda’s body could crash into the invisible barrier shielding the spectators, something unexpected happened.

A wind door appeared in the middle of the arena and swung open, and unlike the last time, when the other Takeda bodies had taken their time emerging, a long chain-like weapon shot out immediately toward the clone representing Takeda.

It coiled around him and dragged him back.

Beatrice, who hadn’t made a single move until now, finally acted, as she formed several hand signs, a massive flaming pattern materializing before her.

In a split second, her flaming mage puppet emerged, hurling its sword toward the wind door, but she wasn’t done yet, as another set of seals appeared before her, summoning two more puppets — one wooden, the other clearly resembling a demonic beast.

But before they could fully emerge, the flaming sword reached the wind door, at that exact moment, the bulky figure the crowd had come to call Earth Takeda stepped out and immediately swung his greatsword.

Bang!

Flames erupted as Earth Takeda blocked the incoming sword, the weapon seemingly detonated by the flaming puppet mage.

When the flames finally cleared, four Takedas stood on the battle stage.

There was the one dragged back onto the stage with a kusarigama, then the ones the crowd had come to call Wind Takeda and Earth Takeda, lastly there was a new Takeda there too and he was wielding the kusarigama, he is a new variant they haven’t seen with his abilities still unclear to everyone watching.

Immediately, the newly appeared Takeda stepped forward, placing his foot on the neck of the fourth Takeda — the clone body — and pressed down.

Since he had dragged him back with his weapon earlier, the clone had already been forced to the ground, and now, apparently no longer needed, the new Takeda simply crushed it beneath his foot.

Causing the clone body to dissolve into fleeting colors that drifted into the air before vanishing completely.

“Now that the lesser distraction is out, you have us,” Wind Takeda said, spreading his hands casually.

“And I have to admit, you’ve really piqued our interest. What do you say... three versus three?”



============================================================
Chapter 1052: Chapter 1052: Who Wins It?
============================================================

“Why is someone from our clan who is this good even allowed to go to such a run-down academy?” Aeron asked the Blade Clan elder who had stayed behind to brief them on what they needed to do, since they too would be fighting soon.

“Well, he is what the clan would like to call an unexpected growth,” the elder replied.

“What is that supposed to mean?” Kaelus asked this time, as he had been interested in matters concerning Lucas for quite a while, even if he acted like he didn’t care.

He just didn’t like the fact that there was someone who wasn’t part of the Blade Stars yet was that strong, and he literally didn’t know much about him.

The feeling of the unknown, not knowing his strengths or what made him tick, was another thing that pissed Kaelus off.

As someone who wished to become the clan’s next leader, he had to be the strongest or at least have the future elders firmly behind him, otherwise he might not last long before being overthrown.

Because in the Blade Clan it was all about strength ruling above all else, though there were rare cases where the strongest didn’t want to rule, Kaelus still didn’t like the fact that a clan member who had supposedly remained unknown all his life and supposed to be unimportant suddenly just appeared out of nowhere and was now receiving the same treatment as the core mages the clan intended to nurture.

“Like I said, he’s an unexpected growth, he literally just returned to the clan for his first holiday, and is already a force to be reckoned among the young generation with his realm, this is what immediately caught the elders’ attention.”

“And after his martial display in the Blood Moon war, they decided to start grooming him properly, giving him private lessons, helping him acquire the right techniques that fitted him and everything. In fact, they were even adamant about pulling him out of the War God Academy.”

“But he seemed really adamant about wanting to return, the clan elders felt that without the Northern Kingdom supporting the academy, it would become even harder for him to grow, and clearly they didn’t want a mage who had shown elder-rank potential to just waste away, but he fought hard for it, and the Patriarch agreed.”

“Literally, they just felt he might have a master he truly respected in the academy, so they gave him some pills to help cover his cultivation demands, that’s all I know,” the elder concluded.

“Well, I guess he really loves the academy then, let’s go prepare , we still have to fight another Northern Kingdom academy too, though he might be family, but he’s on the other side of the coin now, let’s just wish him luck,” Aeron said as he walked away from the viewing window.

Though he might have said that, but even he didn’t truly believe Lucas could weave any magic capable of turning the tide against the School of Silent Night.

After facing them firsthand and losing he and the team had a lot of grievance until the Grand Elder told them not to feel bad about losing, since Takeda hadn’t even been serious against them, as he rely on just two main body clones as if looking for a fair fight when he could have overwhelmed them from the start, he just made them realise with someone like Takeda the odds were insurmountable.

_ _

Looking at the Takedas before him, Lucas couldn’t help worrying, though Wind Takeda was proposing a fair 3-on-3 battle, it clearly wasn’t so, it almost felt like they were underestimating them.

Beatrice had already summoned three puppets and still looked like she had another card to play with the coffin she was resting her back against, yet the Takedas seemed to overlook all that and simply counted her as one opponent.

“What are you stalling for? It’s clearly a good setup. We’ll attack first if you don’t make the first move, you know that, right?” Wind Takeda said again as Earth Takeda focused his gaze on Lucas.

Meanwhile, the new Takeda wielding a kusarigama turned toward Beatrice, and as for the last remaining Takeda, it was obvious who his target was.

“GO!” Lucas shouted, as he didn’t want them attacking first, so he moved towards them immediately after giving the command, with Lucas charging at them he had his glaive coated in wild Qi energy and the air was quaking under the pressure, the two other Takedas on the other hand just simply sidestepped before splitting off in opposite directions.

Meanwhile, the Earth Takeda, who was far bulkier, charged forward. In response, Lucas swung his glaive to meet the sweeping slash head-on.

Bang!

A thunderous explosion rang out as their weapons clashed in a deadlock.

“Arrggh!” Earth Takeda bellowed, channeling his strength and Qi through his blade in an attempt to shove Lucas backward.

But after a few tense seconds, he realized it wasn’t that easy, Lucas’s Qi had an unrelenting quality, stubborn, and refusing to yield an inch, sensing he might lose this initial exchange, which would look bad for morale and the cheering crowd behind him, Earth Takeda decided to pull out a trick.

He shifted his left leg forward into a grounded martial stance, and that wasn’t all, from his legs, a brownish hue spread upward to his knees, as he used his leg to channel mana into the dark ground beneath them.

Lucas didn’t know much about spells, let alone earth-element ones, but he wasn’t about to wait and find out what his opponent was brewing.

As he spread his mental senses wide, scanning everything around him for potential threats, while he used the weight of the clash to push himself backwards.

Bang!

Spiked rocks erupted from the ground, thrusting upward with ferocious speed.

Lucas backpedaled in controlled steps, dodging the deadly protrusions, but the spikes kept coming, relentlessly, driving him toward the arena’s edge.

He twisted his body and swung his greatsword in a wide arc, as a blade of pure Qi shot forth in a crescent moon form, shearing through the rising spikes in a clean line, the energy wave continued onward, slicing apart every earthen protrusion all the way back to Earth Takeda at the source.

In response to the now weakened blade Qi, all he did was brace his sword against his left shoulder, he gripped the hilt firmly with his right hand, holding the blade angled downward from above, while his left shoulder acted as an additional brace.

The Qi energy slammed into it, shoving him backward, while he dug his heels deep into the ground, carving a long furrow in the earth, thin wisps of smoke curled from the blade as the Qi energy finally dissipated.

At the VIP section, the King of the Northern Kingdom couldn’t help gritting his teeth in anger.



============================================================
Chapter 1053: Chapter 1053: Shock 1.
============================================================

This was all because the new ground tiles he had installed were being destroyed right before his eyes. Over the past few days, the amount of money he had to pay the mages responsible for restoring the arena to its proper shape had been steep.

One of the envoys overseeing the competition had advised him to switch to much higher-grade stone tiles, as not only would it reduce the repairing time but also make it harder for the Tier 6 participating mages to damage them.

Yet now, those very tiles that were supposedly built to withstand mid-rank power were still being destroyed.

First, Earth Takeda seemed to completely ignore the natural limits of things and somehow manipulated the same dark rock tiles that he had been told that mid-rank mages would struggle to manipulate, and he had done it easily, as if that wasn’t enough, he dug his legs into the ground and dragged a long trench across the stone surface.

The sight made him grit his teeth in anger, and he couldn’t help cursing the War God team, though they weren’t even the ones tearing up the ground, but his prejudice against them made it easier to blame them for his own shortcomings of not getting High rank tiles.

He could already picture the even larger bill he’d have to pay the array mages who handled arena repairs.

_ _

After blocking Lucas’ Qi attack, Earth Takeda shifted his sword, trying to locate where Lucas had moved, but the moment he adjusted his blade, all he saw was Lucas’ towering figure already before him.

His breathing was slow, mist curling from his mouth, his body flickering with sparks of lightning, his weapon was drawn all the way back to his left side, both hands gripping it tightly, clearly preparing to unleash another devastating strike.

In that motion, in that position, it was obvious to Earth Takeda that Lucas was trying to use his superior strength, which is boosted by his unyielding Qi to knock him clean off the battle stage.

“You’d have to try better than—” Earth Takeda was still speaking when the hit landed.

He had cast a gravity push on himself; normally the spell was used to force an opponent under crushing pressure, but he had twisted it for his own use so he wouldn’t be blasted out of the arena.

Though he had raised his right hand to form a refined circular earth shield to cushion the blow and take some of the hit, however; the glaive’s impact still threw him off balance, smashing him hard into the ground.

Ironically, the gravity push he had cast on himself contributed to how easily he was driven down.

Even then he didn’t stop sliding, as his body continued skidding across the ground, overshooting the range he had intended.

As he slid past, he disrupted Wind Takeda, who had been engaged with Katie in battle as he passed by their middle, both of them watched as Earth Takeda tumbled, twisted midair, and finally drove his greatsword into the ground.

Before pushing his weight down, and slamming his knees down soon after, though he was still sliding backward, fortunately the deeply embedded weapon slowed him enough to halt the momentum that would have carried him off the stage.

With Earth Takeda out of the way, Katie instantly appeared before Wind Takeda using her enhanced speed.

Tribal totem markings appeared from her legs to her ankles flaring up with light that shone even on her equipment set, turning her movement into a blur as she followed through with a right hook aimed straight at him.

He raised his left hand in a high guard to block, then immediately countered with an upward slash toward her inner elbow, but the katana only scraped against the armor covering that area.

Before she could counter with a left uppercut she sent his way, he retreated backwards using the wind element to hasten his steps, allowing him to easily evade her strike, it was clear he had been searching for a slight opening in her defence to exploit and injure her.

“Die!”

While they were still locked on each other, Lucas, who had dashed in to close down Earth Takeda, reached him first instead and decided to engage him by throwing a punch with his right fist.

At that same instant, Wind Takeda pushed out his left hand toward Lucas and cast a Wind Bomb, as a swirling sphere of wind, about the size of a watermelon, shot toward him.

Seeing this, Lucas simply coated his hand with Qi and kept advancing without hesitation.

Bang!

The wind bomb exploded the moment his hand collided with it, scattering sharp wind blades in every direction, several wind blades carved long gashes across his arm, slicing upward.

Despite the injuries and blood pouring out, Lucas didn’t seem to care, with the glaive in hand, he seemed to have abandoned all thoughts of defence, his only intent was forward momentum, pushing relentlessly to deal maximum damage before going down.

After forcing his way through the wind bomb, he planted his feet and took a stance, rotating the glaive overhead with both hands gripping the center as his Qi flowed from his palms into it, coating the weapon once more.

Seeing this, the Wind Takeda ignored Katie and rushed in to close the distance, though his katana was long, but it still couldn’t match the glaive’s reach, and he couldn’t risk giving Lucas too much space to attack freely from a distance away.

As he approached, he executed a quick sweeping motion, slashing twice in rapid succession—clean, efficient sword strikes, but both were deflected by Lucas, who continued rotating his glaive with both hands.

Tink!

Both times his katana was parried by the force of the rotating glaive, the recoil pushed Wind Takeda backward, drawing a frown from him.

Still, he wasn’t about to be outworked, as his blade became coated in a green hue as the air around them grew heavier, and he brought his katana down in a powerful strike.

But at that exact moment, Lucas finally acted, using his left hand, he swept the glaive diagonally from right to left, dragging it downward in a decisive slash.

For a brief instant, Takeda wondered why the mage before him seemed completely unconcerned about the incoming strike, as if he didn’t care for his own safety at all.

Yet that thought didn’t shake his resolve, as he too poured all his strength into the descending katana, fully prepared for whatever clash was about to follow.



============================================================
Chapter 1054: Chapter 1054: Lucas Blade, A Man To Look Out For.
============================================================

With perfectly timed precision, Lucas bent his neck slightly just before the katana came down, the spot where the blade was meant to land was instantly covered by a Tier-5 spirit equipment set which he summoned in a hurry.

Under normal circumstances, such equipment wouldn’t be able to stop Takeda’s blade, Lucas knew that well, so he had made sure to reinforce the area with his Qi, but even with those preparations, when the katana finally descended it still cut into his shoulder, with the blood spurting out and cover his cheeks.

That, however, was exactly what Lucas had been betting on as he drove through the pain, before the katana could drive any deeper, his glaive which had been a little bit slower finally slammed into the Wind Takeda’s ribcage.

A violent shockwave erupted as Wind Takeda was sent flying after he had been hit, even as he was thrown back, he didnt get the chance to retrieve his weapon, as the katana was buried deep in Lucas’s shoulder.

“What the hell?!” Wind Takeda cursed as he tumbled, his body twisting painfully against the hard stone tiles for a few seconds before he finally was able to manipulate the wind surrounding him to cushion himself.

His form was forcefully halted in midair as he tried to regain control.

At that moment, Earth Takeda surged past him, dashing forward like he was surfing on the charging earth pillar he was manipulating, closing the distance, he leapt from the rising earth pillar and swung his weapon with his right hand in a back hand motion at Lucas, who answered back by bringing up his glaive to meet his attack.

Their weapons clashed as they circled one another, exchanging strikes, punches and kicks mixed in as the ringing of steel echoed across the arena, all the while, Lucas continued to fight with the katana still lodged in his left shoulder.

He didn’t dare pull it out, as he knew the instant he did, he’d lose far too much blood, and since he intended to keep fighting till he couldn’t anymore he decided to leave the blade embedded where it was.

As Lucas traded blows with Earth Takeda, the Wind Takeda finally moved back in, launching punches of his own, each fist wrapped in a spherical shell of compressed wind.

Lucas made sure to avoid the hits, after sensing the dangerous aura wrapped around each punch, every strike Wind Takeda threw detonated in the air with a sharp sonic boom, sending shockwaves that made even the mages watching from the stands feel a tremor in their chests.

By now, the entire arena had fallen into stunned silence, they were watching a single mage hold his own against two Tier-6 Takedas, though Lucas was clearly being pressured, but he clearly was refusing to give ground.

As his Qi cloaked his body while faint lightning currents flickered around him, subtly amplifying his attacks as if lending him extra support, it was a shocking sight for many who had expected an easy, one-sided battle.

Instead, Lucas stood firm.

Then, out of nowhere, a blur shot forward, as Katie rushed in to support him, inserting herself between the two Takedas, but the moment she arrived, Wind Takeda reacted instantly, unleashing a rapid combo of kicks.

The first one slammed into her stomach to push her backwards, while the next one snapped upward toward her chin, each kick glowed with a green hue.

He had fought against her before and understood about her spirit equipment set and he was well aware that casual strikes wouldn’t be enough to put her down.

And with Lucas proving troublesome, he had no intention of dragging things out, so his first kick drove her backward, the force alone had been enough to send her skidding backwards as the wind that coated his legs allowed for his hit to affect internally.

Pain exploded through her body immediately, as Katie bent slightly, coughing up blood, but Wind Takeda didn’t stop, as he followed with another vicious kick to her chin, the impact from it lifting her partially off the ground from sheer power.

Seeing this, Lucas tried to break away to help her, but Earth Takeda stepped in immediately, taking a firm stance, he traced a small semicircle with his right foot.

Instantly, five dark, marble-like stones tore free from the arena floor and floated upward, hanging in the air between them, ready to strike.

Then he began kicking them, the first four stones were launched one after another with him alternating from his left and right legs before he twisted his body and slammed his right leg in the final kick of the last floating orb like stone, sending it hurtling toward Lucas.

Lucas was forced to raise his guard and respond to the boulder-like rocks, which were now heavily infused with earth Qi, which made each far denser and heavier than they should have been.

But that was when Wind Takeda finally made his move, as he swiped his right hand downward as if dragging something through the air.

The wind instantly condensed, thought not fully solid, but dense enough that claw-shaped currents became visible, mirroring his motion.

Those wind claws slammed into Katie, striking her hard and sending her flying straight out of the battle ring until her back slammed into the invisible light shield that protected the mages watching in the stands, and now only two mages from the War God Mage Team remained on the battle ring.

Immediately, the crowd supporting the School of Silent Night erupted into loud cheers, as the tension of the battle had worn on them for quite a long time now, and this shift reignited their confidence

With the cheers swelling, Lucas forced himself back using the force from the last attack Earth Takeda sent at him to retreat a few steps, and catch his breath.

He was drenched in his own blood, his body marked by numerous cuts from the wind blades.

Worse still, he could feel serious internal injuries , which had been caused by the Earth Takeda who had had mixed in heavy punches during their clash, his hands were literally reinforced by the refined Stone Fist spell that covered his fist, though they looked like simple gloves on him but they made each and every strike of his brutally heavy.

Yet Lucas still wore that wild smile as he looked toward Wind Takeda, who had turned back to face him alongside Earth Takeda, who was now wielding two earth-forged hammers.

By this point, Lucas could feel his consciousness beginning to fade slightly, he had clearly burned through too much Qi.

Normally, Qi expenditure would only leave a mage exhausted, something recoverable with deep rest, but pushing it this far was dangerously close to draining the life force tied to it.

Using Qi to activate forbidden skills could shorten a mage’s lifespan, but for someone like Lucas, who possessed an enormous reserve of Qi, he had never before reached such a level of exhaustion.

He turned toward Beatrice, who was still locked in battle with the last main Takeda body on the stage , this one had proved to have water-element affinity,

“Sorry for falling short Beatrice...My stop should be happening soon so let me help you get rid of one.”



============================================================
Chapter 1055: Chapter 1055: Shock 2.
============================================================

After saying that, Lucas immediately dashed forward, though he knew he didn’t have much strength left and would soon burn himself out; however, at that moment he was simply grasping at whatever power remained to keep fighting.

He closed the distance and swung his glaive in a backhand motion with his right hand, but the Earth Takeda calmly brought one of his hammer down, pinning the glaive to the ground.

Before he could process what was happening, Lucas had drawn on the last scraps of Qi he had left.

As a crude sword made purely of Qi formed in his left hand, and he thrust it forward, seeing a weapon appear out of nowhere, the Earth Takeda quickly reinforced his defence, by summoning rocky earth armour around his bulky body, its surface etched with rough lines.

Even so, when the blade pierced him, it had only been able to dig slightly into his flesh, as a glint escaped Lucas eye’s, clearly he had another plan, as he forced the remainder of his mana onto the blade, using it to channel the faint lightning he had gathered around his body.

Which, in turn, transferred the remaining lightning into the Earth Takeda’s body after it reached the sword, shocking him with a very high voltage, as Lucas had amplified the lightning’s power by imbuing it with more mana turning it much violent.

“Arrggh!”

The Earth Takeda convulsed violently under the current.

[> Mid Shinobi Spell – Tempest Spear <]

The Wind Takeda who was not far away reacted instantly to support a fellow body by casting a spell, as the air compressed around him, forming four spiralling wind lances that shot forward with a sharp forward motion from his hand.

Lucas, who had fought fiercely since the start, didn’t even attempt to dodge the move, he was completely spent, that was the last of his mana he used to strengthen the lightning, his stamina completely drained, so he simply stood there, one hand still bracing the Qi blade.

As the wind lances slammed into him, they disappeared as if they had never existed, twisting out of existence and merging back with the surrounding air, losing the semi-solid form they had taken to attack.

Though it might have looked like they vanished harmlessly, but that wasn’t entirely true, their effect only showed a second later.

On Lucas’s chest, four spots sank inward where the wind spears had struck, forcing him to bend slightly as he struggled to catch his breath from the powerful force that slammed into him when those four spots appeared, but it didn’t end there, from those four impact points, a spiralling force emerged, driven by the surrounding wind.

Before Lucas could muster any resistance, he was flung out of the battle ring by those spiralling forces pressing against his chest.

After travelling several meters outside the arena, the spiralling wind weakened and faded, causing his body to drop heavily with a dull thud.

This had only happened so easily because he was completely spent and couldn’t resist the force or react in time. Now, the Wind Takeda turned his attention to why the lady he had considered the least threatening was still standing on the battle stage.

“Are you playing around? You should have defeated her by now,” the Wind Takeda said, looking toward the Water Takeda.

“Why not try it yourself, since you think you have a better chance of beating her?” the Water Takeda replied calmly, not even bothering to argue.

Meanwhile, the Earth Takeda had collapsed on the stage after Lucas flew out of the stage, though technically he was still in the fight by being on the stage, but he continued shivering seriously, with his body suffering from lingering numbness.

And even though Lucas only had a weak lightning affinity, when combined with the unyielding nature of his Qi and excess Mana, he had been able to amplify its effects, and with each strand of lightning stacked and overlapped, it multiplied the impact, for the Earth Takeda, who had endured that lightning for some time before the wind attack forced Lucas out of the fight, it meant he would need quite a while to recover from his current predicament.

Now that only one mage remained on the War God Mage Team, the crowd naturally turned their attention to Beatrice.

To many of them, they still didn’t understand why someone like her was even part of the team.

No matter how they tried to picture it, she hadn’t actively fought alongside the others so far, some even saw her as dead weight, and discussions quickly began about why she was the vice captain instead of Lucas, whom many believed deserved the role since he often led the team whenever Alec was absent.

As if that wasn’t enough, conspiracy theories started forming.

Some speculated that her position might be tied to her belonging to an ancient clan, though Lucas too was also a member of an ancient clan, but clearly his clan was not tied to the Northern Kingdom like the Marionette clan was, and people began to feel the War God Mage Academy had simply allowed Beatrice to hold the vice captain spot as a political balance, especially since Alec Gordons, who wasn’t from an ancient clan, was already the team captain.

But regardless of what the crowd thought, Beatrice, who was now the last War God participant still on the battle stage, had worries of her own.

She had truly hoped she wouldn’t have to use the puppet stored inside her coffin, yet it no longer seemed optional, deep down, she knew there was no way she could hold off the two remaining Takedas with just her three main Puppets.

Clearly, the Water-affinity Takeda had been able to fight against her three puppets alone without even breaking a sweat, and on equal footing, this was while she was also actively controlling their movements with her mana threads to make things harder for him.

For puppeteers, once they reach the stage where they can turn living creatures into puppets, those puppets can sometimes fight freely based on instinct.

In several cases, such living puppets even awaken fragments of their past consciousness, allowing them to use spells or skills they learned before becoming puppets with remarkable proficiency.

However, when a puppeteer masters the activation of those skills and has high compatibility with their puppets, they can further enhance the puppets’ combat power by directly controlling them through mana threads which latch on to several joints of the puppet’s body.

By guiding every movement through those threads, the puppeteer can push the puppet’s performance beyond its natural limits.

Beatrice had reached an exceptionally high level of compatibility with her three puppets, especially the flame-mage puppet, whenever she used her mana threads to take direct control, she didn’t just boost its combat strength; she also improved its survivability.

Positioned farther back with a clearer view of the battlefield, she could pull it away from fatal attacks with smooth, precise movements from her fingers.

This constant intervention made it difficult for the Water Takeda to secure a decisive advantage despite his superior physical strength and refined techniques, because whenever he tried to press the flame puppet too hard, Beatrice would withdraw it instantly while sending her other puppets forward to harass him, preventing him from fully neutralizing her main attacker puppet.



============================================================
Chapter 1056: Chapter 1056: Patches.
============================================================

The flaming mage puppet was at the peak of the Tier 6 realm, and there was also the crocodile puppet, which is an ice-affinity demon beast at the High Tier 6 realm.

It often turned the Water Takeda’s water-attribute attacks into ice using its ice-breath skill, deliberately frustrating him and disrupting his offensive rhythm.

Then there was the wooden spider puppet, which acted more like the tank of the puppet squad, whenever Beatrice couldn’t move the other two out of danger fast enough, she would shift the wooden spider forward to shield them from the Water Takeda’s attacks.

Although the wooden spider puppet was also a High Tier 6 Puppet, which means that it wasn’t really capable of dealing significant damage to the Water Takeda. However, it also worked the other way too like a vice versa thing, as the Water Takeda also struggled to seriously harm it.

The Wooden spider puppet had been reinforced with extremely rare Tier 6 wood resources, making it exceptionally durable and difficult to destroy.

Her strategy had been simple: the flaming mage puppet served as the primary damage dealer, while the crocodile acted as a disruptor, and the wooden spider functioned as the defensive tank.

That approach worked well when she only needed to hold one Takeda at bay, but now she had to face two.

She didn’t believe she could continue holding the line alone with just those three, and she remembered the message Alec had left for her, almost as if he had foreseen this very situation.

“Will you surrender, or do we have to make you?” the Wind Takeda asked, stepping into a battle stance as a thin green glow gathered around his hand.

The crowd’s attention shifted entirely to her, while her three puppets formed a loose protective semi-circle around her, leaving a small gap between them as if guarding their master.

Beatrice had her hand extended forward, where thin mana threads stretching from her fingertips connected to each puppet. Then, calmly, she flicked her wrist.

“You three are on your own,” she said softly, severing the mana connections to all three puppets, this action of hers caused many in the crowd who were watching to start murmuring again in surprise, as they couldn’t understand why she would do such a thing, since with her intervention her puppets actually seems way stronger.

But then her next action made the Dean and the Grand Dean, who were seated in the VIP section watching the match, shift slightly in their chairs.

As for the Marionette clan elders who had come to watch her, they were already smiling broadly the moment they realized what she was about to do, because they all knew what was inside the coffin.

They had tried several times to communicate with Beatrice since her team arrived at the Capital, urging her to use the power within the coffin to the fullest, but she had shut down every means of communication with them.

So for them, who had long hoped she would display their clan’s power and put them in the limelight found this situation highly favorable.

“Come out, Patches!” Beatrice said, slapping her hand against the side of the coffin as its door began to creak open slowly.

Normally, Beatrice believed when faced with something like this, her opponents were supposed to rush forward to interrupt whatever she was trying to summon, but the Takedas before her were simply too confident.

They clearly didn’t believe anything she summoned could change the outcome of the battle, they just waited, intending to defeat whatever emerged and break her will in ways she wouldn’t expect.

But when the coffin door finally opened fully, they realized it was still dark inside, though it was completely open, yet a thick dark fog seemed to cover its interior making them unable to see what’s inside.

They tried probing it with their mental energy, but it was instantly repelled the moment it touched the coffin, then two mismatched hands appeared, gripping the right edge of the coffin, followed by another two different hands gripping the left side, slowly pulling the body within outward.

That was how the puppet called Patches appeared for the first time on the capital stage, standing about seven feet tall, with long black hair that covered his face down to the neck.

Though his eyes were still visible through the strands, they were pure white, and unlike the rest of Beatrice puppets with the usual puppet eyes, his was different with a tiny black dot at the center.

He stepped out unsteadily from the coffin, apart from his two main arms, two additional arms had been sewn along the region where his ribs is supposed to be located, most of his body was covered in stitches, as if different body parts had been sewn together, very much like a Frankenstein creation.

Though his upper body was bare, but a sword strap crossed his back, with a small axe hung at his waist, and gloves covered his two primary hands, one of the extra arms was green and muscular, making the audience think of an Orc limb, while the other was scaled like that of a reptilian creature.

Even his chest showed mismatched textures, scaled patches, armored sections, and bare flesh, all held together with dark stitching.

Though the parts had been sewn together, it was clear his body wouldn’t easily come apart even if someone targeted the stitches, there were simply too many to cut through before any real damage could be done, and their dark coloration made many suspect they wouldn’t be easy to unravel at all, clearly they weren’t ordinary thread at all, likely reinforced in some way.

“What the heck is that?” the Wind Takeda finally asked, breaking the silence.

At that moment the entire crowd was quiet, watching the strange puppet with fascination, as Beatrice stepped behind him and used two fingers to press several points on his body.

Each touch made Patches twitch slightly, when she finished, she stepped back and flicked her hand outward, sending another set of thin mana threads toward different points on his body from behind.

Immediately the threads connected, his left eye began to glow with a faint blue hue as he stepped aside to give way to Beatrice behind him, no longer moving awkwardly.

“Let’s do this,” Beatrice said, staring straight into Wind Takeda’s eyes.



============================================================
Chapter 1057: Chapter 1057: Sets.
============================================================

The whole crowd started cheering loudly after hearing Beatrice’s words, the mages supporting the School of Silent Night were all shouting for the Takedas present to make quick work of her.

Meanwhile, the few fanatic mages still supporting the War God Academy began chanting Beatrice’s name loudly, though they had never seen the new puppet before and weren’t aware of what it was capable of, what they knew however was that no matter the odds against them, they would still support the War God Mage Team to the very end.

It was actually surprising to many of the foreign mages present to see a group still stubbornly supporting a team they believed would soon lose, regardless of what Beatrice had shown.

They were especially surprised because the rest of the Northern academies hadn’t received such treatment, and it was the War God Academy who isn’t even among the northern great 8 academies that was getting such treatments which made many of them start suspecting there must be a lot of foul plays present.

Then, before they could fully process what was happening, both Takedas turned into blurs as they rushed toward Beatrice.

Behind the Wind Takeda was a trailing green glow, while a blue hue followed the Water Takeda, from the way they moved, it was clear they planned to avoid the puppets entirely, as if they weren’t even there , and they were just going to go straight for Beatrice.

“I’ll deal with her, help me keep these three busy,” the Wind Takeda said in the last moment diverting the Water Takeda’s attention as wind particles began gathering near his hands, spiralling until two translucent wind katanas formed in his grip.

Once they formed, he didn’t waste any time, as he pushed off the ground with his left leg, and even as he leapt high, he kept pushing higher with his right.

As he stepped through the air like he was walking casually, wind footprints appeared beneath his feet.

The three puppets acting as the first line of defence all looked toward him. In fact, the flaming mage puppet was just about to act, but as he raised his hand to draw his sword, he suddenly felt something curling tightly around his wrist.

Which caused him to raise his head to clearly see what had restrained him from drawing his blade, that was when he noticed the chain-like weapon wrapped around his hand was connected to the weapon wielded by the Water Takeda.

From the sides, the other two puppets were already closing in on him, trying to restrain him and free the flaming mage puppet, to the Water Takeda they were acting more like a pest to him with the way they were acting together as it always made it hard for him to pick them out one by one.

At the same time, the Wind Takeda was already descending from his high jump, but Patches simply stepped forward slowly, positioning himself in front of Beatrice.

His right hand moved behind his shoulder, smoothly drawing the sword strapped across his back.

In one fluid motion, the Wind Takeda spun mid-air, before twisting his dual katanas into a scissoring strike, but the puppet merely raised its sword and blocked the attack.

A loud clang rang out on impact, as a shockwave rippled across the arena floor, with dust lifting in a circular wave around them. Immediately, Takeda opened his mouth to cast a spell, but his mental senses suddenly caught something.

Instinctively, he raised his leg high to block in mid-air.

Bang!

He felt the bones in his leg creak as if they were about to snap upon impact, being an expert, he quickly used his ninjutsu to divert most of the force, and then used the remaining momentum to propel himself upward.

He drifted back down like he was guided by a gentle breeze, somersaulting once before landing lightly on the ground, when he looked up at the puppet again, the casual expression he usually wore was gone.

A throbbing pain pulsed through his left leg, the one he had used to block the punch, which had clearly come from the orc arm stitched onto Patches.

He studied the puppet carefully, even at this distance, he still couldn’t accurately determine its realm.

Meanwhile, the Water Takeda had already pulled back slightly, realizing it was becoming difficult to handle all three puppets at once.

“Wind, I don’t think I’ll be able to handle all three of them like this for long while fighting with a handicap,” the Water Takeda said.

That made the Wind Takeda pause in thought, he had been the one who proposed the three-versus-three fight, but the Earth Takeda still hadn’t gotten back up after his last bout against lucas, which has made him effectively useless since then.

He knew he needed a third fighter to gain a real advantage against Beatrice, yet his pride wouldn’t allow him to summon another Takeda through the wind gate after he had greatly underestimated the war god mages.

As he felt calling one of the remaining two Takedas would feel like admitting weakness, after a moment of thought, he decided it was better to let the Water Takeda go all out instead.

“Do it!” the Wind Takeda said, stepping forward again and swinging his wind-forged blades at Patches, the puppet’s sword movements looked slightly slower than his, yet somehow every strike was still blocked with precise timing.

Meanwhile, the Water Takeda smiled faintly, clearly pleased, he already knew that when the three Tier-6 puppets worked together, gaining an advantage was difficult. Now that he had permission, he pushed off the stage and retreated several steps away from them.

As he moved back, runic tattoos began spreading across his body and ninja attire. Within seconds, parts of the outfit turned a deep icy blue.

His mask shifted colour as well, and even his weapon seemed wrapped in a new energy layer, subtly altering its form.

Frosty air began to seep from his hands, and cold vapour escaped through the tiny openings of his mask, when he lifted his gaze toward the three puppets again, he was now clad in what clearly looked like a Tier-6 spirit equipment set crafted specifically to complement his abilities.

The spectators reacted immediately.

Until now, the Takedas had only fought in their standard ninja uniforms — the official attire of their institution, It also seemed to be inscribed with a Tier-5 protection rune. But now the spectators were realising that apart from possessing that seemingly cheat level special ability, Takeda also seems to have the power to equip every one of those bodies with spirit equipment sets.

And not just any spirit equipment, but likely equipment sets that were perfectly tailored to their individual abilities, the thought alone made many in the crowd shift their gaze toward the Wind Takeda, waiting to see if he would reveal something similar, however he didn’t.

Instead, it was the Water Takeda , who had been fighting defensively up until now, changed in his presence completely, like a whole new person.

The moment his spirit equipment set completely manifested, he dashed forward, abandoning caution as he charged straight at the three puppets.



============================================================
Chapter 1058: Chapter 1058: Sets 2.
============================================================

Seeing him dash so recklessly toward the three puppets without any hint of caution made many spectators uneasy.

However the flaming mage puppet was the one to react first, by dashing forward and thrusting out its left hand forward, and from seemingly nowhere, flaming lances formed as it manipulated the fire particles in the air to conjure them.

With a sharp motion, the lances shot toward the Water Takeda, however; he just weaved through them like a blue phantom, each near miss leaving a faint azure glow in his wake.

Within moments, he was already in front of the flaming puppet.

“Die!” the flaming puppet mage said in a flat, emotionless tone, showing that clearly its only objective was the elimination of the Water Takeda.

His blade wrapped in a blazing aura, swung down in a diagonal motion, the crowd gasped as his strike appeared to connect, but a split second later, they noticed a blue streak sliding past the puppet.

It almost looked as if the Water Takeda had phased through the puppet, but that wasn’t the case, because before the strike had landed, he had already cast the Ice Body Clone spell, so what the flaming puppet had cut was merely an ice replica, which was perfectly positioned to take the hit.

With his spirit equipment set equipped and active, Water Takedas’s speed and strength didn’t just increase significantly, more importantly, it seems to have helped neutralize one of his biggest weaknesses, which was the interference of Ice.

While clad in that set, he could control ice element but that wasn’t all , as he could also instantly convert it to water, freely switching between the primary and sub-elements as needed.

The spectators watched as the blur they’d been tracking began to stabilise, for a moment it resembled flowing liquid, then it solidified, revealing the Water Takeda once again.

Who had used his ninjutsu technique to switch positions, going from standing before the flaming mage puppet one moment to appearing a few meters behind it the next.

“Arrggh!” The flaming mage puppet, finally realising it had been tricked, as he tried to pull his sword free from the frozen sculpture, but that was easier said than done.

As not only was the blade firmly stuck, but frost had already begun creeping up his right hand, travelling through the sword connection.

The Water Takeda didn’t even bother turning back, from experience, he knew the spell would be able to restrain the flaming puppet for at least a minute, and to him that was more than enough time.

With the main damage dealer temporarily neutralised, he lowered his stance and accelerated forward, dashing toward the wooden spider puppet and the crocodile puppet a few meters away.

Each step he took left faint frozen footprints behind, sending the Silent Night supporters into another frenzy of cheers.

The wooden spider advanced first, swinging its two elongated wooden legs in a pincer-like motion to trap him.

But Water Takeda simply rotated his weapon, hooking the curved ends around the incoming limbs while dropping into a low slide, as he slipped cleanly beneath the spider puppet’s body.

He knew damaging that reinforced wooden frame would be difficult, so he didn’t waste effort trying.

Instead, a long icy trail formed behind him as he slid, and when he reached the underside of the puppet, he pushed off the ground and sprang upward into the air...

With him launching into the air like that with all his strength, the trailing end of the Kusarigama chain which was still latched onto the spider puppet’s front legs — tugged violently.

The force from it twisted the puppet off balance, flipping it upside down and exposing its underbelly, by the time the Water Takeda landed atop it, the chain had already slipped free from its legs.

As he immediately casted a spell by forming a hand seal which he pushed downward.

[> Mid Ninjutsu Spell — Ice Age. <]

Using his legs like conduits, frost spread rapidly outward, crawling across the wooden spider puppet’s body, since outright destroying it would take too long, he chose the smarter option, which was immobilisation.

Ice layered over the puppet, stiffening its joints and sealing its movements, turning it into a massive ice sculpture.

Just then, the flaming mage puppet finally broke free, while the crocodile puppet lunged toward him as he stood on the frozen spider’s body.

The Water Takeda flicked his Kusarigama forward in a sharp motion, then tugged the midpoint of the chain to recall it in a fast move after flickering it out.

Fortunately the attack had already triggered before the weapon was recalled, as a thin wave of pale-blue energy shot outward the instant the weapon snapped forward.

The crocodile puppet opened its jaws and exhaled another burst of frost breath to counter it, but while it was occupied, the flaming mage puppet leapt high, sword raised, descending straight toward The Water Takeda.

Yet when it landed, he had struck nothing, the Water Takeda’s body dissolved into a blue afterimage, and when he reappeared, the crowd saw him standing behind the flaming mage puppet, with his chain already looped tightly around its neck, and his right knee raised and pressing at the puppets back.

The puppet reached back with its free left hand, trying to resist, but the Water Takeda released his right grip on the chain which was near the sickle which is at the end of the chain.

Then he yanked hard with his left hand, the chain snapped sideways, dragging the sickle across the puppet’s neck in a brutal arc, eventually slicing clean through.

Immediately, blood began pouring out of the neck, but he clearly wasn’t done with the puppet, now that his weapon was fully free, he gripped the Kusarigama with both hands again and kept flicking and tugging the chain, deliberately slicing through every joint of the flaming mage puppet from behind, as he wasn’t willing to take any chances.

A single cut to the neck might have been enough to disable a normal opponent, but to him what was before him is still a puppet and a weapon of Beatrice, so if she stubbornly tried to force it to keep moving manually with her mana threads, it could still pose a threat.

So he methodically severed each joint, ensuring that even manual control wouldn’t allow it to perform basic movements.

After finishing, he twisted his body, spinning the chain with him, and after two full rotations later, he came to a halt, settling into a stance, as his right hand gripped a section of the chain, using that point to guide the sickle as it flew outward.

The blade hooked cleanly into the open mouth of the crocodile puppet, which had leapt at him again, this time he yanked hard, dragging it down before slamming its body against the ground.

But he wasn’t done as he swung the chain again this time to the opposite direction— another slam.

Then again. And again. He repeated the motion about ten times until he could tell most of the creature’s bones were shattered.

With a smooth flick of his right hand while his left held the opposite end steady, he unhooked the sickle from the crocodile’s mouth.

Finally, he jumped down from the wooden spider puppet, which was still fully encased in ice.

The prolonged contact of his leg on the puppet while channeling frost through his feet had ensured the freezing held firm.

Keeping the spider immobilized had been deliberate , as he believed it was the most troublesome of the three and could easily interfere with his plan to eliminate the others.



============================================================
Chapter 1059: Chapter 1059: Patches And Beatrice 1.
============================================================

Thud!

A light shockwave rippled outward the instant the Water Takeda dropped from atop the wooden spider puppet, which was still completely frozen in place.

Now it was effectively two main bodies against Beatrice, who had only one active puppet left, the Wind Takeda hadn’t moved since his last exchange, and neither had Patches.

The puppet simply stood there, motionless, almost as if it were patiently waiting for them to make the first move.

“Remember when I gently asked you to surrender earlier? I’ll give you the same option again, so are you surrendering, or do we have to make things difficult for you? Because believe me, as strong as that puppet of yours may be, you should know there’s no way it can take both of us at once,” the Wind Takeda said, settling into a combat stance.

Beside him, the Water Takeda casually twirled his weapon, with his left hand holding one end while his right twisted the chain attached to the sickle blade.

“Unfortunately, I know a certain stubborn guy who would do the exact opposite,” Beatrice replied.

“And honestly, I hate that all these responsibilities suddenly fall on me whenever he isn’t around. But what choice do I have?”

“I just happen to be his vice-captain, and he always seems to have this ridiculous amount of faith in me, so even if I wanted to back out now, my conscience wouldn’t let me rest. If you want this win, you’ll have to earn it properly, as I’m not giving you an easy way out.”

“I understand what you’re doing,” she continued calmly.

“You’re hesitating because you think pushing this further might expose more than you intended. Unfortunately for you, things won’t go your way today, because even if I lose... I’ll make sure I go down fighting.”

At first when she started talking, The Wind Takeda had thought she was actually considering his offer, and he had been trying to search inside of his mind for the right words say in order to completely cajole her into letting everything go, but by the time she finished speaking, his brows were already deeply furrowed.

It was now clear to him, she wasn’t going to budge, he could tell that surrendering had never been an option for her.

Then Beatrice finally moved, as her hands dragged through the air as if pulling invisible strings, seeing that motion made the Water Takeda instinctively step back; as he knew from experience that whenever she did that, an attack usually followed.

The Wind Takeda, however, didn’t think much of it at first — until, in the next instant, Patches vanished from his sight, immediately confusion flickered across his face as he quickly attempted to retreat.

Wind wings had already begun forming around his legs, the beginnings of a movement spell, but before it could fully activate, Patches reappeared right in front of him.

The puppet swung its sword diagonally downward from right to left. Reacting instantly, the Wind Takeda raised his conjured wind katana upward, as the two blades merged as he attempted to block while already planning to use the impact to propel himself further back.

But just before the blades were meant to clash, Patches abruptly withdrew its sword, and from below, one of the extra arms (the orc arm), shot forward in a brutal punch.

Because of his upward swing, a small opening had been left in his guard... and it was exploited perfectly by the puppet.

Bang!

The punch landed squarely on his stomach, and for a split second, time seemed to freeze as his body lifted slightly off the ground, then a visible shockwave burst out from his back, and he was violently hurled away in the direction the force travelled a second later.

Meanwhile, Patches twisted on its heel and immediately dashed toward the Water Takeda.

“So I was your real target all along, huh?” The Water Takeda said with a faint smile as he began circling the stage at speed, though it looked as if he had just been running aimlessly earlier, there was far more intention behind his movements.

Now that Patches twisted unnaturally and dashed straight for him, he swung his weapon, causing the chain to coil rapidly around his body until it wrapped tightly across his chest like a belt.

But he wasn’t finished as he did that to free his hands, and immediately he started forming hand seals quickly, and just before he completed them, Patches was already in front of him with his right hand stretched forward.

[> Mid Ninjutsu Spell — Water Seal Prison <]

In that instant, several spots along the path he had run suddenly lit up with a blue hue, as water runes appeared on the ground, and bubble-like tendrils of compressed water surged upward toward Patches.

A single water tendril wouldn’t have been enough to restrain Patches, but more and more wrapped around his arms, legs, and shoulders, tightening with crushing pressure, this wasn’t ordinary water; it was highly compressed, dense enough to act like solid restraints.

Then, from the corner of his eye, the Water Takeda noticed movement , someone was slipping low past the outer range of the water seal prison he had created without triggering any of the traps.

When he got a clearer look, the recognition hit him instantly.

Beatrice.

The very puppeteer whose puppet he was currently exerting his full attention to keep restrained.

He hadn’t expected her to close the distance herself, since the fight began, many had underestimated her, assuming she was just another traditional summoner type of mage, one that was dangerous only through her puppets and otherwise helpless once those summons were restrained.

That assumption was precisely why he had been circling earlier, secretly laying traps across the battlefield, to restrain the puppet.

All of this had been done so that once he kept the seal active, the Wind Takeda could finish the job from above.

In fact, the Wind Takeda, who had been punched away earlier, had already halted his momentum using the wind currents in the air to steady himself after regaining control and was now floating in the air, with semi-visible wind wings flapping behind him as he waited for the Water Takeda’s signal to launch his attack.



============================================================
Chapter 1060: Chapter 1060: Patches And Beatrice 2.
============================================================

But from what he was seeing, it clearly wouldn’t be easy, and now he was truly regretting underestimating the War God Mage Academy Team, as he started to feel if not for his big mouth earlier, he would have just simply summoned another main body and all these would have been all over.

“Water, leave the seal and get out of range,” Wind Takeda said.

“But—” Water Takeda was about to complain, but in the end he said nothing, as he began retreating backward while still keeping both his hands locked in the seal, he wanted to put a good distance between them before letting go, because he knew the moment he broke the seal, the compressed water prison that had been restraining Patches would collapse.

But as he tried to escape, Beatrice swung her hand outward, as barely visible mana threads spread through the air, seeing her make a move and not wanting to take any risks, he cast a spell blowing out from his mouth.

[> Low Ninjutsu Spell – Ice Mist. <]

As he activated it, a cold mist spread outward from his lips into the air, normally, this wasn’t a spell that made much difference at his current realm, but that wasn’t his goal.

What he really wanted was to distinguish where Beatrice’s mana threads were located, and when the mist spread, it revealed them immediately, as thin white lines began appearing in the air as the icy particles clung to the threads like magnets after spreading in the air before him.

He quickly realised the mana strings were already surrounding him, and all that remained was for her to tighten her grip, with danger so close, he couldn’t afford to keep holding Patches in place.

His fingers separated, breaking the seal he had been maintaining with such effort.

The compressed water currents restraining Patches instantly collapsed to the ground once his hands came apart. Meanwhile, Beatrice, who had slid sideways out of Patch’s way, dragged her threads sharply through the air.

Now, with the ice mist hanging in the air, her mana threads became far more visible, though they weren’t completely clear, but everyone could now see the faint white strings suspended across the battlefield, all leading back to a single point — her fingers, which seemed to be controlling well over fifty separate thread positions at once, they keep wondering just where the mana strings were anchored to for it to work the way Beatrice was using them but no matter how much they strained their thoughts they were still unable to come up with anything.

With a forceful yank, she pulled backward with both hands, as the threads that had been floating loosely suddenly converged at incredible speed.

And it just so happened that the one standing at the central point where those strings were about to close in... was the Water Takeda.

“Fuck!” the Water Takeda cursed, shaking his body slightly so his weapon could slide free from his chest, he immediately grabbed one end of the chain with his right hand immediately it started coming off, pouring both his mana and Qi into it , especially an excess amount of his Qi, using it to sharpen the edge of the Kusarigama sickle for what he intended to do next.

He whipped the weapon around himself in a tight arc, as the energized chain sliced through several visible threads he could see at once, forcing an opening. Without hesitation, he retreated.

But just as he retreated, his danger senses screamed, not knowing exactly where the next strike would come from, he dropped low into a knee slide.

As he started to feel something with tremendous pulling force sliced past his face, grazing his cheek.

Pain flared instantly as he could feel his blood pouring, but he ignored it, as he slid he used the momentum to bend his head towards the opposite direction, he then saw Beatrice drawing her left hand back, and he could tell that it was the very motion of that hand that must had tightened the threads enough to nearly carve into his face.

If he hadn’t bent when he did, even he knew that the damage would have been far worse. Still, that didn’t matter now, the important thing was that he had regained distance.

[> Mid Shinobi Spell – Wind Slam. <]

The moment he cleared the immediate danger zone, the Wind Takeda acted without waiting for any signal, from high above, he thrust both palms downward, as a crushing wave of wind pressure started to gather around his hand waiting to descend instantly on intent.

Beatrice reacted at once, stretching her hand toward the wooden puppet which was still frozen near the center of the stage.

“Come here!” she shouted, dragging her hands sharply through the air, though many of the lower-rank mages couldn’t clearly see what she had done, but some mid-rank mages with stronger mental perception had caught it, she had swept her mana threads in a straight line, and they latched onto the frozen wooden spider almost like they had magnetic magic imbued to them not even removing as she yanked that hard.

The ice anchoring the puppet to the ground began cracking slowly before snapping apart, the massive wooden spider shifting back toward its original position.

Beatrice then dashed forward at the same time, sliding in the final moment before the Wind Takeda’s attack fully descended, from the height where the Wind Takeda floated, the air gathered densely around his hands, compressing into a terrifying downward force.

Bang!!!!

The stage seemed to sink under the pressure, debris blasted outward, dust lifting violently as the lower stands closest to the arena were hit by powerful gusts, even spectators far away could feel the oppressive wind.

Beatrice had tried to use the frozen wooden spider puppet as a buffer against the incoming force, but when the Wind Slam fully landed and the massive puppet slammed into her, she couldn’t stop herself from coughing out a mouthful of blood.

Patches fared slightly better, the shockwave forced him down to one knee, but despite the crushing pressure, he refused to collapse completely.

His stitched body trembled as he struggled against the weight bearing down on him.

At the edge of the battlefield, the Water Takeda calmly raised one hand, and a bow formed from condensed water in his grip, its surface shimmering with icy mana, and he drew the string back with his second hand, as a water arrow manifested instantly, pulsing with dense power.

He released it, but he didn’t stop there as he drew again, and again.

He ended up shooting four shots in rapid succession, each aimed at a different vital point.

Under normal circumstances, Patches might have dodged or intercepted them, but under the lingering pressure of the Wind Slam, his movement was severely restricted, he could only brace his different muscles as the arrows struck.

Boom! Boom! Boom! Boom!

Each impact carried not only crushing force but intense freezing energy, wherever an arrow struck, ice spread rapidly across his body, it wasn’t gradual, it raced outward aggressively, locking joints and stiffening movement.

His shoulders were hit first, as frost crawled across them instantly, making it difficult for him to lift his main arms.
