And indeed, they could see the rest of the Central Kingdom crowd splintering: as some cheered for Aeron, and the others cheered for Kealus.

“I have a feeling those two might just fight before the battle even begins,” an Eastern fan joked, causing those beside him to burst into laughter.

Though some Central Kingdom fans overheard it, but they just simply cheered louder, denying the Eastern fans any satisfaction, moments later, the judge continued his announcement.

“Following them are Darius Blade, Talon Blade, and Sora Blade.”

At that moment, the entire Central Kingdom section went wild as they finally got to see the two new faces from the Blade clan Team.

It wasn’t known why these mages hadn’t participated until now, but one thing was certain, and that was the fact that they were far more battle-hardened than the pair of Selara and Neriah Blade who they replaced.

Seeing the Central Kingdom mages cheering that loudly made those in the Eastern wing scrutinize the newcomers closely.

Talon Blade wore flame-colored gauntlets on his hands, with a short spear slung across his back, as for Sora, he had twin tonfa blades resting at his waist.

As the crowd of Easterner fans began assessing their potential battle prowess, but then the judge spoke again, drawing everyone back to silence.

“And from the last tunnel, let us welcome the War God Mage team, who are being led out by the acting vice captain, Lucas Blade,” the judge announced.

Immediately, a wave of voices rippled through the crowd, as murmurs and whispers questioning why an injured mage was being fielded by the higher ups of the War God Academy.

There were even some who grumbled that if the War God Mage Academy team wanted to throw the match away, at least they should have given the opportunity to the mages on their reserve team, who hadn’t gotten a chance to show what they were capable of, since this likely might be their last battle.

They believed that if the War God Mage team was truly prepared to forfeit the match, this would have been their best move, but then Lucas Blade finally stepped out of the tunnel assigned to the War God Mage team, and the entire crowd’s eyes shifted toward him.

His face was as fierce as always, with his greatsword and the staff-like handle for its glaive mode strapped to his back as he strode forward.

“There are no longer any wounds on his body, not even a single scar from the nasty cuts he got the day before,” a certain mage in the otherwise silent crowd finally mustered the courage to mutter.

“I thought they said the War God Academy is a penniless institution and wouldn’t be able to help their mages recover this fast,” another mage added, looking completely perplexed. To him, it was baffling why Lucas appeared in perfect health.

“Why are you people acting so shocked? No matter what that broke institution tries, there’s no way they’ll come out on top of this battle. It just isn’t for them. Let’s bet—I can assure you their team will be the first to be eliminated,” yet another mage declared, acting utterly certain they wouldn’t last.

“Following behind are Brandon Gordons, Arthur Gordons, and Knight Gordons,” the judge continued, listing out the names of the other mages who would represent the War God Mage Academy team.

Though the crowd remained largely silent and subdued, but the fanatic fans perked up their ears immediately they heard the names of those that followed Lucas.

The calling of the name "Gordons" had become deeply associated with the War God team, since their captain was a Gordons. However, in the last match, it seemed all the main clan members of the Gordons clan hadn’t participated.

Now that they heard the name called again, the fans were somehow hoping for a touch of that "Gordons’ magic" to unfold, they began to cheer, though not as loudly as the fans from the other two kingdoms, who were booing, but they were giving it their all to show support for the War God team.

As the three Gordons males strode out of the tunnel, they waved toward the section where their fans were gathered.

“And lastly, the captain of the War God Mage Academy team, Alec Gordons,” the judge announced, and this time the entire Northern fans’ corner fell completely silent, as if they couldn’t believe their ears.

Even the other two sections that had been booing were stunned to see the stubborn Fanatic group in the Northern crowd go quiet, it was at that moment that Alec walked out of the tunnel.

That kimono, that carefree attitude, that smile, the two weapons strapped to his left waist while he rested his left hand on them as he strolled out slowly...

They couldn’t deny it, this was the one mage they’d missed so dearly. In that instant, it felt as if barrels of gunpowder had exploded.

“Alec Is Back!!!!!!!!!!”

The chant "Alec is back" echoed relentlessly as the Northern corner, which was packed with fanatic fans, erupted in shouts, even with their smaller numbers, their voices swiftly overshadowed and overwhelmed the boos.

They cheered so loudly that the fans from the other kingdoms had to stop and stare at the man who had just emerged, from the VIP section reserved for the teams, mages like Dragov Dragonmir and Vincent Whitefang couldn’t help but smile.

“Show us exactly how much you’ve improved!” Vincent murmured while staring at Alec.

At the higher VIP level, just one step above where the participants sat, reserved for the leaders and deans, apart from the deans of the Northern Kingdom who were used to Alec,

Those from the other kingdoms still didn’t rate him highly and just felt the crowd was making too much of a scene for a team that was about to be eliminated.

But while all this was happening, Old Man Alderic didn’t pay them any heed as he kept his eyes fixed on the king of the Northern Kingdom.

The monarch wore a deep frown the entire time; when Alec’s name had been called, he looked completely perplexed, and after seeing him emerge, he clearly harbored a bad feeling about it.

Just seeing the King like that made Alderic happy.



============================================================
Chapter 1071: Chapter 1071: The Last Loser Bracket Match.
============================================================

# The Day Before

After the hugs broke, Alec was quick to ask about the situation, when he heard they’d been defeated, and learned of Lucas’s and Beatrice’s conditions, his gaze turned cold.

But after a few seconds, his expression softened, and he sighed in relief, as he was grateful he’d made it back in time before the Loser bracket commenced, his words coming out as:

“Thank God there’s still a loser’s bracket match, let’s win that and get revenge for the loss against The Silent Night. I’m back now.”

After that, he passed by Lucas’s room and tossed him a Tier 7 healing pill, seeing this, Lucas almost rejected it due to how precious it is, even he knew that the other academy were giving their mage Tier 5 healing pills,

But Alec just scratched his head at his words and said if Lucas didn’t want it, he could throw it out the window, as he wouldn’t be taking it back.

Seeing Alec’s nonchalant expression as he spoke filled Lucas with a mix of emotions, and he couldn’t find the right words to say to express his gratitude because he knew the Tier 7 healing pill was way above what was needed to fully heal him.

“Seriously, look at it from my point of view, I want you out there with me tomorrow. It’s time we show off a little. I guess I’ve been pretty laid-back about this competition from the start, but what do you say, from tomorrow, we go all out?” Alec said to Lucas, cracking his neck awkwardly at first before stepping closer and pointing at him.

Seeing Alec that riled up and eager, even Lucas didn’t realize when he popped the pill into his mouth and swallowed it.

Immediately, he felt his whole body undergoing changes, and his aura growing more robust, he even sensed he wasn’t far from breaking into the Tier 7 mage realm.

The pill hadn’t just healed him; it had cleared out various hidden injuries he had incurred up till now, even he couldn’t deny the effects because he could feel his mana flowing way much better than it has ever done in a while.

He watched Alec walk out the door with a face full of appreciation, he always felt he owed Alec far more than he could ever repay.

It was one of the reasons he’d fought to attend the War God Mage Academy again, even when the clan elders had all tried to make him change his mind with all the rewards and private lessons that they claimed would be available to him.

If anyone had told him that such an opportunity would be handed to him, and that he would turn it down, he would have called it a lie, as never had he even dreamed of such a chance, let alone rejecting it.

But then again, when he reflected on everything he’d gained just from being close to Alec, his decision didn’t feel hard at all again, because he believed he’d progress far better alongside Alec and the team, who inspired him to strive and become a better version of himself, than in the clan, where everything boiled down to cutthroat competition.

After leaving Lucas’s place, he dropped by and slipped a mental pill into Beatrice’s mouth, and two minutes later, she woke up.

Seeing Alec back, she finally sighed in relief, knowing she didn’t need to push herself so hard with the knucklehead around again.

That was how Alec distributed Tier 6 healing pills to the rest of his team members, instructing them to use them immediately as he didn’t want anybody to have hidden injuries.

After wrapping up such matters, he declared that he’d handle the next match alone, however; the others vehemently disagreed, pointing out that it was a five-man team loser’s bracket and he couldn’t go in solo.

_ _

“Alec!!!”

Though more than a few minutes had passed since Alec stepped onto the stage, the crowd’s cheering hadn’t stopped, even after the judge raised his hand to silence them, it took several more seconds before they quieted.

“Well, it seems you’re all riled up and ready for this battle to begin?” the judge asked, as resounding “YES!” replies echoed from every corner.

“Then I pronounce this match begun.” He declared, rocketing into the air at such blinding speed that a faint boom lingered even after he’d vanished from view.

Alec glanced toward the two opposing teams, then turned to his own teammates.

“What do you guys say? You don’t need to do anything at all, leave them all to me. I’ll be quick,” Alec said, trying to convince his teammates for what felt like the hundredth time that they didn’t need to lift a finger, he was trying to make up for the fact that he just disappeared on them.

“Well, I don’t know about the rest, but I think I want to clash against the captain of the Blade team,” Lucas said while looking toward where the Blades stood, this caused Alec who had his back to the others to tilt his head in that direction.

Immediately, he nodded, as if coming to some understanding.

“That can be worked out,” Alec said.

“I would be fighting another mage too,” Brandon swiftly chipped in before Alec could slip out of his good mood.

“Me too!” Arthur, who didn’t want to be left behind, said almost immediately. And before Alec could even process what was happening, even Knight claimed he would battle one mage from the opposing sides.

Alec looked at his supposed teammates and couldn’t envision a comeback that would make them abandon the idea of competing against at least one mage.

“Ok!!” he said as he eventually agreed, while Alec and the rest of the War God Mage team were talking, the whole crowd watched in surprise at how casually they were acting.

Meanwhile, the other two teams, who were close enough to were able to overhear their conversation and catch every word because they all have heightened hearing, just listening to Alec’s act so confidence infuriated them.

In the meantime, Hoshino Ren had seized the opportunity to summon his Bound spirit, Akagami, as a crimson aura spread out as it overlapped with him.

“Shit, we were too busy getting caught up in the boasting of that small team that we didn’t even focus on our main opponents in this round,” Kealus said as he turned to stare at Ren.

But then he noticed something, Ren had already dashed out, heading straight for Alec Gordons, who had his arms crossed, hands tucked into each other’s sleeves due to the extra fabric in his kimono.

Seeing this, Alec smiled and said,

“Spread out.” As he spoke, he moved his left hand and drew his Bloody Changdao from its scabbard in a reverse grip, he raised the weapon high in a defensive stance as Ren, shuffling forward like a specter, appeared before him and swung down his Qi formed Katana.

Bang!

A loud explosion rang out, but unlike what Ren had expected, Alec didn’t fly backward, he didn’t lose his balance—in fact, the hand he’d used to block hadn’t even flung back.

In every scenario Ren had pictured, where he believed a single move would shatter Alec’s infuriating confidence, none of it came to pass, on the contrary, the only thing he’d accomplished was forcing Alec’s hand down slightly.

But in a swift motion, Alec swiped his right hand through the air, with his middle and index fingers extended, at their tips flickered a flame that trailed along, until his fingers pointed straight at Hoshino’s head.

[> Innate Combination Spell - Flaming Bullet. <]

In mid-air, smaller particles of earth began to coalesce into the shape of a sniper bullet at a very fast speed, but that wasn’t all, it glowed a fiery yellowish-red, as if freshly pulled from a smithy’s forge.

The instant the bullet materialized, Ren’s senses screamed danger, with that level of urgency, he knew, if it landed, he wouldn’t like the outcome.



============================================================
Chapter 1072: Chapter 1072: Unpredictable 1.
============================================================

Bang!!

Just as Alec fired the bullet, Ren managed to shift his head to the side, escaping the bullet with just a few inches while using his strength to shove Alec backward, while he dug his legs into the ground dragging a long line along with him.

“Alec!!!” The cheers for Alec grew even louder, prompting Ren to glance behind him as he could feel a bad feeling growing in his mind. There, he saw that the bullet Alec had released struck one of his teammates square in the head,

knocking him out in a single shot, it was at that moment Ren realized Alec had never intended to hit him.

He’d been aiming for the teammate all along, it just so happened that Ren had been in the way, and yet Alec had still decided to take the shot, deceiving him into thinking he was the primary target.

Clearly, Alec had trusted that Ren would be capable of tilting his head and evading the bullet at the last second, and he had done exactly that, though normally his Innate combination attack should have critically injured the mage if it wasn’t able to kill him directly, however; Alec had designed the bullet’s front to be flat, delivering blunt force instead of piercing damage.

He’d also dialed back the power at the final moment to avoid inflicting a critical injury, while Ren was pondering the intricate details of what Alec had just executed, even when he was in a battle with him, it just gave him the chills,

He watched Alec shuffle about almost like he was dancing, with a smile on his face as he dodged various spells hurtled toward him from the other three members of Ren’s team.

“Brilliant,” Ren murmured.

Clang!!

Meanwhile, on the other side, Lucas couldn’t help cursing when he realized Alec had completely ignored the Blade clansmen, leaving them all for him.

And right now, he had to face both Aeron and Kealus, and really, it wasn’t going well. He was taking the beating of his life, and as annoying as it was, he couldn’t get a read on their fighting style.

That was because they had zero synergy whatsoever, yet they both came at him like they were competing for the killing blow.

Literally, the only reason he was able to hold on this long was because it felt like they were fighting themselves internally.

He’d swat away a piercing from Kealus’s Aether blade, only to face a slash from Aeron’s short swords, and whenever he decided to deflect Aeron’s short swords instead, all that awaited him was a cut from Kaelus’s Aether blade.

Knight, Brandon, and Arthur, on the other hand, seemed to be faring much better in their duels, though he’d always believed his clan was among the best in combat, not only him but everyone in the crowd was shocked to see the trio of Gordons clansmen matching the Blade clan blow for blow.

In fact, there was even a hint of suppression from them, showing they held the advantage in the battle.

Darius and Brandon were going slash for slash, and not only did Brandon boast superior Qi execution, but he was also able to cast Earth Lock spells to root Darius in place and force him to take attacks whenever he tried to retreat.

As for Knight, he was up against Sora Blade, a matchup that had a portion of the crowd riled up, every time Sora unleashed his Tonfa Blade weapon, a compressed wind pressure shot out like a ball bullet,

strong enough to cause small wind friction, while his slashes coated his weapon Qi with wind, spreading out like he was executing a Wind Blade spell.

But despite all this, he was still unable to land a hit on Knight, who seemed to slip out of every situation effortlessly; with his Shadow Jump and Shadow Travel spells, he could also move from one spot to another using Sora’s own shadow.

And every time he re-emerged, the first thing he focused on was landing a critical blow to vital areas on Sora’s body, the way he attacked, it was almost as if he didn’t care how much damage he caused, and this was because he believed no matter how critical the injury they incurred, the competition’s healing team would restore the mage to a considerable state.

And finally, Talon Blade and Arthur Gordons went at each other with furious punches. While one’s strikes left blazing, flaming explosions in their wake, the other’s gave off booming metallic clashes as they dashed around the confined area, but Arthur seems to have the upper hand as he has merged with his Golem giving him some level of resistance against the flame but the mage on the other hand seems to have a lot of troubles handling his punches that were augmented by the sharp Metal Elemental qi.

From the corner of his eye, Alec swiftly glanced at them, after confirming they were all holding up okay, he finally focused on his own battle.

“Alec, you bastard! I only said I wanted to face one, why are you not summoning support?” Lucas cursed at his captain, because he could clearly feel that if this continued, he might be the first to get knocked out.

But Alec didn’t bother replying. Instead, he dashed forward toward Hoshino Ren, who was now fully clad in red Qi armor shaped like the warlords of ancient Japan.

[> Mid Tier Spell - Reverse Gravity. <]

Alec cast a spell as he nudged his head upward, then moved his right hand to his left side, drawing his Bone Katana, as he now wielded two weapons.

While he was doing this, the crowd watched as Ren, who was still a few meters ahead, seemed to lose his balance. He was hurled into the air, with his body twisting mid-flight.

Before he could channel his Qi to shatter the purple gravity forces coating him and sending him floating upside down into the skies, Alec was already upon the three remaining members of Shinsei Academy.

Seeing him charge toward them with such confidence, Kurogane Oni, the vice captain of the Shinsei Academy, decided to make the first move by dashing forward to meet him.

He had already summoned his Black Iron Spirit shield, which was engraved with the face of a demon, as he raised it before him and dashed forward like he was attempting a shield bash Alec.

But before he even got close, the ground beneath Alec’s feet ignited in flames, as he used the fire to propel himself into the air, just as he closed in, he stomped his right leg down as if trying to strike Kurogane’s head.

This motion prompted the vice captain to raise his shield overhead to protect himself.

But then, Alec used the shield as a springboard to propel himself further, blasting off at a more ferocious speed than ever, his boots igniting in red flames, while his weapons were coated in black fire.



============================================================
Chapter 1073: Chapter 1073: Unpredictable 2.
============================================================

(Note: In the last Chapter I referred to the vice-captain of Shinsei Academy as Kurogane Oni. That was a mistake on my part. His name is Takeda Shun; Kurogane Oni is actually the name of his bounded spirit, My Apologies.)

After bypassing Shun by making him believe he was the target, Alec appeared before Aoyama Kiri and Kagura Yato.

Kiri had merged with her spirit, allowing her to manipulate wind in a unique way, her spirit was a Whispering Wind Wraith, granting her mastery over wind manipulation, sound manipulation, and an increased heightened perception.

Yato, on the other hand, was the only mage who had an assassin-type spirit among them, his spirit allowed him to perform short-distance teleportation and conjure shadow-formed spirit daggers and other weapons at will.

Looking at him, Alec couldn’t help but compare him to Knight, the moment Alec appeared before them, they attacked simultaneously.

As Yato took the lead, bending low and raising a shadow dagger high as he aimed to drive it into Alec’s right ribs.

At the same time, Kiri clapped her hands together, even before she released them, Alec could already sense that the backlash of the energy she was gathering would be able to destabilize him for a moment.

Suspended in midair, Alec calmly observed and analyzed their coordinated assault, silently acknowledging how impressive their teamwork was.

But he was done admiring, as he finally made his move, by slashing his weapons outward and releasing an X-shaped arc of dark flames that tore through the air toward Kiri.

At the same time, he twisted his body in midair, the sudden maneuver made Yato’s gaze tremble, as he couldn’t understand how Alec had managed to reposition himself like that while he was in the air.

In reality, Alec had used his flames to alter his posture, by manipulating the fire beneath his feet like a pair of rocket thrusters, he had been able to change direction in midair with ease.

As he completed the twist, Alec launched a kick straight toward Yato’s head, executing a brutal overhead strike.

It all happened so fast that neither the crowd nor even Yato could fully understand what was going on. Meanwhile, the wind blast Kiri had been preparing was crushed before it could even go far.

Alec’s twin flaming slashes not only canceled it out but continued surging forward trying to take her out even with a reduced power, this forced her to keep casting additional spells while retreating and trying to drain the energy in the strike.

That was why she couldn’t get close or assist Yato, as Alec had deliberately kept her occupied because Yato had been his target from the start because he believes that Yato was more of a drag to leave in the battle his irritating teleportation.

Bang!

The kick finally landed. All of Alec’s strength crashed into Yato’s head, driving him straight into the ground.

The moment his face struck the arena floor, he lost consciousness and couldn’t even attempt to stand again.

The power behind the kick alone was overwhelming, but when that was combined with the speed at which he had slammed his head into the hard ground, Yato simply blacked out instantly.

With a fluid motion, Alec twisted again and landed.

“Arrgh!”

The instant his feet touched the ground, he realized someone was already in front of him — Shun.

The vice-captain, who he had once used his shield as a pillar to launch himself forward, had now swung it horizontally towards him, aiming to cleave Alec with its sharp, pointed edge.

Alec just calmly stepped backward, evading the attack with ease as he watched the shield miss him by a few inches , and in one swift motion, he swung his bone katana out toward the right side of the shield, targeting a precise point.

Clang!

The moment the blade struck, Shun felt the strength drain from his arm, as his right hand, which was strapped into the shield, was forced wide open, leaving his body exposed.

His eyes widened in that moment, as Alec took full advantage of this and closed the distance with a burst of terrifying speed that was beyond what he was used to, holding the changdao in an inverse grip in his left hand, Alec drove the hilt forward, slamming it into Shun’s neck.

Shun choked violently, gasping for breath, though Alec wanted to finish him off, however; his mental senses picked up a new presence approaching.

So he decided to let him be, knowing he wouldn’t be able to do anything anytime soon in his condition, and in the same smooth motion, he turned again and swung his bone katana in the opposite direction, but this time, he was outplayed and stopped.

And that was because Ren had arrived.

Having finally broken free from the inverse gravity that had restrained him for about five seconds in the air, Ren rushed in and executed an upward slash aimed at Alec’s back.

However, at the last moment Ren saw Alec twist and swing his bone blade out towards his neck, immediately Ren swiftly altered the direction of his own strike, aiming to disarm him instead.

Yet even after doing that when their weapons clashed, he couldn’t hide the irritation rising within him when he saw the faint smile still hanging on Alec’s face.

He stepped closer imitating Alec’s earlier move to close the distance with Shun, but he changed the weapon initiative by placing his right hand at the base of his katana’s hilt while his left hand remained nearer to the guard.

Continuing the motion from before, he thrust the blade forward.

[> Low Tier Spell – Black Ashes Body <]

In response Alec cast a spell, while he moved directly into Ren’s range, Ren’s katana stabbed forward and sank into his flesh, yet Ren immediately felt something strange about it all, despite poking into Alec he could feel as if his weapon hadn’t actually pierced anything solid.

Puchi!

Then, while still glaring at Alec’s smiling face in anger, Ren heard the unmistakable sound of a blade piercing real flesh.

At that exact moment, Alec’s body burst apart into drifting black ash, dissolving before his eyes, and beyond that vanishing figure, Ren suddenly saw the truth, his blade had pierced slightly into Shun’s body.

Though the wound wasn’t deep enough to remove him from the battle, but the implication was clear, Alec hadn’t even been taking them seriously.

He had been toying with them like he had everything under control.

“How dare you! Where is your mage’s pride? Fight me head-on instead of running around!” Hoshino Ren shouted, turning just in time to see Alec’s body reconstructing nearby as the black ashes reformed.

“Hmm.” Alec glanced up at him and frowned, he didn’t like the way Ren spoke to him at all.

“No problem then. Playtime is over. Titan, come out.” Alec said, as beside him, a large icy-blue portal formed, big enough for a towering figure to step through.

Immediately, the surrounding air turned unbearably cold as Titan emerged from the portal, he appeared in his humanoid form, while cladded in a full-body ice armor, his light-green eyes glowing faintly.

The first thing Titan did was bow respectfully toward Alec, many in the northern crowd already knew Alec possessed an Ice Golem, but seeing Titan again after so long made them realize something had clearly changed, they felt there was something very different about him now.

It was then they saw him run a hand over his bald head, pushing it slightly backward, and when he raised it again, a floating ice crown hovered a few centimeters above his head.

“All of you — come at me!” Titan said, his gaze sweeping across every mage on the battlefield who didn’t belong to the War God Mage Team.

The entire crowd fell silent after his statement, the remaining Blade Clan members and the three Shinsei Academy mages that were still conscious were all stunned by the sheer arrogance radiating from the golem.

It was even acting more unrestrained than its own master.

“If you don’t make your move,” Titan continued, “you’d better believe I will make the first one instead.”

This time he didn’t just speak, he released his aura, the oppressive pressure spread across the battlefield, and every mage present, even those from the War God Mage Team, felt a cold chill run down their spines, they could all tell that without any illusion what was before them was a genuine Tier 7 Golem.



============================================================
Chapter 1074: Chapter 1074: Slow Battle.
============================================================

“When did Titan break through to the Tier 7 Golem Realm?” Arthur asked no one in particular, he was just simply too shocked at what he was witnessing, and widely stared at Titan in disbelief.

Due to the immense pressure Titan released, every ongoing fight came to a halt, as all eyes turned toward the Tier 7 golem standing before them.

After observing him for a moment and enduring the biting cold his aura produced, many couldn’t help but glance toward Hoshino Ren, the mage they had previously hailed as someone capable of displaying Tier 7 battle prowess.

For the first time, they realized something important: Ren had never truly emitted a Tier 7 pressure; Yea his spirit was clearly in the Tier 7 realm but it seems he couldn’t execute the realm pressure.

Because what stood before them now was on an entirely different level from anything he had had previously shown when merged with Akagami.

Bang!

Before anyone could even blink, Titan had already appeared in front of Ren, and he thrust his hand forward, his actual hand never even reached Ren — instead, a massive ice hand materialized in midair and slammed into him, sending Ren flying.

“Arrgh!”

Seeing this Shun swiftly rushed forward and raised his shield, trying to cushion the momentum that was carrying Ren away, but even he too was carried along as they were both pushed backward by the overwhelming force.

[> Mid Tier Spell – Wind Slam <]

Kiri, who stood further away, slammed her hands downward and cast one of the wind element’s most dangerous mid-rank attack spells, and then the crushing air pressure descended — yet Titan’s body didn’t move an inch.

Only the floating ice crown above his head trembled slightly under the pressure, then he began walking toward her.

With every step he took, frost spread across the ground, forming what looked like a rolling carpet of ice beneath his feet, he raised his right hand and casually conjured an ice lance.

The spectators could clearly see frost particles gathering together, forming a weapon of remarkable quality with effortless precision, when it was complete, Titan simply flicked his arm and threw it.

The motion looked casual, but the speed at which the lance shot out was anything but casual, as an icy-blue streak tore through the air and impaled Kiri, launching her toward the arena wall.

She didn’t even realize what had happened, to the spectators, it was clear, Titan had thrown the lance, and in a blur it had crossed the distance, pierced her shoulder, and dragged her across the battlefield until she struck the wall.

By the time she registered that she was no longer standing where she had been, her head had already slammed into the barrier, knocking her unconscious.

“We have to attack together! There’s no way you Blade clansmen can win without joining forces to fight this monster!” Hoshino Ren shouted toward the Blade Clan members.

Hearing Ren’s words, Kaelus and Aeron locked eyes, though they might disagree on many things, but they both understood one fact, and that was that they couldn’t allow Alec’s golem to continue eliminating opponents unchecked.

As they knew if it came down to just the two of them against Alec and his golem, they were certain they would lose after all they had seen.

“Why do things always have to get complicated? Why do new challenges always show up just to mess with me?” Kaelus complained as he dashed forward, leaving Aeron behind to deal with Lucas, as the other Gordons clansmen stopped the other Blades.

Seeing him rush off alone, Aeron felt a surge of irritation, he had suggested that they move together after knocking Lucas out, but as usual Kaelus had refused, insisting he would join Ren to defeat the golem.

With his speed, Kaelus quickly closed the distance and reached Titan. Dropping low, he slashed upward with his Katana, at the same time, Ren’s body flickered into place from the opposite side in a sudden teleport-like motion, preparing to strike from above.

But in the very next instant, Alec also appeared as well, teleporting in with the same abrupt movement, with his leg shooting out and slamming directly into Ren, sending him flying away from the clash.

“Why exclude me from the fight?, Do you look down on me so much that you think I can’t participate in a Tier 7 battle?” Alec said coldly as he pushed Ren back.

With that, the battlefield split, Alec and Ren sliding away from the center where Titan and Kaelus faced each other head-on.

Meanwhile, Kaelus slashed upward, aiming straight for Titan’s face, however; Titan simply took a step back and bent backwards.

During that step, however, he pressed his foot down and sent a portion of his mana into the ground, manipulating it a short distance behind Kaelus, in an instant, a jagged ice wall rose behind him.

Kaelus didn’t realize this as he thrust forward continuing his attack, Titan casually pushed out his left hand and gripped the blade.

The sound of metal scraping against Titan’s crystallized ice hand rang out harshly across the arena, the noise it created was unsettling, but in the end the weapon was completely stopped.

When the blade finally came to a halt in Titan’s grasp, Kaelus raised his eyes and stared at the towering golem before him, but Titan had already made a move.

His right fist slammed straight into Kaelus’ face, the impact sent Kaelus flying through the air until his back crashed violently into the jagged ice wall Titan had created earlier.

“Argh!”

Kaelus cried out as the spikes of ice dug into his back.

The spectators watched as Titan casually tossed the sword aside, from the section he had gripped, frost had already begun spreading across the blade.

Many in the crowd couldn’t help but wonder what would happen if he grabbed a person directly and held on for more than a moment.

As Kaelus struggled to open his eyes, he saw Titan already walking toward him, what infuriated him the most was the golem’s pace. Titan always appeared to be walking calmly, yet his speed was anything but slow.

A blue flash burst from Kaelus’ right hand as he summoned the Aether Blade, while a silver light appeared in his left hand as he conjured a spear.

Even though he knew he was in a tight spot, Kaelus was ready to fight Titan head-on. He swung his Aether blade first, releasing a surge of Qi energy toward the golem.

But before the attack could fully form, Titan casually swung out his left hand, as an ice axe materialized in his grip instantly, and with an effortless precision, he deflected the incoming blade energy.

Then he followed up with a brutal uppercut, driving his right fist upward toward Kaelus.

Titan’s uppercut slammed directly into Kaelus’ jaw, lifting him slightly off the ground. But Titan didn’t stop there, he moved his hand forward, gripped Kaelus by the neck, and shoved him backward.

Bang!!!

Kaelus’ body crashed against the jagged ice wall, he then switched him and slammed again.

Bang!!!

And again.

With every impact, spectators from the other kingdoms visibly flinched.

At the beginning, Kaelus struggled fiercely to break free from Titan’s grip, but after nearly ten brutal slams, his body finally went limp and he lost consciousness.

Many believed it was the repeated smashing of his head against the ice wall that had knocked him out. However, Alec and the mages of the Blade Clan knew Kaelus far too well to think that was the real reason.

Kaelus was an extremely powerful mage with weapon mastery and a good Qi control which means he could take a lot of hits, as a member of the Blade Clan, he could use Qi as a protective layer.

Even under heavy blows, he could have shaped his Qi into something like a protective helmet to reduce the blunt force, which led them thinking that the head slamming wasn’t the only problem he faced.

Titan finally released him and tossed his body onto the ground, and when everyone looked closely at Kaelus’ neck, they noticed it had turned pale, almost frostbitten compared to the rest of his body.

That was when Aeron realized what had truly happened.

Titan’s ice mana had invaded Kaelus’ body the moment he grabbed his neck, and because Titan’s hand stayed in contact with Kaelus’ skin the entire time, he was able to steadily inject and control the frost energy inside him, disrupting Kaelus’ ability to circulate Qi.

The slamming was only meant to accelerate his loss of consciousness, the real battle had been happening inside Kaelus’ body all along.

Tiny specks of frost had already begun forming across Kaelus’ skin, proof that the ice aura Titan injected was still spreading through him, slowly freezing him from within even after he had fainted.

“I said this before, Come at me, or I will make a move.” Titan spoke calmly as he looked around the battlefield.

“It seems none of you are willing to approach... so I will make my move now.” he said but this time, his words left the remaining mages confused.

If this wasn’t him making a move before... then what exactly had they just experienced?



============================================================
Chapter 1075: Chapter 1075: Ice Captains Revealed.
============================================================

“Bulwark, Glacius... Arise!” Titan commanded as he thrust his right hand forward.

Immediately after he spoke , summoning runes lit up across the frozen ground he had just walked over.

They glowed with an icy radiance, forming intricate magical patterns, before anyone could properly process just what was happening, two of Titan’s top Captain-rank golems began rising from the ground, as if some unseen force was lifting them upward.

The mages on the battlefield stared in shock, those watching from the stands were even more confused, while the spectators in the VIP sections struggled to comprehend what they were witnessing.

A golem summoning other golems.

Such a phenomenon had only ever been mentioned in ancient scriptures, none of them had ever seen something like this with their own eyes, yet here it was happening right in front of them.

While many were still trying to understand how such a thing was possible, the battle between Ren and Alec had already resumed in earnest, as both mages exchanged at least two swift strikes before separating again.

The first clash came when Alec slashed forward, after he had gained the advantage after kicking Ren in the side and forcing him slightly off balance.

But at the last moment Ren had twisted his blade with incredible precision, deflecting Alec’s Changdao just before the strike could land properly.

Ren immediately followed with a diagonal counter-slash after pushing the weapon away, but Alec blocked it and swiftly dashed backward, widening the distance between them.

Ren considered pursuing him, but with every step Alec took while retreating, small spikes of earth began protruding from the ground, though they weren’t very large, but they were enough to make Ren wary, so instead of charging recklessly, he chose to halt his advance.

At that exact moment, two new auras appeared on the battlefield.

Ren’s eyes shifted toward the corner of his vision, and what he saw made his expression change, there were already two additional ice golems who now stood on the arena floor.

Ren looked stunned.

Alec, however, barely seemed surprised at all, from the very beginning, Alec had been the one who told Titan not to reveal too much.

As he didn’t want Titan’s abilities becoming public knowledge yet, which was why he had instructed him to summon at least two of his Minion Golems instead to finish things up.

Titan, however, had simply been enjoying himself. Even so, he had carefully restrained his strength, limiting it to the level of a Low Tier 7 realm and nothing more.

Alec knew that if Titan fought with his true strength, the battle would end before it even properly began, after all, Titan’s full battle capability could rival that of a newly advanced Tier 8 mage.

That was the main reason Alec had insisted on him summoning the minion golems, because then at the very least, the mages present would still be able to exchange blows with them instead of being completely overwhelmed.

“Looking away while fighting me?, I’m trying to figure out whether you’re that confident... or just that stupid.” Alec said coldly, as he swiped his left hand from the right to the left in a back handed motion, causing spikes of earth to erupt from the ground beside Ren, aiming at his sides.

Ren responded instantly as he pushed off the ground and was retreating, his katana flashed as he slashed outward towards the spikes, releasing a crimson Qi wave that shattered the spikes in an instant.

But Alec was already on him while he was busy getting rid of his spike problem, Alec descended with a downward slash, forcing Ren to raise his left hand and guard with the crimson handguard that was conjured on his body using his Qi.

The clash produced a sharp screech as the changdao scraped against the reinforced guard, before Ren could recover, Alec followed with a front kick, driving him backward.

Alec pressed the attack relentlessly.

They barely moved from their positions, yet their arms blurred from speed as they exchanged strike after strike, every clash of their blades sent sparks of light scattering into the air.

Many spectators simply couldn’t understand how Alec was able to fight evenly against Hoshino Ren — a mage backed not only by Tier 7 power but also by the vast combat instincts of Akagami merged within him.

Some fans from the Central Kingdom even began mocking Ren, claiming he must be the weakest Tier 7 mage.

But that assumption came only from an outsider’s perspective. In reality, the battle unfolding before them was far more complex than they realized.

There was simply no way Ren would be able to deal with Titan. Although they technically shared the same realm, yet Titan possessed far greater battle power.

Even while holding back, it would be extremely difficult for someone like Ren, who only had Tier 7 prowess to inflict any real damage on him.

And since Ren couldn’t deal meaningful damage, it became much easier for Titan to simply suppress him through sheer force.

As for the clash between Alec and Ren, while many spectators believed they were evenly trading blows, the experts in the audience saw something entirely different.

They could clearly see that Alec was suppressing Ren, his movements were fluid—flicking, twisting, and redirecting attacks with effortless precision, despite Ren possessing the combat instincts of Akagami, Alec fought with remarkable ease.

The reason was simple. Alec had achieved Advanced Weapon Mastery, while Ren’s mastery largely came from the experience of his spirit.

Even though Ren had merged with Akagami, his physical body could not perfectly replicate Akagami’s techniques, because of that limitation, the flaws in Ren’s combat execution were gradually becoming more apparent before Alec who had advanced mastery as the battle continued.

Meanwhile, on the other side of the battlefield, Bulwark and Glacius had fully entered the fight, the icy ground that Titan had created began spreading even further as the two Captain-level ice golems appeared.

Bulwark carried an enormous shield strapped across his back, resembling the equipment of a tank-class mercenary.

In his right hand he held a smaller knight-style shield, while his left hand wielded a rounded shield with a half-moon cut near the elbow; its edges were far more crystallized than the rest of the icy construct, making it look dangerously sharp.

Glacius, on the other hand, wielded an elegant yet deadly ice spear, the spearhead looked extremely lethal, and at the base of the shaft there was a secondary blade meant for stabbing or hooking opponents in close combat.

“Knock them all out.”

That was all Titan said, as the two golems immediately dashed forward, heading straight toward the remaining Shinsei Academy mages—Shun and Kiri.



============================================================
Chapter 1076: Chapter 1076: Ice Captains Revealed 2.
============================================================

“I’ll take the one with the shield, handle the other one,” Shun said as he saw the two ice golems charging straight toward them.

Bang!!!

With every step Bulwark took, the ground trembled slightly, and seeing the massive golem charging while clearly building momentum made Shun feel threatened, so he decided to strike first.

He spun and hurled his shield toward Bulwark.

In his mind, the outcome seemed obvious, since the two shields Bulwark carried in his hands were smaller, Shun expected him to be forced into a tight defensive position.

He began imagining Bulwark would raise both of his arms up to block in an high defense, only to be pushed backward by the impact, the rebound would then send his shield flying back to him, giving him the chance to seize the initiative and rush forward himself to close the distance.

But reality rarely unfolds the way one imagines, because the moment Bulwark saw the shield flying toward him at high speed, he suddenly dropped low and slid forward.

The large shield strapped to his back pressed against the ground, acting almost like a sled that transported him.

Because most of the battlefield had already been covered in Titan’s ice domain, Bulwark’s sliding speed actually increased as he glided across the frozen surface, as Shun’s shield passed just right above him.

With that single movement, Bulwark not only avoided the attack but also closed the distance far faster than Shun expected.

And he came to a stop when he got into range of Shun, Bulwark lashed out with his right leg, sweeping it hard into Shun’s right leg.

Whack!

The strike was so sudden that Shun immediately dropped to one knee as his balance collapsed, before he could recover, Bulwark followed up with a brutal uppercut, driving the small shield mounted on his right arm diagonally into Shun’s jaw.

Just moments before the blow landed, Shun had already stretched his hand out, trying to recall his thrown shield after realizing his attack had been completely bypassed.

But now his shield, which had been hanging in midair and returning to him, suddenly lost its power after the uppercut landed and dissipated in the air.

Although the strike had momentarily stunned him, Shun still had a bit of strength left, as he tried to throw a punch downward, but Bulwark simply raised his arm and covered his face while he cast a spell.

In the next moment, a block of ice suddenly formed around Shun’s head, causing him to stagger backward, struggling to breathe as the freezing construct imprisoned his face.

Once the ice block formed, Shun completely abandoned the punch he had intended to deliver, and with Shun effectively neutralized, Bulwark stepped forward and launched another uppercut.

This time the blow was decisive, cursing Shun to lose consciousness instantly, this time he was unable to resist the darkness that overtook him as his body levitated slightly into the air before falling back with a loud thud sound.

On the brighter side, the block of ice encasing his head shattered into fragments as he collapsed.

“Bulwark!”

Just as he finished dealing with his opponent, Bulwark heard his name being called, he didn’t even bother turning to look.

Instead, he simply twisted his body and swung his left arm outward, sending the curved shield with the half-moon cut spinning toward the opposite side.

Clang!

The shield flew straight into a barrage of wind blades that had been rushing toward Glacius, intercepting and dispersing them, with the threat handled, the shield rebounded through the air.

Glacius extended his left hand and caught it effortlessly, as he spun and sent it back towards Bulwark back, Kiri, upon seeing her attack blocked, quickly began casting another spell, but it was already too late.

Glacius had closed the distance, he didn’t just turn to send the shield flying; he had also used that same momentum to swing his ice spear, specifically positioning the shaft sideways.

The blunt strike cracked against Kiri’s head, knocking her unconscious instantly.

Just like that, Hoshino Ren became the only active member left from the Shinsei Academy.

And judging from the way his battle with Alec was unfolding, things weren’t looking promising for him either.

At that moment, Ren was already panting heavily, the prolonged use of his merged state had clearly begun to backfire, and it seemed he had shot himself in the foot by maintaining it for too long.

In truth, Ren had never properly reached the level required to merge with a much higher-ranked spirit. Normally, however, he was able to defeat his opponents before the strain of that limitation began to show.

But against Alec, things were different, he hadn’t been able to get the upper hand against Alec at all. In fact, deep down he could tell that if Alec truly wanted to end the fight, he probably could have done so already.

Yet the battle continued, and Alec kept fighting using only his Changdao which is coated in Blood Killing Intent, that was how Ren’s Crimson Qi continued clashing against Alec’s bloodlust-infused blade.

Now, however, Ren had used far too much Qi and was reaching his limit, and maintaining Akagami’s Crimson equipment manifestation for such a prolonged period had pushed him to the brink.

He wasn’t just in a dire state, he was also completely exhausted, as he knew drawing further power from Akagami to stabilize himself would begin draining his life force, yet as he looked at Alec who seemed increasingly relaxed while casually deflecting his katana—Ren couldn’t shake the feeling that even that sacrifice might not be enough to secure victory.

Alec watched him silently, waiting, but the longer he observed Ren, the more it seemed like he wouldn’t gain much more from this fight again.

Ren believed Alec had been toying with him, but that wasn’t the truth, for the first time since Alec’s blade and sword mastery entered the advanced level, he had encountered someone capable of pushing him to genuinely swing his weapon with full focus.

Alec had been completely locked into the battle, at the same time, he had been receiving real-time notifications that his mastery was improving.

In a way, Ren had unknowingly become a grinding partner for Alec’s mastery progression, and the smile on Alec’s face came from that satisfaction.

But Ren misunderstood it, from the very beginning, Ren had felt that Alec never took them seriously, largely because of Alec’s calm and confident demeanor.

In reality, it wasn’t arrogance, it was simply Alec’s confidence in his own strength.

“Let’s end this. One final move,” Ren said as he raised his glowing crimson katana high into the air.

He poured every last bit of Qi he had left into the blade, as the energy surged violently, and behind him a towering phantom image began to manifest, the figure happened to be Akagami and he mirrored Ren’s movement, amplifying the pressure radiating from him.

Seeing this, Alec smiled, he had heard a great deal about Ren’s power from Agnes before the battle, and out of respect he decided to go all out for this final exchange.

“Alright.” Alec replied calmly.

He spun his sword once through the air before sliding it back into its scabbard, the moment the blade settled inside, the scabbard began to vibrate slightly.

Alec then took his stance, with his legs spread apart, while his right foot slid forward, and his right hand rested firmly on the hilt of the changdao, while his left hand pressed against the middle of the scabbard to stabilize it.

At the same time, both mages prepared their finishing techniques.

[> Mid Tier Skill – Cleave <]

[> Mid Tier Skill – Iaido <]



============================================================
Chapter 1077: Chapter 1077: Qualified.
============================================================

“Leave them. They are mine.”

While Alec and Hoshino Ren were locked in their clash, Titan decided to deal with the Blade Clan members himself.

When he spoke, his gaze fell directly on Brandon, Arthur, and Knight, he completely ignored Lucas, who was still bleeding but seemed to be holding his own against Aeron.

Both of them were laughing wildly as they exchanged blows, their weapons clashing repeatedly while enhanced by Qi.

Although Lucas appeared to have the advantage in raw strength, as each swing of his glaive forced Aeron several steps back, but Aeron was clever enough to maneuver around some of the attacks and search for faster ways to take Lucas down.

However, with lightning coursing through Lucas’ body, his reaction speed and evasiveness had greatly increased making it hard for Aeron to swiftly finish him in a very fast speed.

Because of that, he managed to stay on his feet and avoided getting trapped or pinned down by Aeron’s tactics.

After Titan spoke, the three Gordon clan members reluctantly stepped back to clear the way, though they weren’t happy about it—in fact, they felt confident about winning the fight with a little more time, but when it came to Titan, there was a small amount of fear they couldn’t ignore.

With the Gordons moving aside, Titan began walking toward the three Blade Clan fighters, his expression still cold and emotionless, seeing they had little choice than to face this scary Golem before them, the three of them raised their weapons and rushed towards him together.

The first to reach him was Darius, he was gripping his claymore with both hands, as he swung from the left in a powerful horizontal cleave, aiming straight for Titan’s neck.

Not far behind him, Talon Blades attacked from the right, the moment his fist shot forward, a small image of a roaring dragon formed in flames around his arm.

Though only the head of the dragon was clearly visible, the fire extended outward like a blazing neck following the path of his punch.

Meanwhile, Sora took the rear position, as he struck from behind, delivering long-range support attacks, every time he punched forward with his tonfas, compressed wind pressure shot out toward Titan.

And in a split second, Titan demolished all three of them.

First, a short ice axe materialized in his left hand, as he swung it and hooked the blade of Darius’ claymore, trapping the weapon mid-swing.

Before Darius could react, Titan’s right leg slammed into his knee, forcing him down, as Darius lost his balance and began to fall, Titan’s leg rose again and smashed into his jaw, sending him sprawling backward in an awkward crash.

With Darius temporarily out of the fight, Titan extended his left palm and caught Talon’s flaming dragon punch head-on.

Not only did he stop the attack, but he tightened his grip around Talon’s fist as his frost energy quenched the fiery energy that was being released by Talon’s fist, at the same time, Titan swung his left hand, and an ice mace materialized instantly. In one swift motion, he whacked it against Talon’s head from the side.

The blow knocked Talon unconscious immediately, while the ice mace broke down back into ice element particles.

Titan then grabbed Talon’s limp body and pulled him in front of himself just as the compressed wind blasts fired by Sora arrived.

Boom!!!

Titan used Talon’s unconscious body as a human shield while the wind pressure attacks slammed into his back, each impact made Talon’s body jerk violently.

The powerful wind strikes tore into his back, causing ruptures in his clothing and flesh, but Titan clearly didn’t care, once the barrage ended, Titan casually tossed Talon’s body aside.

At that moment, Darius suddenly reappeared, bending low with his claymore poised as he thrust it forward toward Titan.

Titan simply caught the blade with his bare hand.

Frost energy immediately spread across the weapon, racing along the steel and creeping toward Darius’ grip. Realizing the danger, Darius barely managed to wrench his hand free before the ice reached him.

But Titan was already moving again.

His left hand shot forward and grabbed Darius by the neck, lifting him into the air as he gasped for breath and tried to break Titan’s lock on his neck.

However, Titan’s freezing aura kept seeping into him, as Darius’ face began turning pale, as if the blood inside his body were slowly freezing.

Seeing this, Sora launched another attack toward Titan, this time though, Titan shifted the suspended Darius to the side and thrust his right hand forward toward Sora’s position, not interested to let him run wild.

In the next instant, jagged ice spikes erupted from the ground, shooting up toward where Sora stood.

It happened so fast that Sora had no chance to escape, the ice spikes ensnared him completely, lifting him off the ground, pushed him until he was pinned him against the arena wall as if he had been crucified, his arms spread wide while the rest of his body was trapped inside thick, jagged ice.

Once Titan finished dealing with Sora, he turned his gaze back toward Darius. Slowly raising his right hand, he pointed two of his fingers toward Darius’ head and fired a thin ice beam.

Boom!

A muffled sound echoed, the condensed energy within the beam wasn’t powerful enough to kill him, but it carried just enough force to knock him unconscious instantly.

While this was happening, Alec and Ren’s final attacks finally collided.

In the aftermath of the exchange, their positions had completely switched, with Alec now standing where Ren had been moments ago, while Ren stood where Alec had once been.

The entire arena fell silent, and then a second later the crowd noticed a long line of blood stretched across Alec’s chest.

Gasps erupted from the section filled with Northern Kingdom supporters, as they couldn’t believe that he was cut, but Alec simply placed his hand on his chest, touched the blood and smiled.

“That wasn’t half bad,” Alec said calmly.

“That was... a beautiful sword skill,” Ren replied with his back still turned to Alec, his voice trembling slightly as he spoke before his body collapsed forward onto the ground.

Only then did the spectators realize that Ren had actually been the one who lost the exchange, but then he had fallen face-first, making it difficult for them to immediately see the true extent of Alec’s strike.

With Ren down, only Aeron remained standing, however; the remaining fighters of the War God Team stepped back and allowed the duel between Aeron and Brandon to continue without interference.

In the end, both fighters exhausted themselves completely and collapsed, with Bradon looking like he might go into a coma, even so, the War God Mage Academy still had four mages left standing.

At the moment the result became clear, and the fanatic supporters erupted into deafening cheers even before the judge dictated them the winner.

Once again, Alec’s name echoed throughout the arena as he reintroduced himself in spectacular fashion, but while his teammates celebrated and waved toward the roaring crowd, Alec wasn’t looking at them.

Instead, his gaze remained fixed on Takeda, who had been watching the entire battle, though, considering what he had heard from Agnes, Alec supposed that the body watching the battle was most likely not one of Takeda’s main bodies.

It was probably just a clone, one that Takeda probably must have sent to observe the match and gather information about who his next opponents would be.



============================================================
Chapter 1078: Chapter 1078: Thoughts About The New Team.
============================================================

Alec’s name echoed loudly through the arena as the fanatic supporters among the Northern Kingdom crowd chanted it again and again, the way they celebrated made it seem as though they had already won the entire tournament.

Meanwhile, Alec’s gaze remained focused on Takeda, even though Agnes had mentioned that the one watching was likely just a clone, Alec still had the strange feeling that the real Takeda might somehow be able to observe through it.

And if that was the case, Alec wanted him to understand something clearly, that deep down, he was already looking forward to defeating him.

_ _

About two minutes later, Alec had left the stage, walking through the same tunnel his team had used to enter earlier.

Even after the battlefield was cleared and the participants had left, the discussions throughout the arena didn’t stop, in the VIP sections, the high-ranking spectators were already engaged in heated conversations.

“Finally, he’s back, let’s see what’s new this time.” Dragov said as he stood up.

The rest of the Bloodline Academy team rose with him, their expressions filled with interest, they too looked clearly eager for a chance to battle the War God Mage Team.

From the Ember Citadel team, however, only one person was present—the vice-captain, El-Masri.

She didn’t say a word. Instead, she simply frowned before standing up and heading toward the exit, what she had witnessed today weighed heavily on her mind.

As she walked, she kept calculating the probability of her team defeating the other qualified teams.

Secretly, she has been hoping that whichever team emerged from the loser’s bracket would be weaker and give her team better chances of winning against them in case they were paired together in the semi final.

But deep down, she had already realized something troubling, what she had just witnessed might have been an even greater monster than she anticipated.

After all, Alec had gone head-to-head with a mage whom her entire team had struggled to defeat, even after relying on careful strategies and teamwork. And he hadn’t just fought Ren...

He had won.

Yes, Titan might have been responsible for the weakened state Ren found himself in the fight, but El-Masri refused to let that cloud her judgment, because she understood one undeniable truth.

Her team wouldn’t just have to deal with Alec, they would also have to face his golem... Him and the rest of his team.

_ _

While the mages who had come merely to spectate the match began leaving after confirming who the fourth team would be, many other events were still unfolding within the first and second VIP levels.

On the second VIP level, Old Man Alderic burst into loud laughter, unable to contain his excitement. This time, no one dared say anything to interrupt him.

Even the Dean looked satisfied, the War God Mage Academy had managed to defeat two academies at once, an achievement that spoke for itself.

In doing so, Alec had not only silenced any attempts to downplay their victory, because claiming their win was unimpressive would also imply that the other teams they face were much weaker,

But they had also been able to qualify in a way that no one could reasonably question, after defeating both teams alone without having to do it three-ways.

Meanwhile, the King of the Northern Kingdom stormed out in anger, and a few minutes later, he appeared inside his throne room, where two mages knelt before him.

“Why in the hell was the information about Alec returning to the city not reported to me immediately?” the King demanded.

The two mages trembled in fear. After a few seconds, one of them finally gathered the courage to step forward and speak.

“My King, I am deeply sorry, the space axis was used very late last night, and we only received the report this morning. At the time, we didn’t think much of it. It was only after we arrived at the arena and saw him appear that we realized he must have been one of the mages who used the space axis during the night.”

Seeing his companion speak, the second mage reluctantly added his own explanation.

“And... my King, you previously instructed us never to disturb you at night under any circumstances, since that is the time you spend with the queens in your harem.”

“Insolence!” The King roared the moment he heard that.

The two mages immediately pressed their foreheads to the floor, trembling as they begged for mercy.

Though the King was furious at what had been said, it also gave him a moment to calm down and think more clearly.

If the news about Alec had truly been brought to him that late at night, he probably would have dismissed the messengers before they even had the chance to explain.

In fact, if he had been in a worse mood than usual, he might have even ordered them beheaded on the spot, and in the morning he had already spent time having breakfast with one of his queens before heading straight to the battle arena.

Thinking about it carefully, he realized there really might not have been any proper way for them to reach him in time.

“But if he came back... then what happened in Estonia City?” the King asked.

This second question immediately put the two servants in an even more difficult position, and it took them some time to gather their thoughts, but after nearly ten minutes they were able to explain everything they had heard about the events in Estonia City.

Though they couldn’t provide full details, only the main reports that had been passed along through their channels, according to the information they received, after several days of besieging the Gordons Clan the City Lord army was finally able to breach into their core territory, where the decisive battle had begun.

At first, everyone believed the City Lord’s army would win.

However, as the fighting continued, the spies stationed around the area reported that the sounds of battle were growing increasingly violent.

Before anyone could understand what had truly happened, everything changed.

The battle that they predicted would last days and it would end with the Gordons Clan collapsing and its surviving members hunted down like stray dogs for information about their other clansmen who were able to escape—never occurred.

Instead, the opposite happened, the Gordons Clan won.

The outcome caused a massive uproar, and what followed forced the capital to intervene in a matter of jurisdiction.

After all, the city’s main governing force had effectively been overthrown.

King Charles would have gladly used the situation as an excuse to punish and eliminate the Gordons Clan entirely, but he knew better than to act recklessly.

With the kingdom’s laws in place and countless witnesses capable of testifying to what truly happened, it would be foolish for him to fabricate accusations.

In fact, given how things had unfolded, the only option he could think of now was to instigate the Dragonmir Clan Patriarch to act.

After all, one of the cities governed by a branch member of his clan had now fallen into the hands of another clan.

The truth was, he had already known about the City Lord’s intention to attack the Gordons Clan, but just like how the War God Mage Academy had spies with-in Estonia City itself, he had secretly sent his own mages a few days earlier to investigate the clan that had managed to produce a talent like Alec.

That investigation was when he first learned that the Gordons Clan would soon be attacked, yet even after discovering such a situation, he chose to do nothing.

Deep down, he actually supported the move. Since he couldn’t openly dirty his own hands, he had been more than willing to let those minor branch mages of the Dragonmir Clan deal with the Gordons for him.

If the attack had succeeded, he would have simply swept the entire matter under the rug.

But now that they had lost, the situation could no longer be hidden.

He would have to either appoint a new City Lord or formally recognize the Gordons Clan as the new guardian clan of Estonia City.



============================================================
Chapter 1079: Chapter 1079: Another Format.
============================================================

By the next day, all four teams that had qualified for the semi-finals were already waiting for the match format and battle pairings to be announced.

From the changing room, they could clearly hear the roaring cheers of the crowd as everyone waited for the day’s matches to begin, while they were still waiting, the Dean walked in, accompanied by Miss Selene, who had come to wish them good luck.

After all, she had been the one who originally selected the team, and seeing how far they had progressed filled her with pride, they had definitely grown far beyond anything she had imagined when she first chose them.

After the team greeted her and she exchanged a few brief words with Alec, the Dean finally spoke.

“The team we will be facing has been confirmed,” the Dean began.

That single sentence immediately captured everyone’s attention as they turned toward him.

“We will be facing the Bloodline Academy, and the format for this match has also been decided, it will be something called a Rune-Point Domination Match.”

He paused briefly before continuing.

“The arena array system will include something known as a Creation Stone.”

“Although it’s called a Creation Stone, its function isn’t limited to changing the terrain, it will also be able to generate a temporary astral body for each of you. That astral body will be deployed onto the battlefield in place of your real body.”

“The most important part of this system is that each of you will have three lives during the match.”

Everyone in the room began smiling as they waited for the rest of the explanation.

However, the more Alec listened, the more uneasy he felt, from his experience, tournament formats rarely favored them and something about this one made him suspect it might backfire.

“And by three lives,” the Dean continued,

“I mean that if you are killed during the battle, you will be able to reincarnate and rejoin the fight, since it’s only your astral body participating, your real body won’t be harmed with the same wound that was inflicted on you during the dead period.”

“However, keep this in mind, that each death will leave you feeling extremely fatigued once the battle ends. It’s simply a side effect of the astral connection, nothing too serious.”

Hearing this, no one seemed to have any problems with it. After all, they clearly understood what it meant to be able to reincarnate three times in a battle.

They were fully aware that such an advantage would naturally come with some form of cost, and since to them the only price to pay was temporary fatigue after the match, it didn’t seem like a serious drawback to them.

“There will be five Rune Points spread across the battlefield,” the Dean continued.

“Your mission is to capture and control them, the more Rune Points your team controls, the faster your score will increase.”

He then paused briefly before adding the final rule.

“And there is one restriction—summons are not allowed.”

The moment the Dean finished speaking, everyone finally understood why this new format had been introduced, even the most simple-minded mage could tell that this rule had been specifically designed to target them, because they were the only team surviving who seems to have a summoning type of mage available.

Alec, on the other hand, merely chuckled after hearing the rule, it was obvious to him that summoning Titan during the previous match must have frightened certain people enough that they decided to impose restrictions to prevent him from using that advantage again.

And by those people, Alec was thinking about King Charles of the Northern Kingdom and the group of mages who carried out his orders must have talked and worked out things with the Judges.

What Alec couldn’t understand, however, was why they hadn’t arranged for his team to face the School of Silent Night instead, as it was publicly acknowledged as the strongest team of the moment.

He had actually been looking forward to that matchup.

The fact that their captain, Takeda, had been responsible for leaving Lucas and Beatrice in such terrible condition still irritated him.

Even though he knew Takeda had simply done what was necessary to qualify, Alec couldn’t completely shake off the grudge he felt.

Still, since that battle wasn’t happening now, he forced himself to focus on the opponent his team had actually been assigned to face, the Bloodline Academy.

When he thought about it carefully, he realized how disadvantageous the situation seemed for them.

First of all, Alec was already familiar with this type of battle format, even though his teammates looked slightly confused, he had seen similar systems in games he used to play back on Earth, games like Call of Duty or Mech Arena, where the objective revolved around capturing and holding strategic points on the battlefield.

But this situation was very different, one of the rules stated that a player had to remain inside a rune circle for ten uninterrupted seconds before their team could successfully capture the point.

After a rune point was captured, it would generate one point per second for the controlling team.

That meant the only way to win was to constantly maintain control over more rune points than the opponent, and the total score required to win was 500 points.

Alec couldn’t help but feel that this format had clearly been designed to target them, if he had been allowed to summon his golems, securing the rune points would have been far easier.

On top of that, Beatrice could have also used her puppet to help them cover grounds, but since her puppets also counted as summons, she was affected by the restriction as well.

He shook his head slightly, realizing that whoever designed the format had thought everything through very carefully, they clearly believed the rest of Alec’s team wouldn’t pose much of a threat, especially since Bloodline Academy was known for having an extremely well-balanced team.

Of course, there was still another way to win the match, and that is by eliminating every member of the opposing team if they couldn’t compete effectively in the rune point battle.

But even that possibility had been partially neutralized by the three-life reincarnation system.

Earlier, his teammates had been celebrating the advantage of having multiple lives, but they hadn’t realized that it also made it harder to win through pure combat.

Alec simply smiled.

He felt strangely satisfied knowing that he had scared certain people enough for them to constantly change the rules just to limit him, and honestly, he welcomed the challenge.

If anything, it only made him more determined to break their carefully crafted system in a way they would never expect.

After all, nothing brought him more satisfaction than shocking people who were certain he couldn’t win.



============================================================
Chapter 1080: Chapter 1080: The Re-Match.
============================================================

While the Dean was busy bringing them up to speed on how the new battle format would work, outside in the arena the judge was already addressing the crowd, explaining the updated rules and how the match would be carried out.

_ _

After a few minutes, all the proper introductions had been completed by the judge. It was finally time for him to usher in the teams that would be competing.

“For the two matches taking place today,” the judge announced, “we will first have Bloodline Academy facing War God Mage Academy. The second match will be between the Ember Citadel Team and the School of Silent Night.”

From inside their tunnels, the teams could clearly hear the judge’s voice echoing through the arena.

It wasn’t as if they didn’t already know who their opponents would be, but hearing it announced again made everything feel far more real.

For the mages of Ember Citadel, simply hearing the name School of Silent Night sent chills down their spines. Even though no one openly talked about it, it had become an unspoken truth among the competing teams—everyone was trying their best to avoid facing them.

Not only had no one figured out a reliable way to counter Takeda, who still hadn’t even revealed his full strength, but fighting someone who possessed five bodies was already troublesome enough.

There were also rumors circulating that Takeda’s main bodies wouldn’t be considered summons under the new rules. The restriction only applied to external clones or summoned entities, meaning his core ability would remain largely unaffected.

As Babatunde sat quietly, listening from the Ember Citadel side, he couldn’t help but let out a small snicker.

To him, it almost felt ridiculous.

The judges had introduced a new rule that supposedly restricted certain abilities, yet it barely affected the School of Silent Night at all. If anything, it only placed small limitations on them while pretending the playing field had been balanced.

The more he thought about it, the more it seemed as if the judges were subtly favoring Takeda—almost like they were quietly guiding him toward victory.

_ _

“Now let’s welcome the first team into the battle arena, from the right side, the mages of Bloodline Academy,” the judge announced, beginning the introductions.

As they stepped out, Dragov Dragonmir walked at the forefront, a short distance behind him was Damien, followed by Carmilla, Lupus Connor, and finally Kira Nightfang.

It almost looked as if they had arranged themselves according to their hierarchy within the team.

As usual, their uniforms were striking and stylish, as each mage wore the same base design, but the edges were accented with different colors, almost as if the uniforms had been tailored to complement each of them individually.

The primary color of the attire was a dark crimson-black, giving off an intimidating aura.

For Dragov, the edges of his uniform, designed almost like a military coat—were cyan. Damien’s accents were a vivid blood-red, perfectly matching the nature of his vampire bloodline, while Carmilla’s color was also red, but hers carried a slightly darker shade, far less bright than Damien’s.

Meanwhile, Lupus’s trim was silver, reminiscent of the moon, while Kira’s was pure black, making it seem as if her uniform carried no special accent at all.

The moment the Bloodline Academy team appeared, many of the high-class mages from the Northern Kingdom began to cheer loudly.

Meanwhile, the fanatical supporters of War God Mage Academy simply sat back and looked around with a certain air like the Bloodline Academy was none of their concern for the day.

To them, Alec had returned, and that alone meant that no obstacle truly mattered, they firmly believed that since Alec had once defeated Dragov, he would be able to do it again, this time with even less effort.

Seeing the northern crowd, which greatly outnumbered them, finally divided now that two academies from the Northern Kingdom were facing each other brought smiles to the faces of many mages from other kingdoms who had chosen to sit back and watch this match neutrally.
